nodes.summary: Extracts the authorized storyboard from the explicit payload key or nested under the quinn_r upstream_output delivery, validating shape and failing loud when neither resolves.

nodes.name: _auto_designations
nodes.summary: Auto-designates the Epic-14 engine's recommended video/animation slides for a no-HIL segment run when no Gate-2M designation override is provided.

nodes.name: act
nodes.summary: Motion-planner act entry point: decodes the envelope payload, runs build_motion_plan, and returns the updated RunState with the produced plan and trail entry.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Motion-planner specialist's 9-node scaffold graph wiring the deterministic 07D.5 producer act body into the standard receive-through-handoff transitions.

nodes.name: build_motion_planner_graph
nodes.summary: Assembles and compiles the motion-planner StateGraph with the nine scaffold nodes and fixed transitions.

nodes.name: payload_contract.py
nodes.summary: Ratchet-D consumed-payload contract for the motion-plan producer: the frozenset of envelope keys it reads (upstream_output, authorized_storyboard, motion_designations, perception_artifacts, bundle_path) validating the 07D.5 dependency edge.

nodes.name: narration_join.py
nodes.summary: Shared helper that joins narration segments across cluster sub-slides, detecting phantom segment ids (referenced but absent) and collapsed segment ids (merged during clustering) so VO narration stays aligned with the storyboard.

nodes.name: join_narration_segments
nodes.summary: Joins per-sub-slide narration segments into a coherent sequence, reconciling segment ids against the storyboard's expected set.

nodes.name: phantom_segment_ids
nodes.summary: Returns segment ids referenced by narration that do not exist in the storyboard, flagging phantom references.

nodes.name: collapsed_segment_ids
nodes.summary: Identifies storyboard segment ids that were collapsed (merged) during narration join so downstream consumers can account for them.

nodes.name: _act.py
nodes.summary: Act-phase implementation for the Quinn-R quality-review specialist: resolves run mode, gathers slides, runs the G5 quality-check battery (grounding, fidelity, sensory bridges, pre/post-composition validators) and writes the authorized document plus a dispatch-trail summary.

nodes.name: run_g5_checks
nodes.summary: Executes the full G5 quality-check battery for a run mode, dispatching to grounding checks, fidelity detection, sensory-bridge perception, and composition validators, aggregating findings.

nodes.name: act
nodes.summary: Quinn-R act entry point: decodes the envelope payload, runs G5 checks, records economics/cost data, writes the authorized document, and returns the updated run state with a trail entry.

nodes.name: fidelity_detector.py
nodes.summary: Detects narration-to-slide fidelity issues for Quinn-R by classifying visual references in VO text and matching them against perceived slide artifacts using shared figure-token normalization.

nodes.name: classify_fidelity_reference
nodes.summary: Classifies a narration text reference into a fidelity-reference category (e.g., figure, visual group) for downstream matching.

nodes.name: detect_fidelity
nodes.summary: Main fidelity-detection routine: builds an artifact map from perception outputs and checks that narration references (figures, numerals, visual groups) are actually present on the slides.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: LangGraph specialist graph for Quinn-R following the 9-node scaffold (receive/plan/act/verify/reflect/emit_spans/gate_decision/finalize/handoff): assembles the review prompt from sanctum references, invokes the LLM, parses the QRR verdict, and runs pre/post-composition act phases.

nodes.name: _parse_qrr
nodes.summary: Parses and validates the Quinn-R review (QRR) JSON returned by the LLM, raising QRRParseError on malformed or contract-violating output.

nodes.name: _invoke_quinn_r_llm
nodes.summary: Invokes the resolved chat model with the assembled Quinn-R prompt and records model-resolution metadata on the run state.

nodes.name: build_quinn_r_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs the compiled LangGraph StateGraph for Quinn-R by wiring the standard 9-node specialist scaffold via the shared contract builder.

nodes.name: payload_contract.py
nodes.summary: Declares the payload contract constants/shapes Quinn-R expects in its dispatch envelope; a thin dependency-free contract module.

nodes.name: quality_control_dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Dispatch layer for Quinn-R quality-control checks: runs G5 grounding validation, Storyboard-B review, and dynamically loads pre-composition and post-composition validator modules, converting failures into structured content-error findings.

nodes.name: run_g5_grounding
nodes.summary: Runs the G5 grounding check verifying that narration/content claims are grounded in the source corpus, producing structured error records for ungrounded content.

nodes.name: run_storyboard_b_review
nodes.summary: Reviews Storyboard-B artifacts for structural and content correctness, raising StoryboardBInputError on malformed inputs and returning findings.

nodes.name: run_precomposition_validators
nodes.summary: Dynamically loads and executes pre-composition validator modules against run artifacts, aggregating their findings for the G5 verdict.

nodes.name: run_postcomposition_validators
nodes.summary: Dynamically loads and executes post-composition validator modules (after video/audio assembly) and aggregates their findings.

nodes.name: sensory_bridges_dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Bridges Quinn-R to the sensory-perception subsystem by locating and invoking a perceive callable via module stubs, wrapping failures in SensoryBridgeDispatchError.

nodes.name: dispatch_to_sensory_bridges
nodes.summary: Loads the sensory-bridge perceive callable and dispatches slide artifacts to it, returning perception results or raising a structured dispatch error.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: LangGraph specialist graph for Tamara: parses the operator-instructions envelope, assembles a design spec during the act phase, and wires the standard 9-node scaffold via the shared contract builder.

nodes.name: _parse_operator_instructions_envelope
nodes.summary: Decodes and validates the operator-instructions envelope payload for Tamara, raising OperatorInstructionsParseError on contract violations.

nodes.name: build_tamara_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs the compiled LangGraph StateGraph for Tamara using the shared specialist scaffold contract.

nodes.name: _act.py
nodes.summary: Act-phase implementation for the Texas retrieval specialist: loads retrieval directives and bundle outputs, anchors extracted claims, and performs crash-safe, lock-protected bundle hardening with staged atomic writes, transaction markers, manifest refresh, and recovery of interrupted hardening transactions.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements a full write-ahead-marker transaction protocol (stage, publish marker, fsync directory, recover) around the source-bundle carrier, honoring the operator's extraordinarily-robust-carrier invariant.

nodes.name: _exclusive_hardening_lock
nodes.summary: Context manager acquiring an exclusive filesystem lock over the bundle-hardening critical section so concurrent hardening attempts cannot interleave.

nodes.name: load_bundle_outputs
nodes.summary: Public wrapper that loads a bundle snapshot under the hardening lock and returns its parsed outputs.

nodes.name: _anchor_extracted_claims
nodes.summary: Anchors extracted retrieval claims back to source sections of the extracted primary source, attaching cross-validation hints and word-floor expectations.

nodes.name: _mask_inline_code_spans
nodes.summary: Masks inline code spans in markdown so claim anchoring and word counts are not distorted by code content; handles nested backticks and edge positions.

nodes.name: _recover_hardening_transaction
nodes.summary: Crash-recovery routine that inspects a published transaction marker, validates staged versus hardened targets, and either rolls the interrupted bundle-hardening transaction forward or cleans it up safely.

nodes.name: _harden_bundle_locked
nodes.summary: Core lock-held bundle-hardening procedure: authenticates snapshots, stages bytes atomically, publishes the transaction marker, validates hardened targets, refreshes the manifest, and durably removes the marker on success.

nodes.name: act
nodes.summary: Texas act entry point: decodes the envelope, loads the retrieval directive, verifies retrieval scope and exit-status consistency, hardens the source bundle, and returns the updated run state with dispatch-trail entries.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: LangGraph specialist graph for Texas: enforces the sanctum lock (digest-pinned persona references), decodes the dispatch envelope, and wires the standard 9-node scaffold; exports the sanctum-lock assertion reused by other specialist graphs.

nodes.name: assert_sanctum_lock
nodes.summary: Verifies the current sanctum manifest digest matches the pinned digest, raising SanctumLockViolation if persona reference files drifted since the lock was taken.

nodes.name: build_texas_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs the compiled LangGraph StateGraph for Texas using the shared specialist scaffold contract.

nodes.name: retrieval_dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Dispatches Texas retrieval work by resolving the directive's corpus working directory and invoking the retrieval wrangler via the project's venv Python, delegating bundle post-processing to the act module.

nodes.name: _directive_corpus_cwd
nodes.summary: Resolves the corpus working directory declared by a retrieval directive, validating that the referenced lesson corpus path exists on disk.

nodes.name: dispatch_retrieval
nodes.summary: Runs the retrieval pipeline for a directive: launches the wrangler with the venv interpreter in the corpus cwd, checks exit status, and returns the produced bundle outputs.

nodes.name: _act.py
nodes.summary: Act-phase implementation for the Tracy retrieval-planning specialist: assembles a prompt from sanctum references and the provider registry, parses LLM output into retrieval intents, builds the retrieval-intent contract, and records posture selection.

nodes.name: assemble_tracy_prompt
nodes.summary: Assembles the Tracy LLM prompt from sanctum references, the retrieval-provider registry, and the decoded envelope payload.

nodes.name: parse_retrieval_intents
nodes.summary: Parses the LLM response into validated retrieval intents, falling back to a default intent and raising RetrievalIntentParseError on contract violations.

nodes.name: build_retrieval_intent
nodes.summary: Builds the canonical retrieval-intent structure (providers, queries, scope) handed off to Texas for execution.

nodes.name: act
nodes.summary: Tracy act entry point: decodes the envelope, invokes the LLM with the assembled prompt, parses retrieval intents, records the posture selection, and returns the updated run state.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: LangGraph specialist graph for Tracy wiring the standard 9-node scaffold; the act node delegates to the Tracy act module for retrieval-intent planning.

nodes.name: build_tracy_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs the compiled LangGraph StateGraph for Tracy using the shared specialist scaffold contract.

nodes.name: posture_dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Small helper recording Tracy's retrieval posture selection after validating it against the configured set of valid intent classes.

nodes.name: record_posture_selection
nodes.summary: Validates a selected retrieval posture against the loaded intent classes and records it for the run.

nodes.name: _act.py
nodes.summary: Act-phase implementation for the Vera fidelity-review specialist: parses the FTR (fidelity trial review) LLM output and runs the staged gate checks G0 through G4, dispatching modality perception, recording hard failures, writing the review trace, and summarizing findings on the run state.

nodes.name: _parse_ftr
nodes.summary: Parses the fidelity-trial-review JSON produced by the LLM into structured findings, raising FTRParseError on malformed output.

nodes.name: _dispatch_modalities
nodes.summary: Dispatches slide artifacts to the sensory-bridge perception modalities as part of Vera's gate sequence, collecting perception results for fidelity checks.

nodes.name: act
nodes.summary: Vera act entry point: decodes the payload, runs the G0-G4 gate battery in order, records economics data and hard failures, writes the trace artifact, and returns the updated run state with a summary trail entry.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: LangGraph specialist graph for Vera following the 9-node scaffold: reads sanctum references under digest lock, assembles the fidelity-review prompt, invokes the LLM, and parses the FTR verdict before delegating to the act module.

nodes.name: _parse_ftr
nodes.summary: Graph-side parser that validates the full fidelity-trial-review structure returned by the LLM against the Vera contract before it enters the gate pipeline.

nodes.name: build_vera_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs the compiled LangGraph StateGraph for Vera using the shared specialist scaffold contract.

nodes.name: sensory_bridges_dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Bridges Vera to the sensory-perception subsystem by locating and invoking a perceive callable through module stubs, wrapping failures in SensoryBridgeDispatchError; parallel to Quinn-R's equivalent dispatcher.

nodes.name: dispatch_to_sensory_bridges
nodes.summary: Loads the sensory-bridge perceive callable and dispatches slide artifacts to it on Vera's behalf, returning perception results or raising a structured dispatch error.

nodes.name: _act.py
nodes.summary: Act-phase implementation for the Vision slide-perception specialist: resolves execution mode (realtime vs batch), iterates slide rows, calls the vision provider with retry on transient errors, and records PerceptionArtifact results and cache state on the run.

nodes.name: _perceive_with_retry
nodes.summary: Wraps the provider's perceive call with retry logic for retryable provider errors (timeouts, transient SDK failures).

nodes.name: _act_batch
nodes.summary: Batch-mode act path: builds vision batch rows for all slides and runs perception through the OpenAI batch route, polling to terminal state and mapping results back to slide ids.

nodes.name: act
nodes.summary: Vision act entry point: decodes the payload, selects realtime or batch execution, runs perception over slide rows, and returns the updated run state with artifacts and a trail entry.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: LangGraph specialist graph for Vision wiring the standard 9-node scaffold; the act node delegates slide perception to the Vision act module and payload contract.

nodes.name: build_vision_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs the compiled LangGraph StateGraph for Vision using the shared specialist scaffold contract.

nodes.name: payload_contract.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic contract for the vision provider's response: defines VisionProviderResponse with a closed role-tier enum validator, mapping raw provider output onto the PerceptionArtifact model.

nodes.name: VisionProviderResponse
nodes.summary: Pydantic model validating the vision provider's JSON response, enforcing that role-tier values stay within the closed enum before conversion to a PerceptionArtifact.

nodes.name: provider.py
nodes.summary: Vision LLM provider adapter: builds the perception prompt and OpenAI message payload for a slide PNG, applies prompt caching, invokes the model, strips/parses the JSON response into the payload contract, and maps SDK exceptions to VisionProviderError/VisionProviderTimeout.

nodes.name: build_perception_openai_messages
nodes.summary: Builds the OpenAI chat message payload (system perception prompt plus base64 PNG image content) used for both realtime and batch vision calls.

nodes.name: _parse_response
nodes.summary: Decodes and JSON-strips the model response, validating it against the VisionProviderResponse contract and surfacing parse failures as provider errors.

nodes.name: perceive_png
nodes.summary: Main provider entry point: resolves the chat model, applies the prompt-cache key, sends the slide PNG for perception, tracks cached-token usage, and returns the parsed perception response.

nodes.name: VisionProviderError
nodes.summary: Structured exception carrying provider-failure context (retryability, provider detail) for vision perception calls.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: LangGraph specialist graph for Vyx: parses the operator-instructions envelope and assembles storyboard instructions during the act phase, wiring the standard 9-node scaffold via the shared contract builder.

nodes.name: _parse_operator_instructions_envelope
nodes.summary: Decodes and validates the operator-instructions envelope payload for Vyx, raising OperatorInstructionsParseError on contract violations.

nodes.name: build_vyx_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs the compiled LangGraph StateGraph for Vyx using the shared specialist scaffold contract.

nodes.name: _act.py
nodes.summary: Act-phase implementation for the Wanda audio-bed specialist: derives bed specs from narration tracks, generates audio beds through the live Wondercraft API client, persists the audio bytes, and builds the compositor invocation for downstream assembly.

nodes.name: _call_generate_audio_bed
nodes.summary: Calls the Wondercraft client to generate a single audio bed for a bed spec, handling API errors and returning the generated asset reference.

nodes.name: generate_audio_beds
nodes.summary: Generates all audio beds for the run: iterates bed specs, invokes Wondercraft generation per bed, writes audio bytes to disk, and records outputs for the compositor.

nodes.name: act
nodes.summary: Wanda act entry point: decodes the envelope payload, generates audio beds, updates cache state, and returns the run state with a trail entry.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: LangGraph specialist graph for Wanda wiring the standard 9-node scaffold with sanctum-reference loading; the act node delegates audio-bed generation to the Wanda act module.

nodes.name: build_wanda_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs the compiled LangGraph StateGraph for Wanda using the shared specialist scaffold contract.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: LangGraph specialist graph for the Workbook Producer wiring the standard 9-node scaffold; the act node delegates to the workbook payload contract for workbook document production.

nodes.name: build_workbook_producer_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs the compiled LangGraph StateGraph for the Workbook Producer using the shared specialist scaffold contract.

nodes.name: parity.py
nodes.summary: SPOC anti-drift parity guard for the Gamma styleguide library: computes canonical resolution digests, builds JSON-safe parity receipts, compares styleguide resolutions between surfaces, and classifies mismatches and crashes into structured verdicts.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses canonical JSON serialization plus digest comparison so two independent resolution paths can be proven equivalent without sharing state.

nodes.name: canonical_resolution_digest
nodes.summary: Computes a stable digest over the canonical JSON form of a styleguide resolution so independent resolution paths can be compared byte-for-byte.

nodes.name: compare_styleguide_resolution
nodes.summary: Compares two styleguide resolutions (name/digest pairs plus receipts) and returns a parity verdict identifying any drift between the resolving surfaces.

nodes.name: _classify
nodes.summary: Classifies parity outcomes into detailed verdict categories (match, mismatch kinds, crash detail) driving how drift between styleguide resolution paths is reported.

nodes.name: resolver.py
nodes.summary: Gamma styleguide resolver: loads the style-guide library, expands guide records into API and Studio surface parameter sets, resolves scripted entries, and provides the top-level resolve_styleguide entry point that turns a guide selection into concrete generation settings.

nodes.name: load_style_guides
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the style-guide library from disk, raising StyleguideError on missing or malformed guide records.

nodes.name: classic_surface_keys
nodes.summary: Enumerates the classic-generation surface keys a styleguide record may set, defining the closed vocabulary of Gamma API parameters.

nodes.name: expand_record
nodes.summary: Expands a styleguide record into its full surface parameter set, delegating to the API or Studio expansion path based on production mode.

nodes.name: resolve_scripted
nodes.summary: Resolves a scripted styleguide selection (pre-picked entries) into concrete expanded records for the run.

nodes.name: resolve_styleguide
nodes.summary: Top-level entry point resolving a styleguide selection into the concrete generation settings consumed by Gary and the CD specialist.

nodes.name: _base.py
nodes.summary: Shared Pydantic-v2 idiom helpers for the app.models.state family: centralizes the tz-aware-datetime and UUID4-version-pin validators so state and verdict models reuse them instead of duplicating per file.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberate single source for the bundle 14-idiom checklist validators, preventing G6 MUST-FIX drift like the 31-1 datetime findings.

nodes.name: enforce_tz_aware
nodes.summary: Field validator helper that rejects naive datetimes, guaranteeing every stored timestamp is timezone-aware.

nodes.name: enforce_uuid4_version
nodes.summary: Field validator helper that pins UUID fields to version 4, rejecting other UUID versions.

nodes.name: specialist_state.py
nodes.summary: Specialist persistence state shape for Slab 7a conversation artifacts: pins a specialist run status, cache prefix, POSIX artifact paths, and the model-resolution trail per trial/gate.

nodes.name: SpecialistState
nodes.summary: Pydantic state model for one specialist invocation: schema version, trial/specialist/gate identity, status, cache prefix + hash, POSIX-normalized summary and artifact paths, model-resolution trail, and tz-aware updated_at.

nodes.name: specialist_state_schema_text
nodes.summary: Emits the canonical JSON Schema text for SpecialistState, used by shape-pin tests and schema exports.

nodes.name: trial3_transcript.py
nodes.summary: Trial-3 operator transcript schema (Story 7c.21, FR-7c-51): event and transcript models whose gate identifiers are closed against the live production-gate surface from the pipeline manifest, with a shape-pin test guarding drift.

nodes.name: TrialEvent
nodes.summary: One transcript event: UUID4 event id, manifest-closed gate id, event type, tz-aware event_at, optional operator id, and payload digest.

nodes.name: Trial3Transcript
nodes.summary: Top-level Trial-3 transcript: UUID4 trial id, tz-aware started/completed timestamps, ordered event list, and schema version pin.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Public parity-contract DSL surface: re-exports the declaration model, @parity_contract decorator, surface registry, and sanctum-alignment API from the private submodules, with a lazy __getattr__ hook.

nodes.name: _audit.py
nodes.summary: Self-registration audit harness for parity-contract declarations: import-walks discovery roots to trigger decorator registration, checks surface and sanctum cardinality against a declared floor, and emits a registration manifest. Includes a CLI main.

nodes.name: AuditResult
nodes.summary: Pydantic result model for an audit run: surface and sanctum cardinalities, cardinality floor, pass/fail status, and failure reason.

nodes.name: _import_walk_roots
nodes.summary: Imports every module under the given discovery roots (optionally including the reference surface) so import-time @parity_contract decorators register their surfaces.

nodes.name: emit_registration_manifest
nodes.summary: Writes the currently registered parity-contract surfaces to a JSON manifest with a UTC timestamp.

nodes.name: run_self_registration_audit
nodes.summary: Runs the full audit: import-walks discovery roots, compares registered surface and sanctum-alignment counts against the declared floor, optionally emits the manifest, and returns an AuditResult.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses arguments and runs the self-registration audit, returning a non-zero exit code on failure.

nodes.name: _declaration.py
nodes.summary: Parity-contract transport declaration model: defines the Transport and GateFamilyId literals and the SurfaceTransportDeclaration shape that pins which transports (cli/http/mcp) a gate surface must and may support.

nodes.name: SurfaceTransportDeclaration
nodes.summary: Pydantic declaration for one gate surface: surface id, mandatory and optional transport sets (validated disjoint/non-empty), and an optional alias_of restricted to known gate families.

nodes.name: _decorator.py
nodes.summary: Decorator entry point for parity-contract self-registration: @parity_contract builds a SurfaceTransportDeclaration and registers it at import time, returning the decorated callable unchanged.

nodes.name: parity_contract
nodes.summary: Decorator factory taking surface id, mandatory/optional transports, and alias_of; constructs the declaration, registers it in the surface registry, and returns the wrapped function untouched.

nodes.name: _reference_surface.py
nodes.summary: Reference parity-contract registration placeholder (Story 7c.0b): a minimal decorated function proving import-time @parity_contract registration works before Story 7c.1 migrates the real transport-parity tests onto the DSL.

nodes.name: _registry.py
nodes.summary: In-memory registry for parity-contract surface declarations: registers declarations keyed by surface_id, raises DuplicateSurfaceError on collisions, and exposes iteration plus a test-only clear hook.

nodes.name: register_surface
nodes.summary: Registers a SurfaceTransportDeclaration in the module-level map, raising DuplicateSurfaceError when the surface_id is already present.

nodes.name: iter_registered_surfaces
nodes.summary: Yields all registered surface declarations for audit and manifest emission.

nodes.name: _sanctum.py
nodes.summary: Sanctum-alignment declarations for parity writers: a Pydantic declaration (bmb-pattern vs cora-sidecar-exception, with mandatory rationale for exceptions), an in-memory registry keyed by writer_id, and JSON manifest emission.

nodes.name: SanctumAlignmentDeclaration
nodes.summary: Declaration of how a parity writer aligns with its sanctum: writer id, sanctum path, alignment kind, and an exception rationale required when kind is cora-sidecar-exception.

nodes.name: declare_sanctum_alignment
nodes.summary: Builds and registers a SanctumAlignmentDeclaration, raising DuplicateSanctumAlignmentError on repeated writer ids.

nodes.name: iter_sanctum_alignments
nodes.summary: Yields all registered sanctum-alignment declarations for audit and manifest emission.

nodes.name: emit_sanctum_alignment_manifest
nodes.summary: Writes registered sanctum alignments to a JSON manifest with a UTC timestamp.

nodes.name: override_api.py
nodes.summary: Runtime model-override flow: computes cache impact for a node/model change from the pipeline manifest, issues a ModelOverrideWarning with a staleness-bound confirm token, applies confirmed overrides against RunState, and emits override events to the ledger.
nodes.languageNotes: Two-phase submit/apply protocol with a sha256 confirm token that binds trial, node, model, and cache state so stale confirmations fail loudly (OverrideTokenStaleError).

nodes.name: _affected_nodes
nodes.summary: Walks the pipeline manifest downstream of a node to list nodes whose caches an override would invalidate.

nodes.name: compute_cache_impact
nodes.summary: Computes the cache-state delta a proposed model override would cause for a trial: affected downstream nodes and resulting cache state label.

nodes.name: _confirm_token
nodes.summary: Derives the sha256 confirm token binding trial id, node id, new model, and current cache state, making stale confirmations detectable.

nodes.name: submit_override
nodes.summary: Phase 1 of the override protocol: validates the trial/node/model request, computes cache impact and cost delta, and returns a ModelOverrideWarning carrying the confirm token and expiry.

nodes.name: apply_override
nodes.summary: Phase 2 of the override protocol: verifies the confirm token against current cache state (raising OverrideTokenStaleError when stale), mutates RunState/CacheState, appends to the override ledger, and emits an OverrideLedgerEvent.

nodes.name: decision_card_meta_for_trial
nodes.summary: Builds DecisionCardMeta for a trial from the registered run state, for surfaces that render override decisions as decision cards.

nodes.name: override_warning.py
nodes.summary: Warning model returned before a runtime model override is applied: captures trial/node identity, requested vs current model, estimated cost delta, affected nodes, cache-state delta, and the confirm token with issue/expiry ordering enforced.

nodes.name: ModelOverrideWarning
nodes.summary: Pydantic warning model with UUID4, tz-aware, and sha256 confirm-token validation plus an expires_at > issued_at ordering check.

nodes.name: sanctum_warning.py
nodes.summary: Strict warning model for sanctum mutations surfaced at the next gate: file path, before/after hashes, tz-aware mutation time, and a suggested invalidating commit.

nodes.name: SanctumWarning
nodes.summary: One operator-visible sanctum mutation warning with tz-aware mutated_at enforcement.

nodes.name: sanctum_watcher.py
nodes.summary: Watches sanctum markdown files for in-flight mutations: snapshots sha256 hashes around specialist invocations, raises SanctumWarning records per trial, tracks a mutation counter, and emits SanctumMutationLedgerEvents to the ledger.
nodes.languageNotes: Thread-safe watcher combining a filesystem event handler with begin/finish invocation hash snapshots so mutations are attributed to the invocation window they occurred in.

nodes.name: SanctumWatcher
nodes.summary: Core watcher: start/stop lifecycle, per-invocation hash snapshots (begin_invocation/finish_invocation), and handle_path diffing that registers warnings and emits sanctum-mutation ledger events.

nodes.name: _SanctumEventHandler
nodes.summary: Filesystem event adapter forwarding on_modified/on_created paths into the SanctumWatcher handler.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for app.manifest that re-exports the manifest loader, compiler, lane helpers, schema models, and exception types as the public manifest API.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel; re-exports the built-in-shadowing `compile` with a noqa A004 marker.

nodes.name: compiler.py
nodes.summary: Compiles a validated PipelineManifest into a LangGraph StateGraph: adds nodes/edges, resolves specialist dispatch builders, and runs compile-time contract lint (gate codes, model-config refs, frozen-graph version, conditions, decision-card schemas, dependency cycles).
nodes.languageNotes: Separates run_graph vs dev_graph lanes per architecture decision D4; specialist handlers are resolved via dotted builder refs from a YAML dispatch registry.

nodes.name: _passthrough_node
nodes.summary: Builds a Slab-1 stub passthrough node handler that records the specialist id without doing real work.

nodes.name: production_gate_ids
nodes.summary: Returns the frozenset of node ids in a manifest that are production gates.

nodes.name: _resolve_builder_ref
nodes.summary: Imports and validates a 'module:attribute' builder reference from the dispatch registry, raising CompileError on missing modules or non-callable targets.

nodes.name: _specialist_dispatch_node
nodes.summary: Builds a node handler that resolves a specialist's graph builder, compiles the specialist sub-graph, and invokes it with the run state.

nodes.name: _production_gate_node
nodes.summary: Builds the handler for a production gate node (HIL pause point) in the compiled run graph.

nodes.name: _orchestration_node
nodes.summary: Builds a no-op orchestration node handler used when a manifest node has no dispatchable specialist (with the reason recorded).

nodes.name: _resolve_production_handler
nodes.summary: Chooses the correct handler for a manifest node: production gate, orchestration no-op, or specialist dispatch based on the dispatch registry.

nodes.name: _validate_model_config_refs
nodes.summary: Asserts every node's model_config_ref points at an existing YAML file under the repo, per FR25/NFR-M2.

nodes.name: _validate_model_ids_in_model_config_refs
nodes.summary: Loads each referenced SpecialistModelConfig and asserts its default and per-node-override model ids all exist in the PipelineRegistry catalog.

nodes.name: _validate_frozen_graph_version
nodes.summary: Asserts the runtime/graphs/v{frozen_graph_version} directory exists for the manifest's declared frozen graph version.

nodes.name: _validate_conditions
nodes.summary: Asserts every EdgeSpec.condition names a registered condition function, raising CompileError with the sorted known set otherwise.

nodes.name: _validate_decision_card_schemas
nodes.summary: Resolves each edge's decision_card_schema dotted reference at compile time so broken schema refs fail loudly before runtime.

nodes.name: _validate_dependency_cycles
nodes.summary: Builds the specialist dependency graph from node declarations and DFS-detects cycles, raising CompileError with the cycle path.

nodes.name: _add_node_and_edges
nodes.summary: Populates a StateGraph with every manifest node (resolved to its handler) and wires plain and conditional edges, including START/END mapping.

nodes.name: compile
nodes.summary: Main entry: runs all compile-time lint checks over the manifest then builds and returns the StateGraph topology using stub passthrough handlers.

nodes.name: compile_run_graph
nodes.summary: Production variant of compile: validates the run-lane manifest and builds the StateGraph with real specialist dispatch handlers from the dispatch registry.

nodes.name: conditions.py
nodes.summary: Condition-function registry for EdgeSpec.condition resolution; Slab-1 stub exposing only always_true/always_false with a resolve() lookup that fails loudly on unknown names.

nodes.name: resolve
nodes.summary: Looks up a condition function by name in the registry, raising KeyError listing known names when absent.

nodes.name: exceptions.py
nodes.summary: Exception types for the manifest loader and compiler, kept import-light so tooling can catch errors without pulling in the full Pydantic schema graph.

nodes.name: ManifestValidationError
nodes.summary: Raised by the manifest loader on malformed input; wraps Pydantic ValidationError as a stable ValueError subclass preserving the named-violation path.

nodes.name: CompileError
nodes.summary: Raised by the manifest compiler when compile-time contract lint fails (bad refs, unknown conditions, cycles, missing files).

nodes.name: ManifestSchemaImportError
nodes.summary: Raised when a dotted manifest reference cannot be imported or does not resolve to the expected object type.

nodes.name: gate_fold_manifest_emit.py
nodes.summary: Emits the Slab-7a gate-fold audit manifest: derives gate fold-chain entries from the pipeline manifest and writes a deterministic YAML audit artifact, with a CLI main.

nodes.name: gate_fold_entries
nodes.summary: Walks manifest nodes' fold_with/fold_target chains, validates them (unknown targets, cycles), and produces sorted gate-fold audit entries.

nodes.name: render_gate_fold_manifest
nodes.summary: Serializes gate-fold entries plus provenance (source manifest path, generated-at timestamp) into deterministic YAML text.

nodes.name: emit_gate_fold_manifest
nodes.summary: Loads the pipeline manifest, renders the gate-fold audit YAML, and writes it to the output path (creating parent dirs).

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Argparse CLI entry that emits the gate-fold manifest to the requested output path.

nodes.name: gate_topology.py
nodes.summary: Operator-facing CLI that dumps the gate topology derived from the pipeline manifest and can diff it against the committed gate-fold audit YAML.

nodes.name: render_topology
nodes.summary: Loads the manifest and renders one formatted line per gate-fold entry (optionally unfolded).

nodes.name: render_audit
nodes.summary: Compares live gate-fold entries against the committed audit YAML and renders per-gate-code match/drift lines.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Argparse CLI entry offering mutually exclusive topology-dump and audit-diff modes over the pipeline manifest.

nodes.name: lanes.py
nodes.summary: Lane-specific compilation helpers: coerces a path-or-manifest input, enforces the run-lane constraint, and delegates to the compiler for validated (compile_run_graph) or production-composed (compose_run_graph) graphs.

nodes.name: compile_run_graph
nodes.summary: Coerces the input to a PipelineManifest, asserts run-lane, compiles it via the manifest compiler, and returns the compiled LangGraph app.

nodes.name: compose_run_graph
nodes.summary: Coerces the input manifest and delegates to the production compile_run_graph (real specialist dispatch) path.

nodes.name: loader.py
nodes.summary: YAML-to-PipelineManifest loader: single load(path) entrypoint that safe-loads YAML, validates against the Pydantic schema, and normalizes all failure modes into ManifestValidationError.

nodes.name: load
nodes.summary: Reads a manifest YAML file, validates it into a PipelineManifest, and re-raises missing-file, parse, and Pydantic errors as ManifestValidationError with named violations.

nodes.name: refs.py
nodes.summary: Dotted-reference resolution helper that imports 'module:attribute' strings to live Python objects with optional base-class enforcement, raising ManifestSchemaImportError on failure.

nodes.name: resolve_dotted_ref
nodes.summary: Parses and imports a 'module:attribute' reference, optionally asserting the resolved object subclasses an expected base class, with named errors at each failure point.

nodes.name: schema.py
nodes.summary: The manifest-as-graph-config contract: Pydantic v2 models (PipelineManifest, NodeSpec, EdgeSpec, projection and learning-events configs) plus gate-classification predicates, realizing architecture decision D6.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic v2 with model_validator invariants (unique node ids, resolvable entrypoint/edges, mutually exclusive fold fields) enforced at parse time.

nodes.name: LearningEventsConfig
nodes.summary: Top-level manifest learning-events configuration carrying the schema_ref for emitted learning events.

nodes.name: StepLearningEventsConfig
nodes.summary: Per-node learning-events declaration: whether the step emits, which event types, and the schema reference.

nodes.name: NodeSpec
nodes.summary: Per-node manifest spec: id, specialist binding, model_config_ref, dependencies and projections, gate/fold metadata, pack anchors, and rationale — with validators enforcing fold-field exclusivity and projection/dependency disjointness.

nodes.name: ProjectionSpec
nodes.summary: Declares a dependency projection: which upstream specialist a projected input comes from and under what key.

nodes.name: EdgeSpec
nodes.summary: Manifest edge spec: from/to node ids, optional condition name for conditional routing, dispatch envelope flag, and decision-card schema reference.

nodes.name: PipelineManifest
nodes.summary: Root manifest model (schema_version, lane, entrypoint, frozen_graph_version, nodes, edges, pack/generator/learning-events metadata) with validators guaranteeing non-empty unique nodes and resolvable entrypoint and edge endpoints.

nodes.name: is_orchestration_only
nodes.summary: Predicate: true when a manifest node is pure orchestration (no specialist content contribution).

nodes.name: is_folded_gate
nodes.summary: Predicate: true when a gate node has been folded into another gate via fold_with/fold_target metadata.

nodes.name: is_content_free_gate
nodes.summary: Predicate: true when a gate node carries no content contribution of its own (pause-only gate).

nodes.name: is_pack_excluded
nodes.summary: Predicate: true when a node is excluded from the pack (orchestration-only or content-free gate).

nodes.name: composition.py
nodes.summary: Compile-time component composer: assembles only the selected lesson-plan components' manifest nodes into one PipelineManifest (topo-sorted, fail-closed on cycles/missing deps), computes the S2 two-part content-addressed digest, and verifies composition replay.
nodes.languageNotes: Fragment registry bindings to the modality/component-type registries are validated at import time; freeze-after-assembly preserves the single-frozen-graph tamper-evident replay invariant.

nodes.name: CompositionError
nodes.summary: Raised when component composition fails (unresolved dependency, cycle, or invalid fragment binding); composition fails closed.

nodes.name: CompositionReplayError
nodes.summary: CompositionError subclass raised when replaying a recorded composition no longer reproduces the recorded two-part digest.

nodes.name: ComponentFragment
nodes.summary: Frozen descriptor of one composable component: its modality refs, owned manifest node ids, dependencies, version, base flag, and synthetic-node attachments.

nodes.name: _validate_fragment_registry_bindings
nodes.summary: Import-time check that every component fragment binds to registered modalities and component types, de-orphaning the lesson-plan registries.

nodes.name: _resolve_order
nodes.summary: Topologically sorts the selected components over the producer-consumer DAG, failing closed on unknown components, unresolved dependencies, or cycles.

nodes.name: _resolve_excluded_target
nodes.summary: Follows edges through excluded nodes to find the next kept node, guarding against cycles in the walk.

nodes.name: _bridge_edges
nodes.summary: Rewrites manifest edges so paths through excluded components' nodes are bridged directly between kept nodes, deduplicating the result.

nodes.name: _apply_synthetic_attachments
nodes.summary: Splices synthetic component nodes into the edge list after their declared attach-after anchors.

nodes.name: _prune_absent_optional_inputs
nodes.summary: Removes dependency and projection references to specialists whose components were not selected, so composed nodes never demand inputs from absent producers.

nodes.name: compose_manifest
nodes.summary: Core composer: resolves component order from the selection, keeps only owned nodes, bridges edges, applies synthetic attachments, prunes absent optional inputs, and returns the assembled PipelineManifest.

nodes.name: component_content_hash
nodes.summary: Computes a content-addressed SHA-256 for one component from its owned manifest nodes and model-config closure.

nodes.name: compose_and_digest
nodes.summary: Composes the manifest for a selection and produces the two-part digest record: content-addressed input-closure digest plus canonicalized composed-graph topology digest.

nodes.name: verify_composition_replay
nodes.summary: Re-runs compose_and_digest for a recorded TwoPartDigest and raises CompositionReplayError if either digest half no longer matches (tamper-evidence).

nodes.name: ping.py
nodes.summary: MCP ping tool registration that invokes the shared app.runtime.minimal_node exactly as the FastAPI /invoke route does, keeping the Story 1.1d transport-parity assertion falsifiable.
nodes.languageNotes: Load-bearing invariant: never re-implement the no-op — both transports must import the SAME callable for byte-for-byte parity.

nodes.name: register
nodes.summary: Registers the ping tool on an MCP Server, listing its schema and handling calls by invoking minimal_node and returning the JSON payload.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: PipelineRegistry model catalog (architecture D2/D13): each entry carries model identity, provider, context window, tier, pricing, and availability; registry-level invariant ties default_model_id to an available entry.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic v2 with UUID4 enforcement and Decimal pricing; registry-bump tiers (additive vs subtractive vs provider-change) are governance policy documented in the module.

nodes.name: RegistryEntry
nodes.summary: One model catalog entry: model_id, provider and tier closed enums, context window, Decimal pricing, availability flag, and UUID4 identity validation.

nodes.name: PipelineRegistry
nodes.summary: Root registry model holding entries plus default_model_id and auto_select_enabled, with a validator asserting the default resolves to an available entry.

nodes.name: registry_check.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point (python -m app.models.registry_check) that loads app/models/registry.yaml, validates it against PipelineRegistry, and exits 0/1 with named violations on stderr.

nodes.name: load_registry
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the registry YAML file into a PipelineRegistry model.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI main: validates the registry and reports success or the named validation violation, returning the process exit code.

nodes.name: selection_policy.py
nodes.summary: ModelSelectionPolicy for the cascade's auto-select fourth level: when-predicate rules with preferred tier and ordered fallback model chains; conflicting rules fail loudly at YAML load.

nodes.name: SelectionRule
nodes.summary: One auto-select rule: a when predicate matched against a context dict, a preferred tier, and an ordered fallback chain of model ids.

nodes.name: ModelSelectionPolicy
nodes.summary: Policy root holding the rule list with a validator that rejects overlapping when-predicates with incompatible preferred tiers (no silent conflicts, per AC-1.3-B).

nodes.name: selector.py
nodes.summary: Model-cascade resolver (FR17-FR21): per_call override, then per_specialist model_config.yaml, then registry default, then policy-driven auto-select; each resolution produces a ModelResolutionEntry with a deterministic cache-prefix hash.
nodes.languageNotes: NFR-I6: cache_prefix_hash is SHA-256 over sorted-keys canonical JSON — never Python's process-randomized hash().

nodes.name: ResolveResult
nodes.summary: Result pair returned by resolve(): the resolved model_id plus the ModelResolutionEntry audit record.

nodes.name: ModelResolutionError
nodes.summary: Raised when the full cascade (including auto-select) cannot resolve a usable model id.

nodes.name: _compute_cache_prefix_hash
nodes.summary: Deterministic SHA-256 over a sorted-keys canonical-JSON tuple of the resolution inputs, giving stable cache-prefix hashes across processes.

nodes.name: _load_registry
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the PipelineRegistry YAML used by the cascade.

nodes.name: _load_specialist_config
nodes.summary: Loads a specialist's model_config.yaml into SpecialistModelConfig for the per_specialist cascade level, tolerating absent files.

nodes.name: _make_entry
nodes.summary: Builds a ModelResolutionEntry capturing requested/resolved model, cascade level, reason, timestamp, and optional cache-prefix hash.

nodes.name: _try_auto_select
nodes.summary: Consults ModelSelectionPolicy rules against the call context and walks each matching rule's fallback chain for an available model.

nodes.name: resolve
nodes.summary: Main cascade resolver: tries per_call, per_specialist, registry_default, then auto_select in order, validating availability at each level and returning the model id with its resolution audit entry.

nodes.name: specialist_model_config.py
nodes.summary: SpecialistModelConfig Pydantic shape for each specialist's model_config.yaml: preferred default model, optional per-node overrides, and default temperature, read at the per_specialist cascade level.

nodes.name: SpecialistModelConfig
nodes.summary: Frozen per-specialist model preferences: specialist_id, default_model, per_node_overrides, and temperature, with extra fields forbidden.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the replay regression helpers, re-exporting trial discovery types and the regression harness errors/results, with a lazy __getattr__ alias shim.

nodes.name: discovery.py
nodes.summary: Closed-trial discovery for the replay regression harness: enumerates the migration-native replay surface from frozen Marcus baseline fixtures and lists legacy state/config/runs artifacts as reference-only inputs.

nodes.name: TrialRef
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass reference to one discovered closed trial: id, kind, baseline paths, and closure metadata.

nodes.name: _as_trial_ref
nodes.summary: Parses a baseline fixture directory into a TrialRef, validating required files and closure metadata before admitting it as a replayable trial.

nodes.name: list_closed_trials
nodes.summary: Enumerates all closed, replayable trials discovered from the frozen baseline fixture tree.

nodes.name: get_closed_trial
nodes.summary: Looks up a single closed trial by id, raising if it is not among the discovered trials.

nodes.name: list_legacy_run_artifacts
nodes.summary: Lists legacy state/config/runs artifact directories kept as reference-only inputs for later parity work.

nodes.name: regression.py
nodes.summary: Trial-replay regression harness (Slab 5a acceptance): replays closed trials through the local M3 trial runner, projects and compares canonical payloads against frozen baselines, and fails loudly on pack-hash, sanctum-fingerprint, or manifest-digest drift; includes a CLI.
nodes.languageNotes: Two modes (fail-loud vs warn-on-clone); typed drift-error subclasses of ReplayError name the exact invariant that broke.

nodes.name: ReplayError
nodes.summary: Base replay-harness error carrying the trial id and drift context; subclassed by named drift errors (pack hash, sanctum fingerprint, manifest snapshot, budget, no-trials).

nodes.name: ReplayTrialResult
nodes.summary: Per-trial replay outcome record: trial id, status, digests compared, and mismatch details.

nodes.name: ReplayBatchResult
nodes.summary: Aggregate result over all replayed closed trials: per-trial results plus overall pass/fail.

nodes.name: _normalize_clone_sanctum
nodes.summary: Normalizes clone-sanctum fingerprint fields in a payload so warn-on-clone mode can compare replays across sanctum clones.

nodes.name: _project_replay_payload
nodes.summary: Projects a trial envelope into the canonical replay-comparison payload: gate events, verdicts, pack hashes, sanctum fingerprints, and manifest digests, normalized for deterministic comparison.

nodes.name: replay_trial
nodes.summary: Replays one closed trial via run_local_m3_trial, compares the projected payload and live manifest digest against the frozen baseline, and raises the specific named drift error on mismatch.

nodes.name: replay_all_closed_trials
nodes.summary: Discovers and replays every closed trial within a time budget, aggregating per-trial outcomes into a ReplayBatchResult and failing on zero discovered trials.

nodes.name: build_parser
nodes.summary: Builds the argparse parser for the replay CLI (trial selection, mode, budget flags).

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI main: runs single-trial or all-trials replay, prints JSON-able results, and maps drift errors to a non-zero exit code.

nodes.name: compiled_graph_digest.py
nodes.summary: Frozen-graph digest helpers: the legacy single-string compiled-graph digest used by the Slab-5a replay harness, plus the S2 two-part content-addressed digest (input closure + composed topology) with schema-versioned mismatch detection.
nodes.languageNotes: digest_schema_version is embedded so legacy 4-field digests are never silently compared against two-part records (Murat binding).

nodes.name: _normalize_json_value
nodes.summary: Recursively normalizes a value into JSON-stable form (sorted keys, canonical scalars) for deterministic hashing.

nodes.name: canonical_sha256
nodes.summary: Computes the SHA-256 hex digest of a payload's canonical-JSON byte encoding.

nodes.name: _load_dispatch_snapshot
nodes.summary: Loads the specialist dispatch-registry snapshot used as a digest input, tolerating absent registry files.

nodes.name: compute_compiled_graph_digest
nodes.summary: Legacy single-string digest over node ids, edge tuples, versions, and the dispatch snapshot of the whole manifest — the replay harness's manifest-drift sentinel.

nodes.name: TwoPartDigestMismatchError
nodes.summary: Raised when a recorded TwoPartDigest does not match a freshly computed one (composition drift).

nodes.name: TwoPartDigest
nodes.summary: S2 two-part content-addressed digest record: input-closure digest plus composed-graph digest with embedded digest_schema_version.

nodes.name: assert_two_part_digests_match
nodes.summary: Compares two TwoPartDigest records field-by-field (including schema version) and raises TwoPartDigestMismatchError naming the diverging part.

nodes.name: minimal_node.py
nodes.summary: Shared minimal LangGraph no-op node — the single source of truth invoked by the smoke test, the FastAPI /invoke route, and the MCP ping tool so transport-parity tests stay byte-for-byte falsifiable.
nodes.languageNotes: Load-bearing invariant: three call sites must import this exact callable; re-implementing it anywhere silently breaks the 1.1d parity test.

nodes.name: minimal_node
nodes.summary: The no-op node handler returning the canonical smoke payload dict shared by all transports.

nodes.name: server.py
nodes.summary: FastAPI runtime server (Slab 1 substrate): GET /health with Postgres reachability probe and POST /invoke calling the shared minimal_node; bound to 127.0.0.1 only per NFR-S2.

nodes.name: InvokePayload
nodes.summary: Pydantic request body model for the POST /invoke route.

nodes.name: _postgres_status
nodes.summary: Probes Postgres reachability for the health endpoint with skip-not-fail semantics per sandbox-AC discipline.

nodes.name: create_app
nodes.summary: Builds the FastAPI app with /health and /invoke routes, exposing bound_host on app.state for test introspection.

nodes.name: runtime_server.py
nodes.summary: python -m app.runtime_server entry point that boots the FastAPI app via uvicorn on 127.0.0.1, resolving the port from RUNTIME_PORT (default 8765) with named-error validation.

nodes.name: _resolve_port
nodes.summary: Resolves the listen port from the RUNTIME_PORT env var with range validation and a named error on invalid values.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Boots uvicorn with the runtime FastAPI app bound to 127.0.0.1 on the resolved port.

nodes.name: smoke_test.py
nodes.summary: python -m app.smoke_test harness with two modes: default substrate smoke wiring a one-node graph against minimal_node, and --full pipeline smoke compiling the 33-node migrated v4.2 manifest end-to-end through passthrough specialists.

nodes.name: _compile_substrate_graph
nodes.summary: Loads the substrate stub manifest and wires a one-node StateGraph around minimal_node for the default smoke path.

nodes.name: run_smoke
nodes.summary: Runs the substrate smoke graph and returns the canonical 1.1c smoke payload.

nodes.name: run_full_smoke
nodes.summary: Loads the full pipeline manifest and compiles it via the manifest compiler to run the migrated pipeline end-to-end through passthrough specialists (M1 acceptance evidence).

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI main choosing between substrate smoke (default) and --full pipeline smoke, printing the payload and exit status.

nodes.name: specialist_envelope.py
nodes.summary: Defines SpecialistEnvelope, the Pydantic request/response envelope that wraps a single specialist invocation with correlation identity (UUID4 request_id, tz-aware created_at) so the orchestrator can pair outputs to inputs across async boundaries.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic v2 model with extra='forbid', validate_assignment=True, and field_validators delegating to shared _base enforcement helpers.

nodes.name: SpecialistEnvelope
nodes.summary: Base envelope model carrying specialist_id, UUID4 request_id, payload_in, optional payload_out (a SpecialistReturn), and a timezone-aware creation timestamp; validators enforce UUID4 version and tz-awareness.

nodes.name: specialist_return.py
nodes.summary: Defines SpecialistReturn, the Pydantic model for a specialist's response carrying a verb (accept/edit/reject style), payloads, and a tz-aware timestamp, with a cross-field invariant check on verb/payload consistency.
nodes.languageNotes: Cross-field invariants are delegated to a dedicated validators module, keeping the model declaration lean.

nodes.name: SpecialistReturn
nodes.summary: Base return model with specialist_id, verb, payload, edit_payload, reject_reason, and timestamp; validators enforce tz-awareness and verb-vs-payload consistency invariants.

nodes.name: specialist_return_validators.py
nodes.summary: Standalone validator function enforcing that a SpecialistReturn's verb is consistent with its edit_payload and reject_reason fields, raising ValueError on contradictory combinations.

nodes.name: enforce_specialist_verb_payload_consistency
nodes.summary: Checks that the return verb agrees with the presence/absence of edit_payload and reject_reason, raising ValueError when the combination is contradictory (e.g., reject without reason).

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the canonical specialist scaffold, re-exporting the graph builder, state pins, and sanctum-lock exception as the template surface every concrete specialist mirrors.

nodes.name: sanctum_exceptions.py
nodes.summary: Declares SanctumLockViolation, the exception raised when a specialist's sanctum reference files drift from their pinned digest manifest.

nodes.name: SanctumLockViolation
nodes.summary: Exception type signaling that a specialist's sanctum content no longer matches its locked digest.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: State pins for the canonical scaffold specialist: ScaffoldEnvelope and ScaffoldReturn subclass the base envelope/return models and pin specialist_id to '_scaffold' with a 50KB payload cap.
nodes.languageNotes: The specialist-id pin via ClassVar + model_validator(mode='after') is the template pattern replicated by every concrete specialist state module.

nodes.name: ScaffoldEnvelope
nodes.summary: Scaffold request envelope pinning specialist_id to '_scaffold' and enforcing a 50KB serialized payload_in cap.

nodes.name: ScaffoldReturn
nodes.summary: Scaffold return payload pinning specialist_id to '_scaffold' via an after-mode model validator.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Aria specialist, re-exporting its graph builder and envelope/return state models.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: State pins for the Aria specialist: AriaEnvelope and AriaReturn subclass the base models, pin specialist_id to 'aria', and carry the aria_build_spec output field.

nodes.name: AriaEnvelope
nodes.summary: Aria request envelope pinning specialist_id and enforcing the 50KB payload_in cap.

nodes.name: AriaReturn
nodes.summary: Aria return payload with the aria_build_spec field and a specialist-id pin validator.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the CD (creative director) specialist, re-exporting its graph builder and envelope/return state models.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: State pins for the CD specialist: CdEnvelope and CdReturn subclass the base models, pin specialist_id to 'cd', and carry cd_directive plus styleguide_resolution output fields.

nodes.name: CdEnvelope
nodes.summary: CD request envelope pinning specialist_id and enforcing the 50KB payload_in cap.

nodes.name: CdReturn
nodes.summary: CD return payload carrying the cd_directive and styleguide_resolution fields with a specialist-id pin validator.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: State pins for the Compositor specialist: CompositorEnvelope and CompositorReturn subclass the base models, pin specialist_id, and carry the assembly_output field.

nodes.name: CompositorEnvelope
nodes.summary: Compositor request envelope pinning specialist_id via an after-mode model validator.

nodes.name: CompositorReturn
nodes.summary: Compositor return payload carrying the assembly_output field with a specialist-id pin validator.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Dan specialist, re-exporting its graph builder and envelope/return state models.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: State pins for the Dan specialist: DanEnvelope and DanReturn subclass the base models, pin specialist_id to 'dan', and carry the dan_aux output field.

nodes.name: DanEnvelope
nodes.summary: Dan request envelope pinning specialist_id and enforcing the 50KB payload_in cap.

nodes.name: DanReturn
nodes.summary: Dan return payload carrying the dan_aux field with a specialist-id pin validator.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Desmond specialist, re-exporting its graph builder and envelope/return state models.

nodes.summary: State pins for the Desmond specialist: DesmondEnvelope and DesmondReturn subclass the base models, pin specialist_id to 'desmond', and carry the desmond_handoff output field.

nodes.name: DesmondEnvelope
nodes.summary: Desmond request envelope pinning specialist_id and enforcing the 50KB payload_in cap.

nodes.name: DesmondReturn
nodes.summary: Desmond return payload carrying the desmond_handoff field with a specialist-id pin validator.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Enrique specialist, re-exporting its graph builder and envelope/return state models.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: State pins for the Enrique specialist: EnriqueEnvelope and EnriqueReturn subclass the base models, pin specialist_id to 'enrique', and carry the enrique_audio output field.

nodes.name: EnriqueEnvelope
nodes.summary: Enrique request envelope pinning specialist_id and enforcing the 50KB payload_in cap.

nodes.name: EnriqueReturn

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Gary specialist, re-exporting its graph builder and envelope/return state models.

nodes.summary: State pins for the Gary specialist: GaryEnvelope and GaryReturn subclass the base models, pin specialist_id to 'gary', and carry the gary_slide_output field.

nodes.name: GaryEnvelope
nodes.summary: Gary request envelope pinning specialist_id and enforcing the 50KB payload_in cap.

nodes.name: GaryReturn
nodes.summary: Gary return payload carrying the gary_slide_output field with a specialist-id pin validator.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: State pins for the Irene specialist: IreneEnvelope and IreneReturn subclass the base models, pin specialist_id to 'irene', and carry irene_lesson_design plus irene_pass_2_envelope output fields.
nodes.languageNotes: Slightly extended variant of the scaffold state pattern, carrying two output fields to bridge Irene's pass-1/pass-2 lesson-design flow.

nodes.name: IreneEnvelope
nodes.summary: Irene request envelope pinning specialist_id and enforcing the payload_in size cap.

nodes.name: IreneReturn
nodes.summary: Irene return payload carrying irene_lesson_design and irene_pass_2_envelope fields with a specialist-id pin validator.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Irene pass-1 specialist, re-exporting its graph builder and envelope/return state models.

nodes.summary: State pins for the Irene pass-1 specialist: IrenePass1Envelope and IrenePass1Return subclass the base models and carry lesson_plan, locked_scope, learning_events, and artifact_path output fields.

nodes.name: IrenePass1Envelope
nodes.summary: Irene pass-1 request envelope pinning specialist_id and enforcing the 50KB payload_in cap.

nodes.name: IrenePass1Return
nodes.summary: Irene pass-1 return payload carrying lesson_plan, locked_scope, learning_events, and artifact_path fields with a specialist-id pin validator.

nodes.summary: State pins for the Kim specialist: KimEnvelope and KimReturn subclass the base models, pin specialist_id to 'kim', and carry the kim_coursearc output field.

nodes.summary: Kim request envelope pinning specialist_id and enforcing the 50KB payload_in cap.

nodes.name: KimReturn

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Generated package barrel for the kira specialist, re-exporting build_kira_graph plus the KiraEnvelope/KiraReturn state pins as the package's public surface.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: State pins for the kira specialist (text-to-video motion-asset dispatch (Kling)): defines KiraEnvelope and KiraReturn, Pydantic subclasses of the shared specialist envelope/return contracts that pin specialist_id to "kira" and enforce the 50KB payload cap.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic v2 model_validator(mode="after") pins a ClassVar specialist id and enforces a serialized payload-size cap; part of a uniform generated scaffold shared by all specialist packages.

nodes.name: KiraEnvelope
nodes.summary: Dispatch envelope for the kira specialist; subclasses SpecialistEnvelope, pins schema_version 1.0 and specialist_id "kira", and rejects payload_in over the 50KB cap.

nodes.name: KiraReturn
nodes.summary: Return payload for the kira specialist; subclasses SpecialistReturn, pins specialist_id "kira", and carries an optional MP4 motion-asset path produced by Kling dispatch via the motion_asset_path field.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Generated package barrel for the mira specialist, re-exporting build_mira_graph plus the MiraEnvelope/MiraReturn state pins as the package's public surface.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: State pins for the mira specialist (prompt-pack assembly from operator instructions): defines MiraEnvelope and MiraReturn, Pydantic subclasses of the shared specialist envelope/return contracts that pin specialist_id to "mira" and enforce the 50KB payload cap.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic v2 model_validator(mode="after") pins a ClassVar specialist id and enforces a serialized payload-size cap; part of a uniform generated scaffold shared by all specialist packages.

nodes.name: MiraEnvelope
nodes.summary: Dispatch envelope for the mira specialist; subclasses SpecialistEnvelope, pins schema_version 1.0 and specialist_id "mira", and rejects payload_in over the 50KB cap.

nodes.name: MiraReturn
nodes.summary: Return payload for the mira specialist; subclasses SpecialistReturn, pins specialist_id "mira", and carries the assembled Mira prompt pack via the mira_prompt_pack field.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Generated package barrel for the motion planner specialist, re-exporting build_motion_planner_graph plus the MotionPlannerEnvelope/MotionPlannerReturn state pins as the package's public surface.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: State pins for the motion planner specialist (deterministic motion-plan production (07D.5 producer node)): defines MotionPlannerEnvelope and MotionPlannerReturn, Pydantic subclasses of the shared specialist envelope/return contracts that pin specialist_id to "motion_planner" and enforce the 50KB payload cap.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic v2 model_validator(mode="after") pins a ClassVar specialist id and enforces a serialized payload-size cap; part of a uniform generated scaffold shared by all specialist packages.

nodes.name: MotionPlannerEnvelope
nodes.summary: Dispatch envelope for the motion planner specialist; subclasses SpecialistEnvelope, pins schema_version 1.0 and specialist_id "motion_planner", and rejects payload_in over the 50KB cap.

nodes.name: MotionPlannerReturn
nodes.summary: Return payload for the motion planner specialist; subclasses SpecialistReturn, pins specialist_id "motion_planner", and carries the produced motion plan via the motion_plan field.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Generated package barrel for the quinn r specialist, re-exporting build_quinn_r_graph plus the QuinnREnvelope/QuinnRReturn state pins as the package's public surface.

nodes.name: state.py
nodes.summary: State pins for the quinn r specialist (LLM-backed review (QRR) of composition artifacts): defines QuinnREnvelope and QuinnRReturn, Pydantic subclasses of the shared specialist envelope/return contracts that pin specialist_id to "quinn_r" and enforce the 50KB payload cap.

nodes.name: QuinnREnvelope

nodes.name: ask_a_enrichment.py
nodes.summary: Strict, model-free Pydantic contracts for the workbook Ask-A research seam: Scite query derivation, retrieval scope, cited knowledge entries, execution receipts, and the contribution output with closed retryable/completed disposition rules.
nodes.languageNotes: Frozen strict Pydantic v2 models with model_validator invariants and Literal-closed disposition enums; digest-bound identity throughout.

nodes.name: AskARetrievalScopeV1
nodes.summary: Versioned retrieval scope binding the derived Scite query, posture, and ability inputs under a scope digest so any scope change invalidates downstream identity.

nodes.name: derive_scite_query
nodes.summary: Deterministically derives the Scite literature-search query text from lesson ability inputs for the Ask-A research dispatch.

nodes.name: build_scope
nodes.summary: Builds the digest-bound AskARetrievalScopeV1 from ability inputs and posture, computing the canonical scope digest.

nodes.name: match_scope_associations
nodes.summary: Token-matches retrieved evidence text against scope ability tokens to compute deterministic ability associations for a knowledge entry.

nodes.name: AskAKnowledgeEntryV1
nodes.summary: One cited knowledge-pool row (title, DOI, evidence excerpt, ability/bold-term associations) with entry invariants enforced at construction.

nodes.name: AskAExecutionReceiptV1
nodes.summary: Execution receipt recording how the Ask-A dispatch ran (provider, counts, cost posture) with a build factory and receipt-binding validator.

nodes.name: AskAResearchIntakeV1
nodes.summary: Intake artifact binding the retrieval scope, accepted knowledge entries, and indexed losses under a digest so the research packet mint is reproducible.

nodes.name: AskAContributionOutputV1
nodes.summary: Terminal Ask-A contribution with closed disposition rules: retryable (demand not ready, dispatch disabled, credentials unavailable) vs completed (empty, degraded, ready) built via typed factories.

nodes.name: ask_b_hot_topics.py
nodes.summary: Strict contracts for the Ask-B late hot-topics research seam (38.2): narrower than Ask-A (no bold terms, no skeleton binding), with a versioned deterministic ability-association token rule that participates in the scope digest.
nodes.languageNotes: The ability-association algorithm version (ask-b-association.v1) is folded into the scope digest so any rule change invalidates all downstream digests.

nodes.name: AskBAbilityTokenMatchV1
nodes.summary: Records the matched ability tokens for one entry-to-ability association, requiring a non-empty token set.

nodes.name: AskBRetrievalScopeV1
nodes.summary: Ability/scene-scoped Ask-B retrieval scope with derived hot-topics query and digest binding covering the association algorithm version.

nodes.name: derive_hot_topics_query
nodes.summary: Deterministically derives the hot-topics research query from scoped ability and scene inputs for the Ask-B dispatch.

nodes.name: build_scope
nodes.summary: Builds the digest-bound AskBRetrievalScopeV1 from ability/scene inputs, embedding the association algorithm version in the digest.

nodes.name: match_ability_associations
nodes.summary: Applies the ask-b-association.v1 token rule: matches stored evidence-window text (title + excerpt) against scope ability tokens to assign supports_ability_ids.

nodes.name: AskBKnowledgeEntryV1
nodes.summary: One Ask-B hot-topics knowledge row with per-ability matched-token records; entries with zero ability association are rejected into an indexed loss.

nodes.name: AskBExecutionReceiptV1
nodes.summary: Execution receipt for the Ask-B dispatch with build factory and receipt-binding invariants.

nodes.name: AskBResearchIntakeV1
nodes.summary: Intake artifact binding the Ask-B scope, accepted entries, and losses under a reproducible digest for the hot-topics packet mint.

nodes.name: AskBContributionOutputV1
nodes.summary: Terminal Ask-B contribution with closed retryable/completed disposition rules mirroring the Ask-A contract shape.

nodes.name: check_on_learning_projection.py
nodes.summary: Pure Promise-bound retrieval-check (check-on-learning) contract/writer/gate seam (Story 37.3): source-grounded answer keys, an extensive candidate gate detecting answer leaks, enumeration tricks and numeric/negation drift, and anti-forgery result binding.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately side-effect free: no runtime, filesystem, provider, or persistence work; gates use unicode-normalization witnesses to detect leaked answers.

nodes.name: RetrievalCheckItem
nodes.summary: One retrieval-check question with its atomic source-grounded answer claim; validates the answer is atomic and traceable.

nodes.name: CheckOnLearningRequest
nodes.summary: Closed-authority request binding the Promise projection result, source claims, and answer claims that a check writer may draw from.

nodes.name: CheckOnLearningWriterCandidate
nodes.summary: Honest writer-candidate boundary: the writer's proposed check items with declared claim references, validated before gating.

nodes.name: CheckGateReceipt
nodes.summary: Gate receipt reconciling pass/fail status with itemized diagnostics; a receipt whose status contradicts its recorded failures is rejected at construction.

nodes.name: _numeric_witnesses
nodes.summary: Extracts normalized numeric witnesses from text (handling unicode digits, separators, and formats) so the gate can compare question/answer numerics against source claims.

nodes.name: _candidate_gate
nodes.summary: The main candidate gate: verifies claim bindings, detects answer leakage (supplied alternatives, unicode/symbol enumeration, complete copies), and validates numeric and negation fidelity against source claims.

nodes.name: offline_check_on_learning_writer
nodes.summary: Honest offline writer seam that declares the check unavailable rather than fabricating content when no live writer is wired.

nodes.name: compose_check_on_learning
nodes.summary: Top-level composition: runs the pre-gate on the Promise authority, invokes the writer, gates the candidate, and returns a digest-bound CheckOnLearningResult with anti-forgery replay binding.

nodes.name: deep_dive_enrichment.py
nodes.summary: Strict contracts and pure A2 citation-coverage gate for Deep Dive enrichment (37.2b): enriched claims are typed at the writer boundary as skeleton-inherited or pool-cited (never neither/both), with markdown rendering and the workbook-review contribution artifact.
nodes.languageNotes: Honesty boundary declared in-module: the machine gate proves citation-binding arithmetic only; semantic follow-from remains an operator prose spot-check WARN.

nodes.name: DeepDiveSkeletonBindingV1
nodes.summary: Digest-bound binding of the authored 37.2a skeleton (sections, claims, bold terms) that enrichment builds on; exposes the skeleton's source-claim ref set.

nodes.name: DeepDiveEnrichmentRequestV1
nodes.summary: Bound enrichment request combining the skeleton binding, the Ask-A cited pool rows, and overlay-covered objectives under digest identity.

nodes.name: EnrichedDeepDiveClaim
nodes.summary: Typed writer-boundary claim: carries either inherited skeleton source_claim_refs (role skeleton) or pool citation_refs (role enrichment) — construction rejects claims with neither or both.

nodes.name: DeepDiveEnrichedWriterResult
nodes.summary: Honest local shape of the enrichment writer's output: enriched sections with typed claims and bold terms, validated before the coverage gate runs.

nodes.name: DeepDiveEnrichmentGateReceipt
nodes.summary: Reconciled gate receipt for the enrichment coverage gate: per-claim citation bindings, coverage arithmetic, and failure rows that must agree with the pass/fail status.

nodes.name: deep_dive_enrichment_gate
nodes.summary: The A2 citation-coverage gate: verifies every enrichment claim's citation_refs resolve into the Ask-A pool, skeleton claims are inherited faithfully, bold-term and marker discipline holds, and coverage arithmetic reconciles — including defense-in-depth rows for hand-built payloads that bypass model construction.

nodes.name: DeepDiveEnrichedResultV1
nodes.summary: Honest terminal enrichment result binding the request, gated writer output, and gate receipt under a shared digest.

nodes.name: compose_deep_dive_enrichment
nodes.summary: Composition entry point: invokes the enrichment writer on a bound request, runs the coverage gate, and assembles the DeepDiveEnrichedResultV1 (or a failed disposition).

nodes.name: build_deep_dive_enrichment_request
nodes.summary: Builds the enrichment request from the skeleton result, research packet pool rows, and overlay-covered input, computing the binding digests.

nodes.name: WorkbookReviewContributionV1
nodes.summary: Persistable workbook-review contribution artifact reconciling the enrichment result, execution receipt, and disposition for downstream consumers (glossary, review band).

nodes.name: load_workbook_review_contribution
nodes.summary: Loads and revalidates a persisted WorkbookReviewContributionV1 from a run directory, rejecting shape-corrupt payloads.

nodes.name: render_deep_dive_markdown
nodes.summary: Renders the enriched (or degraded-skeleton) Deep Dive to workbook Markdown, stripping citation markers from learner-facing prose.

nodes.name: deep_dive_enrichment_provider_contract.py
nodes.summary: Pure provider-payload identity and normalization for the Deep Dive enrichment LLM surface (37.2b), with its own contract mode and normalizer version (v2 adds first-appearance bold_terms reordering) so journal identity never conflates the skeleton and enrichment surfaces.
nodes.languageNotes: Reuses canonicalization/digest helpers from deep_dive_provider_contract by design (do not re-derive).

nodes.name: _prose_first_appearance_terms
nodes.summary: Scans enriched prose for bold-term markers and returns terms ordered by first appearance, used to reorder the bold_terms metadata deterministically.

nodes.name: _reordered_to_first_appearance
nodes.summary: Reorders the provider's bold_terms metadata to prose first-appearance order (the v2 normalizer behavior change) without altering term content.

nodes.name: normalize_deep_dive_enrichment_provider_payload
nodes.summary: Normalizes a raw enrichment-writer provider payload into canonical journal-identity form under the v2 normalizer version, applying the first-appearance reorder.

nodes.name: deep_dive_from_run.py
nodes.summary: M3-safe adapter from frozen lesson authority on disk to the DeepDiveSkeletonRequest: loads transcript segments and slide inventory via a duplicate-key-safe YAML loader and maps slide-authority paths to authenticated source claims.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a custom yaml SafeLoader subclass that rejects duplicate mapping keys to prevent silent authority overwrite.

nodes.name: _speaker_note
nodes.summary: Extracts and canonicalizes the speaker-note narration text for a slide from the frozen authority, tolerating format variants while rejecting ambiguity.

nodes.name: _slide_inventory
nodes.summary: Builds the ordered slide inventory from the slide-authority map, validating containment and ordinal consistency.

nodes.name: _load_segments
nodes.summary: Loads transcript segments from the run's frozen sources using the duplicate-key-safe YAML loader, raising typed authority errors on invalid or unavailable input.

nodes.name: build_deep_dive_request
nodes.summary: Main adapter: assembles the DeepDiveSkeletonRequest from frozen lesson authority — narration source spans, source claims from authenticated slide sources, ability inputs, and the Promise projection — with full containment validation.
