nodes.name: deep_dive_projection.py
nodes.summary: Pure source-bound contracts and gates for the Deep Dive skeleton (37.2a): request/result models, bold-term markers, the skeleton gate verifying claim refs and numeric/negation fidelity, plus deterministic and offline writer seams.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately dependency-free (no runtime/provider/persistence imports); it is the hub contract module the rest of the deep-dive family imports.

nodes.name: DeepDiveSkeletonRequest
nodes.summary: Closed-authority skeleton request: narration spans, source claims, ability inputs, and bold-term markers a writer may credit — only declared IDs earn credit.

nodes.name: DeepDiveSkeletonWriterResult
nodes.summary: Honest local shape of the skeleton writer's output — ability sections of claims with declared refs and marked bold terms — validated before gating.

nodes.name: DeepDiveGateReceipt
nodes.summary: Skeleton gate receipt whose pass/fail status must match its recorded failure rows; carries authority and candidate digests for replay verification.

nodes.name: DeepDiveSkeletonResult
nodes.summary: Honest terminal skeleton result binding request, gated writer output, status (authored/degraded/unavailable), and gate receipt.

nodes.name: DeepDiveWriter
nodes.summary: Protocol for skeleton writers: callable from request to writer candidate, implemented by deterministic, offline, and live LLM writers.

nodes.name: _gate
nodes.summary: The skeleton gate: verifies every claim's declared refs exist in the request authority, checks numeric witnesses and negation counts against sources, and validates bold-term marker discipline.

nodes.name: deterministic_deep_dive_writer
nodes.summary: Deterministic non-LLM writer that distributes authority claims across ability sections and safely bold-wraps marked terms — a reproducible baseline writer.

nodes.name: offline_deep_dive_writer
nodes.summary: Honest offline writer seam that declares the Deep Dive unavailable instead of fabricating prose when no live writer is wired.

nodes.name: compose_deep_dive_skeleton
nodes.summary: Composition entry point: invokes a DeepDiveWriter on the request, runs the gate, and returns the digest-bound DeepDiveSkeletonResult or a failed-result disposition.

nodes.name: deep_dive_provider_contract.py
nodes.summary: Pure provider-payload identity and normalization for Deep Dive skeleton execution: canonical JSON mapping, payload digest, and the v1 normalizer that fixes raw provider JSON into journal-identity form.

nodes.name: canonical_json_mapping
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes a JSON mapping (sorted keys, compact separators, no NaN) and round-trips it so equal payloads always produce identical bytes.

nodes.name: provider_payload_digest
nodes.summary: Computes the SHA-256 identity digest of a canonicalized provider payload for journal binding.

nodes.name: normalize_deep_dive_provider_payload
nodes.summary: Normalizes the raw skeleton-writer provider payload (bold-term markers, shape fixes) into the canonical v1 journal-identity form.

nodes.name: drill_enrichment.py
nodes.summary: Projects frozen G0 enrichment cards into DrillSpec inputs (Mine 5): read-only card consumption preferring quiz/assessment-like components, with no network or model calls and M3 import-safety (no orchestrator imports).

nodes.name: DrillEnrichmentProjection
nodes.summary: Dataclass result of the enrichment-to-drill projection: the produced DrillSpec plus warnings surfaced during projection.

nodes.name: project_enrichment_to_drill_inputs
nodes.summary: Loads the frozen enrichment card and projects LO-linked practice items into a DrillSpec, preferring quiz/exercise components and falling back to slides, sanitizing IDs along the way.

nodes.name: drill_producer.py
nodes.summary: Renders a validated DrillSpec to its Markdown artifact family (Mine 5) — short practice items only, no revoicing or DOCX — refusing silent zero-byte success on empty drills.

nodes.name: render_drill_markdown
nodes.summary: Renders a schema-validated DrillSpec to canonical Markdown, raising DrillProducerError on an empty drill instead of emitting a hollow artifact.

nodes.name: write_drill_artifact
nodes.summary: Writes the rendered drill Markdown to the target artifact path.

nodes.name: drill_spec.py
nodes.summary: Minimal drill collateral content model (Mine 5): LO-linked short practice items with prompt, expected_focus, and source refs — a distinct artifact family from WorkbookSpec, schema version 0.1.

nodes.name: DrillSourceRef
nodes.summary: One source anchor for a drill practice item: an open-ID-patterned ref_id plus a non-empty locator.

nodes.name: DrillPracticeItem
nodes.summary: One LO-linked practice item with prompt, expected focus, and source refs; whitespace-only fields are rejected.

nodes.name: DrillSpec
nodes.summary: Top-level drill artifact schema (kind closed to drill, version 0.1) holding titled collections of practice items.

nodes.name: glossary_projection.py
nodes.summary: Term-keyed encyclopedia glossary downstream of the Deep Dive (W2/39.1): exactly one entry per bolded Deep Dive term composed only from association-covered Ask-A pool rows, with status-dependent bold-term authority, lean uncovered rendering, and a retained legacy generic-packet path for frozen evidence scripts.
nodes.languageNotes: The legacy pre-39.1 path (project_glossary_articles / default_glossary_writer) is retired from the learner deliverable and retained only for frozen evidence scripts — regression pins reject rewiring it.

nodes.name: project_glossary_articles
nodes.summary: LEGACY pre-39.1 generic-packet glossary projection retained only for frozen W2/W4 evidence scripts; no longer part of the learner deliverable.

nodes.name: GlossaryTermEntry
nodes.summary: One term-keyed glossary entry: headword byte-exact to the bolded Deep Dive term, body composed from covering pool rows, with coverage honesty fields.

nodes.name: GlossaryProjection
nodes.summary: The full glossary projection: ordered term entries plus coverage summary and typed losses; exposes the covered-articles view.

nodes.name: default_term_glossary_writer
nodes.summary: Default deterministic term-glossary writer: synthesizes each entry body from the pool rows whose supports_bold_terms contains the term, without model calls.

nodes.name: project_glossary_entries_for_terms
nodes.summary: Core 39.1 projection: for each bolded term, composes exactly one entry from association-covered pool rows, marking uncovered terms honest-lean and recording typed glossary_term_uncovered losses.

nodes.name: glossary_projection_from_contribution
nodes.summary: Resolves status-dependent bold-term authority from the 07W.3 workbook-review contribution (enriched result terms, degraded skeleton terms, or explicitly-empty) and runs the term projection accordingly.

nodes.name: render_glossary_projection_markdown
nodes.summary: Renders the term-keyed glossary projection to workbook Markdown with the section coverage lead line and lean uncovered-term entries.

nodes.name: glossary_reference_lines
nodes.summary: Builds deduplicated provenance/reference lines (title, DOI fragment) for the sources cited across glossary entries.

nodes.name: render_glossary_markdown
nodes.summary: Legacy-path Markdown renderer for article-brief glossaries, retained for the frozen evidence/compose seam.

nodes.name: lesson_type_classifier.py
nodes.summary: Pure, closed lesson-type classification for pre-work Scene composition: maps evidence to fresh_pain / bridge_identity / skill_build lesson types (and their scene archetypes) with a decisive/insufficient/ambiguous disposition.

nodes.name: LessonTypeEvidence
nodes.summary: Traceable evidence item supporting a lesson-type verdict, with unique non-blank source refs enforced.

nodes.name: LessonTypeClassification
nodes.summary: Closed-disposition classification result binding lesson type, scene archetype, status, and its supporting evidence; contradictory combinations are rejected at construction.

nodes.name: classify_lesson_type
nodes.summary: Pure classifier producing a LessonTypeClassification from lesson evidence, declaring insufficient or ambiguous rather than guessing.

nodes.name: pass1_authority.py
nodes.summary: Canonical durable-identity receipt for Irene Pass-1 lesson plans: turns the retained production-envelope history into a digest-bound receipt so later Pass-1 refinements can reject reuse of identities that disappeared from an intermediate plan. Validation is pure — it never repairs or mutates a plan.

nodes.name: assert_unique_plan_unit_ids
nodes.summary: Raises Pass1PlanAuthorityError if a lesson plan contains duplicate plan-unit identifiers.

nodes.name: validate_receipt
nodes.summary: Validates a Pass-1 plan-authority receipt end to end: version, identity rows, cluster authority, v2 projection shape, and catalog history — rejecting any receipt that misrepresents the retained plan.

nodes.name: finalize_plan_authority
nodes.summary: Builds the finalized digest-bound plan-authority receipt from the current plan and its source-span catalog, including authenticated anchor-source resolution via the slide authority.

nodes.name: assert_receipt_matches_plan
nodes.summary: Asserts that a previously issued authority receipt still matches the presented plan (digest and identity rows), blocking refinements that resurrect vanished identities.

nodes.name: pass1_call_journal.py
nodes.summary: Crash-safe provider-call evidence journal for Irene Pass-1: committed before dispatch and again after the provider responds, with secret scrubbing, atomic fsynced writes, identity validation, and deterministic replay binding — a pre-dispatch record without a response can never authorize an automatic recall.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses atomic temp-file writes with directory fsync and versioned processor identities (v1/v2) so journal replays are only honored through deterministic validation.

nodes.name: validate_provider_evidence
nodes.summary: Validates the provider-response evidence block (usage metadata, response id, model identity) before it may be journaled.

nodes.name: build_pass1_call_identity
nodes.summary: Builds the digest-bound call identity (prompt digest, model config digest, schema version) that pins one Pass-1 provider dispatch to its journal record.

nodes.name: validate_pass1_call_journal
nodes.summary: Full journal validation: shape, identity, state transitions, and processing consistency — distinguishing safe replayable records from intentionally ambiguous pre-dispatch-only records.

nodes.name: _validate_completed_output_binding
nodes.summary: Verifies a completed journal's output binding: the recorded candidate processing must deterministically re-derive from the raw provider response under the declared processor version.

nodes.name: begin_or_resume_pass1_call
nodes.summary: Opens a new journal (committed before dispatch) or resumes an existing one for the same call identity, returning a Pass1CallResume describing the safe next action.

nodes.name: record_pass1_response
nodes.summary: Atomically journals the raw provider response and its scrubbed evidence immediately after return, closing the ambiguity window.

nodes.name: record_pass1_candidate_processing
nodes.summary: Journals the deterministic candidate-processing outcome (parsed/normalized response) under the current processor version, validating it against the recorded raw response.

nodes.name: complete_pass1_call
nodes.summary: Marks the journal completed with the final output binding, after which replay is only honored through deterministic validation.

nodes.name: pass1_source_span_catalog.py
nodes.summary: Deterministic exact-source-span selection for Irene Pass-1: the model only chooses stable span identities while this module projects the literal source_refs bytes from the same authenticated source sections the authority validator uses.

nodes.name: Pass1SourceSpanV1
nodes.summary: One catalog span with stable identity, byte range, and digest-validated identity fields tying the span to its authenticated source section.

nodes.name: Pass1SourceSpanCatalogV1
nodes.summary: Versioned catalog of source spans with catalog-level validation (unique identities, digest binding to the source sections).

nodes.name: build_pass1_source_span_catalog
nodes.summary: Builds the span catalog deterministically from authenticated source sections: sentence ranges and word chunks become candidate spans with stable IDs and digests.

nodes.name: project_pass1_source_ref_ids
nodes.summary: Projects model-chosen span identities into the literal source_refs bytes from the catalog, so the model never authors ref bytes directly.

nodes.name: prework_artifact.py
nodes.summary: Versioned, digest-bound handoff between workbook authoring and rendering: the WorkbookBrief payload/artifact models, writer and deep-dive execution receipts, runtime-context persistence, and exclusive atomic file writes.

nodes.name: DeepDiveExecutionReceiptV1
nodes.summary: Execution receipt for the Deep Dive writer with cost-posture and fallback-posture invariants, recording how the contribution was produced.

nodes.name: deep_dive_idempotency_key
nodes.summary: Derives the deterministic idempotency key for a Deep Dive dispatch from the request digest and run identity, preventing duplicate paid calls.

nodes.name: WorkbookBriefRuntimeContext
nodes.summary: Persisted runtime context for workbook-brief authoring (run identity, source roots, origin validation) with a persisted-form projection.

nodes.name: WorkbookBriefPayloadV1
nodes.summary: The versioned workbook-brief payload reconciling pre-work brief, deep-dive result, lesson-type classification, and authoring receipts into one digest-bound handoff.

nodes.name: workbook_brief_contribution_receipt
nodes.summary: Builds the digest-anchored contribution receipt for a workbook brief so orchestrator wiring can verify what was persisted.

nodes.name: read_runtime_context
nodes.summary: Reads and revalidates the persisted WorkbookBriefRuntimeContext from a run directory, rejecting corrupt or mis-originated context.

nodes.name: prework_from_run.py
nodes.summary: Explicit-root adapter for the presentation-support pre-work authoring node: parses the Part-2 scene source (Q5 prompt blocks, payoff slide inventory, forbidden resolution spans) from run files with source-child path validation.

nodes.name: Part2SceneSource
nodes.summary: Frozen container for the parsed Part-2 scene source: raw candidates, payoff slide inventory/keys, and forbidden resolution spans.

nodes.name: load_part2_scene_source
nodes.summary: Loads and regex-parses the Part-2 scene source from run files (Q5 prompt/payoff blocks), validating paths as source children and assembling the Part2SceneSource.

nodes.name: prework_projection.py
nodes.summary: Strict, deterministic pre-work projection contracts for workbook producers: Scene/Promise briefs, composer and transformer protocols, honest offline seams, and pure Markdown projection — M3-safe with runtime wiring deferred to integration stories.

nodes.name: SceneBrief
nodes.summary: The pre-work Scene brief with honest availability status (authored/degraded/unavailable) — content fields are only allowed when the status claims authorship.

nodes.name: PromiseProjection
nodes.summary: The Promise beat projection: ordered promise vows with honest availability, consumed by pre-work rendering and downstream check/reflection seams.

nodes.name: SceneComposeRequest
nodes.summary: Request contract for Scene composition pairing lesson-type classification with objective inputs; paired lesson-shape invariants enforced.

nodes.name: PreWorkBrief
nodes.summary: The assembled pre-work brief (scene, friction scale, promise) whose provenance must match its nested contracts' provenance.

nodes.name: offline_scene_composer
nodes.summary: Honest offline Scene composer seam returning an unavailable-status SceneBrief instead of fabricated prose.

nodes.name: render_prework_markdown
nodes.summary: Pure Markdown projection of the PreWorkBrief in the fixed scene → friction-scale → promise beat order, with honest not-authored stubs.

nodes.name: promise_projection.py
nodes.summary: Strict ratified-objective resolution and pure Promise composition (36.3): resolves which learning objectives are ratified/eligible from plan authority, then composes the Promise projection through a transformer with spoiler and fidelity gating.

nodes.name: PromiseObjectiveResolution
nodes.summary: Honest resolution result listing eligible ratified objectives and per-objective ineligibility reasons drawn from plan authority.

nodes.name: resolve_promise_objectives
nodes.summary: Reads the frozen plan authority and resolves ratified learning objectives eligible for the Promise, validating event types and recording typed ineligibility reasons.

nodes.name: compose_promise_projection
nodes.summary: Composes the Promise projection: runs the transformer over resolved objectives and gates the result for spoilers, numeric drift, and vow shape before returning the projection result.

nodes.name: reflection_projection.py
nodes.summary: Pure Promise-bound closing-reflection projection (Story 37.4): shifted-question prompts with ability cues, an ownership-preflighted model graph, markdown-injection-safe vow text handling, a reconciled gate, and careful asyncio coroutine-disposal machinery for rejected writer candidates.
nodes.languageNotes: Contains unusually defensive machinery: exact-model ownership preflight against subclass spoofing, CommonMark structural checks on vow text, and trusted-base-future cancellation for rejected async writer tasks.

nodes.name: _safe_vow_text
nodes.summary: Sanitizes vow text for safe markdown embedding: rejects thematic breaks, setext underlines, raw HTML block starts, and invisible-only content.

nodes.name: _preflight_owned_graph
nodes.summary: Preflights the request's model graph requiring exact owned model types (not subclasses) on every field, defeating spoofed models that could smuggle unvalidated state past the gate.

nodes.name: ReflectionRequest
nodes.summary: Closed-authority reflection request binding the validated Promise and pre-work brief via recursive-authority validation.

nodes.name: ReflectionWriterCandidate
nodes.summary: Honest writer-candidate boundary for the closing reflection: proposed shifted-question prompt content validated before gating.

nodes.name: ShiftedQuestionPrompt
nodes.summary: The shifted-question reflection prompt with its clauses and required ability cues; prompts without cues are rejected.

nodes.name: ReflectionGateReceipt
nodes.summary: Reconciled reflection gate receipt whose status must agree with its recorded failure diagnostics and digest bindings.

nodes.name: _gate
nodes.summary: The reflection candidate gate: verifies Promise binding validity, ability-cue coverage, and vow-text safety against the request authority.

nodes.name: ReflectionResult
nodes.summary: Terminal reflection result with anti-forgery replay validation: the recorded receipt must re-derive from the bound request and candidate digests.

nodes.name: compose_closing_reflection
nodes.summary: Composition entry point: preflights the owned model graph, runs the pre-gate, invokes the writer (disposing rejected async candidates safely), gates the candidate, and returns the digest-bound ReflectionResult.

nodes.name: research_demand.py
nodes.summary: Projects the 07W.1 Deep Dive skeleton into strict, digest-bound Ask-A research demand and a mirrored Ask-B hot-topics demand, reconciling execution receipts into ready/non-ready dispositions.
nodes.languageNotes: Closed-shape Pydantic v1-suffixed contracts with model_validator-enforced digest binding.

nodes.name: AskAResearchDemandV1
nodes.summary: Closed-shape Pydantic contract describing the Ask-A research demand derived from the deep-dive skeleton, with digest fields pinning its provenance.

nodes.name: AskBHotTopicsDemandV1
nodes.summary: Closed-shape Pydantic contract for the Ask-B hot-topics research demand, mirroring the Ask-A demand at its own coordinates.

nodes.name: _reconcile_execution
nodes.summary: Reconciles a prior Ask-A execution receipt against the freshly derived demand, deciding whether the demand is already satisfied or must re-dispatch.

nodes.name: resolve_enrichment_demand
nodes.summary: Resolves the Ask-A enrichment research demand from the deep-dive skeleton and workbook-brief artifacts, returning a ready demand or an explicit non-ready disposition.

nodes.name: resolve_hot_topics_demand
nodes.summary: Resolves the Ask-B hot-topics demand from run artifacts, mirroring resolve_enrichment_demand for the 07W.4 coordinate.

nodes.name: research_packet.py
nodes.summary: Shape-pinned reader facade that loads coordinate-selected shared research packets (generic, Ask-A, Ask-B) from run.json so every consumer witnesses the same packet_digest; deliberately avoids orchestrator imports.

nodes.name: ResearchPacket
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass-style container for a validated research packet with rows, provenance, and a usability predicate.

nodes.name: load_research_packet
nodes.summary: Loads and shape-validates the research packet contribution at an exact specialist coordinate from run.json, computing the shared packet digest and failing loud on malformed entries.

nodes.name: resolve_for_consumer
nodes.summary: Shared consumer resolver that binds a named consumer to the packet identity so all consumers share one validation and digest path.

nodes.name: resolve_for_hot_topics
nodes.summary: Named resolver returning the Ask-B hot-topics packet at the ask_b_hot_topics@07W.4 coordinate for the trends projector.

nodes.name: review_projection.py
nodes.summary: Strict deterministic contracts for the workbook five-beat Review frame: typed beat models, writer protocol seams, and an honest-status brief builder plus markdown renderer.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses typing.Protocol writer seams so semantic writers stay injectable while the shell remains deterministic.

nodes.name: BookendBeat
nodes.summary: Typed beat model for a review bookend with an honest-status validator that refuses dishonest completion claims.

nodes.name: ReviewBrief
nodes.summary: Aggregate model holding the five review beats that form the workbook review shell.

nodes.name: ReviewWriterResult
nodes.summary: Writer result model that requires an explicit loss reason whenever a semantic writer declines to author.

nodes.name: build_review_brief
nodes.summary: Projects typed pre-work authority into the deterministic five-beat ReviewBrief shell without invoking semantic writers.

nodes.name: render_review_markdown
nodes.summary: Renders a ReviewBrief into the learner-facing review markdown section.

nodes.name: scene_extraction.py
nodes.summary: Traceable scene extraction for pre-work authoring: normalizes scene candidates from source inventory, enforces faithfulness adequacy gates, and exposes the closed scene-composer seam.

nodes.name: SceneSeed
nodes.summary: Validated scene-seed candidate whose scenario flag must match the declared source kind.

nodes.name: SceneProjectionRequest
nodes.summary: Request model built from raw scene candidates with unique inventory-coordinate enforcement, feeding scene composition.

nodes.name: normalize_scene_candidates
nodes.summary: Normalizes raw scene candidate mappings into validated SceneSeed entries, recording drops in a normalization receipt.

nodes.name: _faithfulness_failures
nodes.summary: Token-overlap faithfulness check that lists concrete failures when composed scene text drifts from its source span.

nodes.name: compose_scene_projection
nodes.summary: Runs the full scene projection: seed selection, composer invocation, faithfulness and innocence gates, degrading to an honest degraded scene when gates fail.

nodes.name: slide_authority.py
nodes.summary: Builds and persists the exact final-slide to authored source-slide authority map, refusing ordinal/title/fuzzy joins and resolving each final slide through package-builder unit references and literal source anchors.
nodes.languageNotes: Persistence path uses O_EXCL-style atomic writes with directory fsync and containment-validated file reads.

nodes.name: WorkbookSlideAuthorityRowV1
nodes.summary: Closed-shape row binding one final slide to its authenticated source-slide identity and digest.

nodes.name: WorkbookSlideAuthorityMapV1
nodes.summary: Closed-shape authority map over all final slides with cross-row invariants and a stable map-level digest.

nodes.name: read_slide_authority_map
nodes.summary: Reads and validates the persisted slide-authority map from the run directory, failing loud on shape or digest mismatch.

nodes.name: write_or_validate_slide_authority_map
nodes.summary: Atomically persists the slide-authority map, or validates byte-identity against an already-persisted map to keep the artifact write-once.

nodes.name: resolve_exact_anchor_source
nodes.summary: Resolves a plan unit's literal source anchors against the source inventory, returning the exact authenticated source slide or an explicit failure.

nodes.name: build_slide_authority_map
nodes.summary: Constructs the full slide-authority map by walking final slides through package-builder unit references and exact anchor resolution, recording explicit source identities per slide.

nodes.name: trends_projection.py
nodes.summary: Projects Ask-B research-packet rows into grounded trends and hot-topics workbook backmatter, rejecting model-prior-only topics and rendering a bounded callout section.

nodes.name: ResearchTrendClaim
nodes.summary: Model for a single packet-grounded trend claim carrying topic, confidence, and provenance.

nodes.name: ResearchTrendsBrief
nodes.summary: Aggregate trends brief holding trend claims and hot-topic callouts with a usability predicate.

nodes.name: reject_model_prior_topic
nodes.summary: Rejects trend topics not grounded in wrangled packet rows, enforcing the no-model-prior-fabrication rule.

nodes.name: project_trends_from_packet
nodes.summary: Coordinate-agnostic projection of a research packet into a ResearchTrendsBrief, deriving trend claims and bounded hot-topic callouts from packet rows only.

nodes.name: trends_inputs_from_run
nodes.summary: Run-level intake that reads the Ask-B hot-topics packet at the exact 07W.4 coordinate via the strict resolver and produces trends inputs.

nodes.name: render_trends_markdown
nodes.summary: Renders the trends brief into the workbook backmatter markdown section, including provenance lines.

nodes.name: workbook_enrichment.py
nodes.summary: P5 consumption layer that reads the frozen operator-confirmed G0 enrichment card and projects citation access URLs and pedagogy Bloom levels into workbook producer inputs, plus run-envelope readers and corpus-native further-reading derivation.
nodes.languageNotes: Encodes the Irene honesty bar: a field is honestly consumed only if changing it changes the rendered artifact.

nodes.name: WorkbookEnrichmentProjection
nodes.summary: Projection container carrying further-reading entries, exercise inputs, and LO Bloom bindings derived from the enrichment card.

nodes.name: load_enrichment_card
nodes.summary: Loads the frozen G0EnrichmentResult card payload from the run directory for downstream projection.

nodes.name: _strip_answer_leak
nodes.summary: Scrubs answer leakage from exercise prompts, routing answers to the answer key so learner-facing exercises never reveal solutions inline.

nodes.name: project_enrichment_to_workbook_inputs
nodes.summary: Pure single-source projection of the enrichment card into workbook producer render inputs, wiring P2 access URLs and P3 Bloom levels into the deliverable.

nodes.name: load_run_envelope
nodes.summary: Loads and shape-checks the run.json production envelope, raising RunEnvelopeCorruptError on malformed content.

nodes.name: run_identity_from_run
nodes.summary: Derives the run identity (course, lesson, SME, directive digest) from the run envelope for workbook cover and provenance surfaces.

nodes.name: corpus_native_further_reading
nodes.summary: Derives further-reading entries directly from corpus-native source files under the run's corpus root as a grounded complement to card citations.

nodes.name: ask_a_research_wiring.py
nodes.summary: Exactly-once Ask-A research dispatch at node 07W.2 with a crash-safe journal: builds the retrieval intent from the resolved demand, normalizes provider results into cited entries, and replays completed executions with zero provider calls.
nodes.languageNotes: Journal-based exactly-once pattern: atomic JSON writes plus an idempotency key stored in-journal so replays never re-dispatch.

nodes.name: _build_completed
nodes.summary: Constructs the completed Ask-A contribution output from normalized retrieval results, minting cited entries with credibility classification and execution receipts.

nodes.name: _replay_completed
nodes.summary: Replays a journaled completed Ask-A execution without any provider dispatch, validating journal identity against the current demand.

nodes.name: run_ask_a_research
nodes.summary: Entry point for the 07W.2 Ask-A research node: resolves demand, checks the journal for replay, dispatches retrieval when credentials permit, and persists the contribution exactly once.

nodes.name: ask_b_research_wiring.py
nodes.summary: Exactly-once Ask-B hot-topics dispatch at node 07W.4, mirroring the Ask-A wiring with its own lock, journal, idempotency key, and scope digest — fully isolated from the Ask-A journal and the 04.55 bridge.

nodes.name: _build_completed
nodes.summary: Constructs the completed Ask-B contribution from normalized hot-topics retrieval results, including cited entries, provider config fingerprint, and execution receipt.

nodes.name: _replay_completed
nodes.summary: Replays a journaled completed Ask-B execution with zero provider calls after validating journal identity against the current hot-topics demand.

nodes.name: run_ask_b_research
nodes.summary: Entry point for the 07W.4 Ask-B hot-topics node: resolves the demand, replays from journal when complete, dispatches retrieval, and persists the contribution exactly once.

nodes.name: deep_dive_enrichment_wiring.py
nodes.summary: Exactly-once journaled Deep Dive enrichment dispatch at node 07W.3: distinguishes ambiguous in-progress pauses from recorded provider failures, replays completed and empty-pool declined executions with zero provider calls, and emits stable node-scoped error tags.

nodes.name: deep_dive_enrichment_idempotency_key
nodes.summary: Derives the stable idempotency key binding an enrichment execution to its request digest and model configuration.

nodes.name: _provider_evidence
nodes.summary: Captures the raw provider payload, normalization records, and identity evidence into the journal so replays and audits can reconstruct the exact provider interaction.

nodes.name: _replay_completed
nodes.summary: Replays a journaled completed enrichment execution with zero provider calls, revalidating journal identity and reconstructing the contribution.

nodes.name: run_workbook_review
nodes.summary: Entry point for the 07W.3 workbook-review enrichment node: builds the enrichment request from the skeleton and research pool, manages the exactly-once journal lifecycle including prior-failure pauses and empty-pool honest declines, and dispatches the live enrichment writer.

nodes.name: research_citation.py
nodes.summary: Citation-facing half of the S3 research wiring: mints cited research entries with deterministic source refs and content hashes, and runs the G2 citation-fidelity gate in FAIL mode where a single unresolved citation is RED.

nodes.name: CitedResearchEntry
nodes.summary: Validated cited research entry carrying source_ref, source_hash, credibility tier from a closed set, and provenance fields.

nodes.name: mint_cited_entry
nodes.summary: Mints a CitedResearchEntry from an accepted Texas retrieval row with a deterministic source_ref and stable content-derived source_hash.

nodes.name: apply_triangulation_to_entries
nodes.summary: Marks cited entries as triangulated when multiple independent sources corroborate the same claim surface.

nodes.name: gate_citation_fidelity
nodes.summary: G2 citation-fidelity gate in FAIL mode: raises CitationFidelityError when any citation's source_ref fails to resolve to a retrieval-set row.

nodes.name: assemble_l2_citation_report
nodes.summary: Assembles the L2 citation report combining the fidelity gate result, supplement audit, duplicate detection, and citation manifest.

nodes.name: research_credibility.py
nodes.summary: Maps Texas retrieval rows onto the closed evidence-hierarchy tier set (T1 systematic through lower tiers) using heuristic metadata, per the research-evidence-hierarchy dev guide.

nodes.name: classify_evidence_hierarchy
nodes.summary: Classifies a retrieval row into an evidence-hierarchy tier from provider metadata heuristics; explicitly not a clinical grade of evidence.

nodes.name: workbook_prework_writers.py
nodes.summary: Named live structured-output LLM adapters for the 07W.1 semantic writers: scene composer, promise transformer, deep-dive writer with bold-parity degradation, and deep-dive enrichment writer, all built on a shared structured-writer base.
nodes.languageNotes: Generic _StructuredWriter[T] base binds Pydantic structured output to the cascade-configured chat model with recorded resolution metadata.

nodes.name: _StructuredWriter
nodes.summary: Generic base adapter that invokes the cascade-resolved chat model with structured output, records model-resolution metadata, and standardizes system-prompt assembly.

nodes.name: LiveSceneComposer
nodes.summary: Live LLM implementation of the SceneComposer seam producing pre-work scene candidates under faithfulness prompt constraints.

nodes.name: LivePromiseTransformer
nodes.summary: Live LLM implementation of the PromiseTransformer seam converting objectives into learner-facing promise vows.

nodes.name: LiveDeepDiveWriter
nodes.summary: Live deep-dive skeleton writer that compacts the request payload, detects bold-parity failures, and degrades to a safe deterministic construction rather than emitting invalid prose.

nodes.name: LiveDeepDiveEnrichmentWriter
nodes.summary: Live enrichment writer that weaves research-pool citations into the deep-dive skeleton via structured output, normalizing the provider payload against the enrichment contract.

nodes.name: workbook_wiring.py
nodes.summary: Deterministic orchestration seam for the four-node workbook band: resolves slide authority under a dispatch lock, composes the deep dive through an exactly-once journal, builds workbook-brief factories, and drives the band node lifecycle including artifact activation and reconciliation.
nodes.languageNotes: Largest orchestrator seam in the band (~2200 lines); combines file locking, journal replay, and write-once artifact reconciliation.

nodes.name: _resolve_slide_authority
nodes.summary: Builds the expected slide-authority map from the selected contribution under the dispatch lock, then persists it write-once or validates identity against the already-persisted map.

nodes.name: _compose_deep_dive
nodes.summary: Exactly-once journaled deep-dive composition at 07W.1: builds the skeleton request, dispatches the writer, records provider evidence and receipts, and replays completed executions without provider calls.

nodes.name: _brief_factory_locked
nodes.summary: Lock-held workbook-brief factory that composes scenes, promises, and the deep dive into the WorkbookBrief artifact with authoring receipts.

nodes.name: runtime_context_for_run
nodes.summary: Derives the WorkbookBriefRuntimeContext (run dir, course source root, coordinate identities) for a run, validating source containment.

nodes.name: _reconcile_activated_artifact
nodes.summary: Reconciles a previously activated workbook-brief artifact against the current run state, replacing the brief contribution when digests diverge.

nodes.name: run_workbook_band_node
nodes.summary: Entry point that drives the entire four-node workbook band: slide-authority resolution, brief authoring, Ask-A/review/Ask-B research nodes, artifact activation, and contribution registration into the production envelope.

nodes.name: pass1_source_section.py
nodes.summary: Defines the dual-identity authenticated source-section model for Irene Pass-1 and the canonical digest function over extracted Texas section bodies.

nodes.name: Pass1AuthenticatedSourceSection
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass binding a source section's coordinate identity to its authenticated extracted-content digest.

nodes.name: canonical_extracted_content_digest
nodes.summary: Computes the sha256 digest of a section body under the extraction contract's newline-normalized canonical form.

nodes.name: pass1_generation_lock.py
nodes.summary: Shared same-host file lock serializing Pass-1 generation publication and consumption, with msvcrt/fcntl platform branches.
nodes.languageNotes: Context-manager lock using msvcrt on Windows and fcntl elsewhere for cross-platform advisory locking.

nodes.name: pass1_generation_lock
nodes.summary: Context manager that acquires the exclusive same-host Pass-1 generation lock file, raising Pass1GenerationLockError on contention failures.

nodes.name: _act.py
nodes.summary: Irene Pass-1 lesson-plan coauthoring specialist implementation: assembles the planning prompt from the authenticated source bundle, invokes the chat model with identity binding, normalizes clusters/collateral/fidelity from the response, and persists plan artifacts with authority receipts under the generation lock.
nodes.languageNotes: Fail-loud specialist act: duplicate-JSON-key rejection, prompt-context fit assertion, mode enforcement, and completed-replay validation guard every step.

nodes.name: act
nodes.summary: Public specialist entry point that acquires the Pass-1 generation lock and delegates to the locked act implementation.

nodes.name: _act_locked
nodes.summary: Lock-held core of the Pass-1 act: enforces mode, reads the extracted source bundle, assembles and dispatches the planning prompt, parses and normalizes the response, and writes plan artifacts with receipts.

nodes.name: assemble_pass1_prompt
nodes.summary: Assembles the full Pass-1 planning prompt from sanctum references, planning provenance, source sections, and emission instructions for clusters, fidelity, and collateral.

nodes.name: normalize_clusters
nodes.summary: Normalizes raw cluster emissions from the model into validated plan clusters, enforcing floors, unique ids, and source-span bindings.

nodes.name: normalize_collateral
nodes.summary: Normalizes the model's collateral emissions against the CollateralSpec contract, rejecting malformed or out-of-scope collateral entries.

nodes.name: normalize_fidelity
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes and validates the fidelity emission rows of the Pass-1 response into their stable normalized form.

nodes.name: _decode_pass1_response_v2
nodes.summary: Decodes the v2 Pass-1 response frame from provider text, rejecting duplicate JSON keys and invalid constants before structural validation.

nodes.name: write_lesson_plan
nodes.summary: Persists the authored lesson plan into the run artifacts with canonical serialization and digest recording.

nodes.name: write_plan_authority_receipt
nodes.summary: Writes the plan-authority receipt binding the persisted plan to its digest so downstream consumers can assert plan identity.

nodes.name: validate_pass1_plan_authority
nodes.summary: Validates a plan payload against the Pass-1 plan-authority contract, raising Pass1AuthorityError on digest or shape mismatch.

nodes.name: confirm_plan_units
nodes.summary: Applies operator per-unit ratification verdicts to plan units, rejecting bulk ratification attempts.

nodes.name: _validate_completed_replay
nodes.summary: Validates that a completed prior Pass-1 execution can be honestly replayed by checking artifact digests and identity against the current payload.

nodes.name: payload_contract.py
nodes.summary: Documentation-only Ratchet-D consumed-payload contract for the Irene Pass-1 act, enumerating the exact payload keys the act reads (mode, run_id, upstream_output, bundle_reference, min_cluster_floor).

nodes.name: source_bundle.py
nodes.summary: Shared fail-loud reader that resolves the Texas bundle reference to the extracted corpus text for planner prompts, with a markdown line scanner and primary-section parser; absence of source content raises SourceBundleError and error-pauses the trial.
nodes.languageNotes: Born from the Trial-3 confabulation root cause: planners must never receive bundle metadata in place of extracted source text.

nodes.name: ExtractedPrimarySection
nodes.summary: Value object for one parsed primary section of the extracted corpus with title, body, and section digest.

nodes.name: MarkdownLine
nodes.summary: Classified markdown line record (kind, indent, content) produced by the line scanner for structural parsing.

nodes.name: scan_markdown_lines
nodes.summary: Full markdown line scanner classifying headings, list items, code fences, and continuation lines with tab-expansion-aware column tracking.

nodes.name: parse_extracted_primary_sections
nodes.summary: Parses the extracted corpus markdown into primary sections with digests, failing loud on structural violations of the extraction contract.

nodes.name: _published_bundle_snapshots
nodes.summary: Enumerates and validates published bundle snapshot directories referenced by the Texas bundle metadata, enforcing containment.

nodes.name: read_extracted_source_with_sections
nodes.summary: Reads the extracted source text plus its authenticated parsed sections from the payload's bundle reference in one call.

nodes.name: _act.py
nodes.summary: Deterministic in-graph workbook_producer specialist act (07W terminal sidecar): self-resolves inputs from the run directory, consumes Irene's authored collateral as the workbook blueprint, builds producer inputs from transcripts, enrichment, and research packets, and renders the companion workbook.

nodes.name: act
nodes.summary: Public specialist entry point for the 07W workbook producer: decodes the envelope payload, resolves the run directory, and produces the workbook deliverable with sidecar refs.

nodes.name: WorkbookInputs
nodes.summary: Aggregate container for every input the workbook composer needs: transcript segments, collateral, enrichment projection, research entries, figures, and identity fields.

nodes.name: _apply_exercise_composition_cap
nodes.summary: Caps and balances composed exercises per section by origin group, recording a composition receipt for what was kept and dropped.

nodes.name: _unit_to_enrichment_lo_map
nodes.summary: Maps plan units to enrichment learning-objective bindings so exercises and objectives inherit the correct Bloom levels per unit.

nodes.name: build_workbook_inputs
nodes.summary: Assembles the complete WorkbookInputs from the run directory: transcript segments, source text for the G1 numeric gate, collateral blueprint, enrichment projection, research and glossary inputs, deep-dive contribution, and figure supplements.

nodes.name: produce_workbook
nodes.summary: Runs the WorkbookProducer over the assembled inputs, rendering the markdown and DOCX deliverables and returning produced-asset records.

nodes.name: _reconcile_workbook_brief_authority
nodes.summary: Reconciles the workbook brief artifact's authority digests against the persisted production envelope before composition, failing loud on divergence.

nodes.name: payload_contract.py
nodes.summary: Documentation-only Ratchet-D consumed-payload contract for the workbook_producer act, recording that the terminal sidecar self-resolves inputs from the run directory and only needs the upstream_output trigger edge.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: App-namespace Marcus compatibility barrel that re-exports get_facade from the facade module as the package's single public entry point.

nodes.name: sme_registry.py
nodes.summary: Loads state/config/sme-registry.yaml and resolves per-SME voice, styleguide, attribution, and approval-route profiles; unknown SME keys hard-fail, and unbound SMEs return explicitly marked gaps rather than silently falling back to the Tejal styleguide.

nodes.name: SmeProfile
nodes.summary: Pydantic model of a resolved SME profile (key, display name, styleguide id, attribution, approval route, voice profile ref) with non-empty field validation and unbound/fallback markers.

nodes.name: SmeRegistry
nodes.summary: Pydantic model of the loaded registry file: schema version, name aliases, and the per-SME entry map.

nodes.name: load_sme_registry
nodes.summary: Cached loader that parses and validates the SME registry YAML into an SmeRegistry model, raising SmeRegistryError on malformed input.

nodes.name: resolve_sme_key
nodes.summary: Resolves a display name or alias to a canonical SME key using case-insensitive alias matching; hard-fails with the known-key list on unknown names.

nodes.name: resolve_sme_profile
nodes.summary: Builds a full SmeProfile for a name or key, marking unbound SMEs (e.g. HAI/PHS) as explicit gaps with reasons instead of silently reusing another SME's styleguide.

nodes.name: profiles_diverge
nodes.summary: Compares two SmeProfile instances field-by-field and reports whether they diverge on identity-bearing attributes.

nodes.name: facade.py
nodes.summary: Single Maya-facing Marcus facade hiding the intake/orchestrator duality behind one surface: run_step delegates to the Supervisor, ask performs a cost-tracked ad-hoc LLM call via make_chat_model, and run_4a drives the FourALoop lesson-plan cycle with plan-lock fanout; get_facade constructs it with a sanctum-digest fingerprint.
nodes.languageNotes: Enforces a one-voice contract: internal routing identifiers never appear in Maya-visible strings, pinned by a dedicated duality-leak test.

nodes.name: Facade
nodes.summary: The one Marcus surface Maya interacts with: exposes marcus_identity, run_step (Supervisor delegation), ask (ad-hoc LLM invocation with token/cost accounting into RunState), and run_4a (lesson-plan loop plus plan-lock fanout).

nodes.name: get_facade
nodes.summary: Module-level factory returning the singleton Facade, wiring manifest load, fresh RunState, and a SanctumFingerprint computed from the Marcus sanctum digest.

nodes.name: _adhoc_trace_env
nodes.summary: Context manager that toggles LangSmith tracing environment variables around an ad-hoc ask call and restores prior values afterwards.

nodes.name: _read_marcus_sanctum_digest
nodes.summary: Computes a sha256 digest over the Marcus sanctum activation-allowlist files (name + content), failing loudly if the sanctum root or any listed file is missing.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Marcus-Intake package surface (steps 01-04 plus 4A pre-packet construction) from the Story 30-1 duality split: defines the PrePacketSnapshot shape and extract_pre_packet, which emits a pre_packet_snapshot event through the orchestrator write API.

nodes.name: PrePacketSnapshot
nodes.summary: Pydantic snapshot of the intake pre-packet: run_id, source_ref, operator prompt, and normalized payload.

nodes.name: extract_pre_packet
nodes.summary: Extracts a PrePacketSnapshot from run state and appends the snapshot event to the lesson-plan log via the orchestrator write API.

nodes.name: pre_packet.py
nodes.summary: Builds the irene-packet.md handoff artifact at the step-04/05 boundary: prepare_irene_packet (byte-locked at Story 30-2a) aggregates bundle files, and prepare_and_emit_irene_packet wraps it with sha256-hashed SME source refs and dispatches a pre_packet_snapshot EventEnvelope through a caller-injected orchestrator callable.

nodes.name: prepare_irene_packet
nodes.summary: Pure file-I/O builder that reads extracted.md, metadata.json, operator-directives.md, and optional ingestion receipt from a bundle directory and writes the composed irene-packet.md; locked byte-identical per Story 30-2a.

nodes.name: prepare_and_emit_irene_packet
nodes.summary: Wraps prepare_irene_packet and on success builds a PrePacketSnapshotPayload with content hashes and SME refs, wraps it in an EventEnvelope, and hands it to the injected dispatch callable.

nodes.name: _build_sme_refs
nodes.summary: Derives SourceRef entries for SME source materials from bundle metadata, hashing extracted.md content for integrity binding.

nodes.name: _to_repo_relative_posix
nodes.summary: Normalizes an absolute path to a repo-relative POSIX string, rejecting paths outside the repository root.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel for the Lesson Plan package (Stories 31-1/31-2/31-3): re-exports the schema models (LessonPlan, PlanUnit, Dials, ScopeDecision, FitReport), log write-path, digest helpers, event primitives, modality and component-type registries, the ModalityProducer ABC, and Quinn-R gate surfaces.

nodes.name: blueprint_coauthor.py
nodes.summary: Irene blueprint co-author protocol (Story 29-3): validates a 31-4 blueprint draft asset against its plan unit, writes a deterministic sign-off sidecar markdown recording Irene and writer commentary, and returns an updated PlanUnit carrying a typed blueprint_signoff pointer.

nodes.name: coauthor_blueprint
nodes.summary: Top-level co-author entry point: validates commentary and plan unit, loads the blueprint markdown, writes the sign-off sidecar, and returns a PlanUnit copy with a BlueprintSignoff attached.

nodes.name: compose_blueprint_signoff_markdown
nodes.summary: Renders the deterministic sign-off markdown document combining plan unit identity, blueprint asset reference, both commentaries, and the signing timestamp.

nodes.name: _load_blueprint_markdown
nodes.summary: Safely resolves and reads the blueprint draft markdown from the asset's repo-relative path, rejecting absolute paths, out-of-repo escapes, and missing files with typed errors.

nodes.name: _validate_blueprint_plan_unit
nodes.summary: Checks the plan unit is eligible for blueprint co-authoring, raising BlueprintCoauthorError on scope mismatches.

nodes.name: blueprint_producer.py
nodes.summary: First concrete ModalityProducer (Story 31-4): deterministically renders an offline markdown blueprint draft with stable human-review checkpoint markers and returns a valid ProducedAsset for plan units marked for blueprint handling.

nodes.name: BlueprintProducer
nodes.summary: Concrete ModalityProducer subclass whose produce() validates the blueprint target, renders and composes the markdown draft (with injectable body renderer), writes it under the output root, and returns a ProducedAsset.

nodes.name: render_blueprint_body
nodes.summary: Default deterministic body renderer producing the blueprint draft's markdown sections from the plan unit and production context.

nodes.name: compose_blueprint_markdown
nodes.summary: Assembles the full blueprint document by wrapping the rendered body with header metadata and the stable review-checkpoint markers required downstream.

nodes.name: _validate_blueprint_target
nodes.summary: Rejects plan units not marked for blueprint handling with typed BlueprintScopeError diagnostics before any artifact is produced.

nodes.name: component_type_registry.py
nodes.summary: Closed-set registry (Story 31-3) of the two MVP component types — narrated-deck and motion-enabled-narrated-lesson — composed from atomic modalities, with both a model validator and an import-time assertion enforcing that every modality_ref is registered in MODALITY_REGISTRY.

nodes.name: ComponentTypeEntry
nodes.summary: Pydantic entry describing a composite component type (ref, composed modality_refs, description, prompt-pack version) with an after-validator requiring every modality_ref be registered.

nodes.name: get_component_type_entry
nodes.summary: Positive lookup of a ComponentTypeEntry by ref from the immutable registry, returning None for unknown refs.

nodes.name: coverage_manifest.py
nodes.summary: Story 32-2 coverage-manifest verifier auditing that every lesson-plan envelope surface in the 05-to-13 path preserves lesson_plan_revision and lesson_plan_digest and, where required, gates its live consumer entry path with assert_plan_fresh — verified via AST inspection of consumer modules — then emits the canonical coverage-manifest JSON artifact consumed by 32-3.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Python ast parsing to statically verify that consumer entrypoints actually invoke the canonical assert_plan_fresh gate rather than trusting declared coverage.

nodes.name: CoverageSurface
nodes.summary: Pydantic record of one audited envelope surface: step id, owner story, module path, plan-ref mode, field presence flags, freshness-gate verification result, and status.

nodes.name: CoverageSummary
nodes.summary: Aggregate rollup over surfaces: implemented/pending/deferred counts, plan-ref field coverage, freshness-gate gaps, and the trial_ready verdict.

nodes.name: CoverageManifest
nodes.summary: Top-level manifest artifact model with schema version, timezone-aware generated_at validator, source story key, surfaces, and summary.

nodes.name: CoverageInventoryEntry
nodes.summary: Story-owned inventory row declaring a surface to audit: module path, plan-ref mode, freshness requirement, consumer entrypoint, sample factory, and deferred flag.

nodes.name: verify_plan_ref_fields
nodes.summary: Checks a sample surface instance for the presence of lesson_plan_revision and lesson_plan_digest fields according to its declared plan-ref mode.

nodes.name: verify_assert_plan_fresh_usage
nodes.summary: AST-parses a consumer module to confirm its entrypoint (or a live canonical wrapper) actually calls assert_plan_fresh with the expected argument before other work.

nodes.name: summarize_surfaces
nodes.summary: Folds the audited surfaces into a CoverageSummary including the trial_ready verdict.

nodes.name: build_coverage_manifest
nodes.summary: Drives the full audit: resolves each inventory entry's status, runs field and freshness-gate verification per surface, and assembles the CoverageManifest.

nodes.name: emit_coverage_manifest
nodes.summary: Builds and writes the canonical coverage-manifest JSON artifact to _bmad-output/maps for downstream Story 32-3 consumption.

nodes.name: digest.py
nodes.summary: Canonical-JSON sha256 digest helpers for LessonPlan (Story 31-1): compute_digest hashes the canonical serialization with the digest field cleared, and assert_digest_matches verifies integrity; nested list order is semantic and None-stripping is normalized before hashing.
nodes.languageNotes: Canonical form pins json.dumps with sort_keys, ensure_ascii, and compact separators, plus an ISO-8601 UTC datetime default, so digests are byte-stable across processes.

nodes.name: compute_digest
nodes.summary: Computes the sha256 hex digest of a LessonPlan's canonical-JSON serialization with its digest field cleared before hashing.

nodes.name: assert_digest_matches
nodes.summary: Recomputes a plan's digest and raises if it does not match the stored value, guarding against tampered or stale plans.

nodes.name: _strip_none
nodes.summary: Recursively removes None-valued keys from nested payloads so optional-absent and explicit-None serialize identically before hashing.

nodes.name: event_type_registry.py
nodes.summary: Registry of the nine Gagne-9 plan-unit event labels plus the reserved log event types pre-registered for Story 31-2 emission; validate_event_type warns (not rejects) on unknown-but-well-formed strings so extensions surface in observability, while invalid-regex strings are rejected.

nodes.name: validate_event_type
nodes.summary: Open-string validator: rejects malformed event_type strings, warns once per unknown well-formed type outside the Gagne-9 and reserved-log sets, and passes registered types silently.

nodes.name: events.py
nodes.summary: Event primitives for the Lesson Plan log (Story 31-1): the generic EventEnvelope every log event conforms to, and the ScopeDecisionTransition payload exposing a public two-level actor surface (system/operator) that hides the five-valued internal Marcus-duality actor from Maya-facing payloads.

nodes.name: EventEnvelope
nodes.summary: Generic envelope shape for all lesson-plan log events, validating UUID4 event ids and timezone-aware timestamps.

nodes.name: ScopeDecisionTransition
nodes.summary: First concrete event payload: records a scope-decision state transition with a public actor field for Maya-facing serialization and an audit dump that includes the private internal actor.

nodes.name: to_internal_actor
nodes.summary: Maps a public actor value to the five-valued internal Marcus-duality actor vocabulary used in audit records.

nodes.name: fit_report.py
nodes.summary: Fit-report-v1 validator, canonical-JSON serializer/deserializer, and emit_fit_report log-emission wiring (Story 29-1); Marcus-Orchestrator is the canonical caller of emit_fit_report — Irene produces FitReport instances but must never emit them directly, a contract backed by the 31-2 single-writer enforcement.

nodes.name: validate_fit_report
nodes.summary: Validates a FitReport against the referenced lesson plan: plan-ref freshness (raising StaleFitReportError), known unit ids (UnknownUnitIdError), and per-diagnosis consistency.

nodes.name: serialize_fit_report
nodes.summary: Produces the canonical-JSON serialization of a FitReport with sorted keys, ASCII escapes, and compact separators matching the digest module's conventions.

nodes.name: deserialize_fit_report
nodes.summary: Parses canonical-JSON back into a validated FitReport model instance.

nodes.name: emit_fit_report
nodes.summary: Marcus-Orchestrator-only emission seam: validates the fit report, wraps it in an event envelope, and appends it to the lesson-plan log under the single-writer matrix.

nodes.name: gagne_diagnostician.py
nodes.summary: Deterministic Irene-authored Gagne-9 diagnostician (Story 29-2) that constructs and validates FitReport instances per plan unit — deriving fitness, recommended scope decisions, weather bands, and commentary — while delegating modality validity to the 31-3 registry and never writing to the log itself.

nodes.name: diagnose_plan_unit
nodes.summary: Produces a FitDiagnosis for one plan unit: enforces supported Gagne event types, derives fitness and recommendations, and formats commentary including prior declined rationales.

nodes.name: diagnose_lesson_plan
nodes.summary: Diagnoses every unit of a lesson plan into a complete FitReport, rejecting duplicate diagnosis targets at construction time and binding the report to the plan's revision and digest.

nodes.name: _derive_fitness
nodes.summary: Deterministic rule set mapping a plan unit's event type, modality, and gaps to a fitness verdict.

nodes.name: _format_commentary
nodes.summary: Composes the human-readable diagnosis commentary, weaving fitness rationale, scope recommendation, and normalized prior-declined rationales.

nodes.name: PriorDeclinedRationale
nodes.summary: Small typed shape carrying a previously declined recommendation's rationale so re-diagnosis can surface it in commentary.

nodes.name: log.py
nodes.summary: Append-only JSONL Lesson Plan Log (Story 31-2) with single-writer enforcement via WRITER_EVENT_MATRIX (Marcus-Orchestrator sole writer for five of six mandatory event types, Intake permitted only pre_packet_snapshot), payload shapes (SourceRef, PrePacketSnapshotPayload, PlanLockedPayload), the LessonPlanLog read/write API, and the assert_plan_fresh staleness gate.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-writer rule is triple-surfaced: Literal writer identity in the schema, a runtime matrix check in append_event, and a 12-case parametrized test suite.

nodes.name: LessonPlanLog
nodes.summary: Append-only JSONL log with append_event (writer-matrix enforced), read_events, and latest_plan_revision/latest_plan_digest queries; corrupt lines raise LogCorruptError rather than being skipped.

nodes.name: assert_plan_fresh
nodes.summary: Module-level staleness detector comparing an envelope's lesson_plan_revision/digest against the log's latest values, raising StalePlanRefError on mismatch; the canonical freshness gate audited by the 32-2 coverage manifest.

nodes.name: SourceRef
nodes.summary: Pydantic shape referencing a source artifact by repo-relative path with content hash, validated to stay inside the repository.

nodes.name: PrePacketSnapshotPayload
nodes.summary: Payload shape for the single Intake-permitted log event: irene-packet artifact path (repo-relative validated), content hashes, and SME source refs.

nodes.name: PlanLockedPayload
nodes.summary: Payload shape for the plan.locked mandatory event carrying the locked plan's revision and digest.

nodes.name: modality_producer.py
nodes.summary: ModalityProducer abstract base class (Story 31-3) that every concrete modality producer subclasses; an __init_subclass__ hook rejects subclasses with missing or malformed modality_ref/status class attributes at class-creation time, and produce() must return a ProducedAsset without mutating its inputs.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses __init_subclass__ to enforce ClassVar contracts at class-definition time, since CPython never validates ClassVar type hints at runtime.

nodes.name: ModalityProducer
nodes.summary: Abstract base contract for producers: pinned modality_ref and status ClassVars enforced by __init_subclass__, and an abstract produce(plan_unit, context) returning a ProducedAsset with a valid fulfills pointer.

nodes.name: modality_registry.py
nodes.summary: Immutable closed-set registry (Story 31-3) of the five MVP atomic modalities — slides and blueprint (ready), leader-guide, handout, and classroom-exercise (pending) — exposed through a MappingProxyType with get_modality_entry and ready/pending list queries; widening requires a schema-version bump and changelog entry.

nodes.name: ModalityEntry
nodes.summary: Pydantic entry for one atomic modality with ready/pending status and a validator requiring pending entries to have a null producer_class_path.

nodes.name: get_modality_entry
nodes.summary: Positive lookup of a ModalityEntry by ref, returning None for unknown refs without warning or raising.

nodes.name: produced_asset.py
nodes.summary: Twin input/output contract for ModalityProducer (Story 31-3): ProductionContext as the minimal subclassable produce() input preserving plan revision and digest, and ProducedAsset as the output carrying the fulfills '{plan_unit_id}@{plan_revision}' pointer with a regex pin and a cross-field validator rejecting counterfeit unit-id mismatches.

nodes.name: ProductionContext
nodes.summary: Minimal producer input shape carrying lesson_plan_revision and lesson_plan_digest; designed as an extensibility seam that producers may subclass without a schema bump.

nodes.name: ProducedAsset
nodes.summary: Producer output shape with timezone-aware timestamps, the pinned fulfills regex, and a cross-field validator ensuring source_plan_unit_id matches the unit id parsed from fulfills.

nodes.name: prose_uplift.py
nodes.summary: SME-aware learner-ready workbook prose uplift (Mine 6): removes REVOICE markers, rewrites deixis, and stamps attribution resolved from the Mine 3 SME registry; idempotent on a second pass and measurable via the ProseUpliftDelta metrics shape.

nodes.name: ProseUpliftDelta
nodes.summary: Metrics dataclass comparing before/after prose: revoice-marker and deixis counts with deixis_reduced/markers_cleared verdicts and a dict dump.

nodes.name: uplift_narration_text
nodes.summary: Core uplift transform: strips scaffold and REVOICE markers, rewrites deictic phrases, and applies the base revoicer to produce learner-ready prose.

nodes.name: measure_prose_delta
nodes.summary: Computes a ProseUpliftDelta by counting revoice markers and deixis hits before and after uplift.

nodes.name: sme_aware_prose_revoicer
nodes.summary: Builds a revoicer that stamps attribution from a resolved SmeProfile onto uplifted prose.

nodes.name: prose_revoicer_for_sme
nodes.summary: Convenience factory resolving an SME name or key through the registry and returning the matching SME-aware revoicer callable.

nodes.name: quinn_r_gate.py
nodes.summary: Deterministic Quinn-R step-13 two-branch quality gate for lesson-plan units: non-blueprint units pass via produced-asset quality, blueprint units via Irene+writer sign-off, and out-of-scope units remain as audited Declined nodes with verbatim rationale.

nodes.name: evaluate_quinn_r_two_branch_gate
nodes.summary: Evaluates every plan unit against the two-branch gate (asset-quality branch vs blueprint sign-off branch), collecting per-unit verdicts and prior-declined rationales into a QuinnRTwoBranchResult without mutating the plan.

nodes.name: emit_quinn_r_gate_artifact
nodes.summary: Writes the gate result as a machine-readable JSON artifact under a validated output root for step-13 auditing.

nodes.name: QuinnRUnitVerdict
nodes.summary: Pydantic model capturing a single plan unit's gate verdict: branch taken, pass/fail, reason, and the asset reference that satisfied it.

nodes.name: QuinnRTwoBranchResult
nodes.summary: Aggregate gate result model carrying the plan ref, timezone-aware evaluation timestamp, overall pass flag, unit verdicts, and preserved prior-declined rationales.

nodes.name: quiz_enrichment.py
nodes.summary: Projects a frozen G0 enrichment card into QuizSpec inputs, preferring quiz-typed components and falling back to assessment-like types while staying import-linter Contract M3 safe (no orchestrator imports).

nodes.name: project_enrichment_to_quiz_inputs
nodes.summary: Transforms an enrichment card payload into a validated QuizSpec, sanitizing IDs, mapping learning objectives, selecting quiz-preferred components, and accumulating warnings for empty or degraded sources.

nodes.name: QuizEnrichmentProjection
nodes.summary: Dataclass return envelope pairing the projected QuizSpec with projection warnings and an empty-source flag.

nodes.name: quiz_producer.py
nodes.summary: Renders a QuizSpec into Markdown and writes the quiz collateral artifact to disk, raising QuizProducerError when an honest render is impossible.

nodes.name: render_quiz_markdown
nodes.summary: Composes the quiz Markdown body from a QuizSpec, laying out items, choices, expected answer focus, and source references.

nodes.name: write_quiz_artifact
nodes.summary: Writes the rendered quiz Markdown to the given output path, creating parent directories and verifying the write landed non-empty.

nodes.name: quiz_spec.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic content model for the quiz collateral family: LO-linked short quiz items carrying prompt, choices, expected answer focus, and source references, kept distinct from the workbook and drill families.

nodes.name: QuizSourceRef
nodes.summary: Model binding a quiz item to its source evidence via ref_id, locator, and excerpt.

nodes.name: QuizItem
nodes.summary: Model for a single LO-linked quiz question with prompt, choices, expected answer focus, and source refs; validates non-whitespace text fields.

nodes.name: QuizSpec
nodes.summary: Top-level quiz collateral spec carrying schema version, kind discriminator, validated title, and the item list.

nodes.name: retrieval_narration_grammar.py
nodes.summary: Pure wording seam that turns a Tracy-style retrieval posture result into a single Marcus-voiced narration sentence for research turns; no dispatch, state mutation, or log writes (Story 30-5).

nodes.name: render_retrieval_narration
nodes.summary: Validates a retrieval result mapping, normalizes its posture, and renders the matching Marcus narration sentence (including corroboration-classification phrasing).

nodes.name: schema.py
nodes.summary: Core Lesson Plan schema contract (Story 31-1): Pydantic v2 primitives for LessonPlan, PlanUnit, ScopeDecision state machine, BlueprintSignoff, gap/dial models, and the FitReport diagnosis family that all downstream stories read and write against.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic v2 with strict validation idioms: timezone-aware datetime validators, red-value triple rejection on closed enums, and a private internal actor field alongside a public two-valued actor surface.

nodes.name: LearningModel
nodes.summary: Identifies the pedagogical learning model (id + version) a lesson plan is built against.

nodes.name: PlanRef
nodes.summary: Lightweight reference pinning a lesson plan revision and digest, used across envelopes and gate artifacts to detect staleness.

nodes.name: Dials
nodes.summary: Per-unit tunable dials (enrichment, corroboration) controlling production posture for a plan unit.

nodes.name: IdentifiedGap
nodes.summary: Model for a source-coverage gap identified on a plan unit, with gap id, description, and suggested retrieval posture.

nodes.name: ScopeDecision
nodes.summary: State machine for a unit's scope decision with a two-level actor surface (public system/operator vs private five-valued internal taxonomy), guarded lock transitions requiring Maya ratification, audit dumping, and a validated transition_to method.

nodes.name: BlueprintSignoff
nodes.summary: Records the dual Irene-review + writer sign-off on a blueprint asset, validating repo-relative artifact paths and a timezone-aware signed_at timestamp.

nodes.name: PlanUnit
nodes.summary: The per-unit planning record: event type, source-fitness diagnosis, scope decision, weather band, modality ref, verbatim rationale, blueprint signoff, gaps, and dials — with red-fitness rejection and scope/state consistency validators.

nodes.name: LessonPlan
nodes.summary: Top-level lesson plan aggregate with schema version, learning model, plan units, and optimistic-concurrency revision/digest management via bump_revision and apply_revision (raising StaleRevisionError on conflicts).

nodes.name: FitDiagnosis
nodes.summary: Per-unit source-fitness diagnosis from Irene with fitness rating, commentary, and recommended scope decision / weather band; rejects red recommendations.

nodes.name: FitReport
nodes.summary: Aggregate fit report binding diagnoses to a source ref and plan ref with a timezone-aware generation timestamp and Irene budget accounting.

nodes.name: step_05_pre_packet_handoff.py
nodes.summary: Step 05 pre-packet handoff consumer boundary (Story 30-4): validates the incoming plan-ref envelope and asserts the referenced lesson plan is still fresh before consumption.

nodes.name: Step05PrePacketEnvelope
nodes.summary: Plan-ref envelope model (revision, digest, step id) consumed at the step 05 boundary.

nodes.name: consume
nodes.summary: Validates the raw envelope into Step05PrePacketEnvelope and calls assert_plan_fresh against the lesson plan log before accepting the handoff.

nodes.name: step_06_plan_lock_fanout.py
nodes.summary: Step 06 plan-lock fanout consumer boundary (Story 30-4): validates the incoming plan-ref envelope and asserts plan freshness before consumption.

nodes.name: Step06PlanLockFanoutEnvelope
nodes.summary: Plan-ref envelope model (revision, digest, step id) consumed at the step 06 boundary.

nodes.name: consume
nodes.summary: Validates the raw envelope into Step06PlanLockFanoutEnvelope and calls assert_plan_fresh against the lesson plan log before accepting the fanout.

nodes.name: step_07_gap_dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Step 07 gap-dispatch consumer boundary (Story 30-4): validates the gap-dispatch envelope (unit id, gap type, bridge status) and asserts plan freshness before consumption.

nodes.name: Step07GapDispatchEnvelope
nodes.summary: Envelope model consumed at step 07 carrying plan ref plus per-gap fields: unit_id, gap_type, and bridge_status.

nodes.name: consume
nodes.summary: Validates the raw envelope into Step07GapDispatchEnvelope and calls assert_plan_fresh against the lesson plan log before accepting the gap dispatch.

nodes.name: workbook_producer.py
nodes.summary: Workbook producer for the braid client deliverable: composes the workbook companion to the narrated deck (transcript, fuller narrative, exercises, citations, cover art brief) with Markdown as canonical substrate and DOCX rendered from the same model, plus fidelity audits and a sidecar receipt.
nodes.languageNotes: Single-source-of-truth render path: a composed document model renders to both canonical Markdown and the DOCX deliverable, with deterministic zip pinning so DOCX output is byte-stable.

nodes.name: load_transcript_segments
nodes.summary: Loads and validates transcript segments (narration text, visual refs, source refs) from the segment manifest that feeds workbook composition.

nodes.name: audit_workbook_numeric_fidelity
nodes.summary: Calls the frozen-neck audit_numeric_provenance READ-ONLY on the workbook body against source text and research supplements, converting the warn-only report into the workbook sidecar shape.

nodes.name: audit_citation_fidelity
nodes.summary: Audits workbook citations against the source-ref manifest, reporting resolved vs unsourced citations for the sidecar receipt.

nodes.name: assert_learning_objective_bindings
nodes.summary: Asserts every workbook section binds to a known learning objective and every objective is covered, raising WorkbookFidelityError on unbound or orphaned objectives.

nodes.name: build_cover_art_brief
nodes.summary: Builds the deterministic cover-art brief (themes, palette hints, alt text) keyed to the workbook stem, unit, and event type.

nodes.name: compose_cover_sections
nodes.summary: Composes the workbook cover pages: title/identity lines, run identity, bound objectives, encounter mode, table of contents from body headings, and the cover receipt used for audit.

nodes.name: compose_workbook
nodes.summary: Central 440-line composition function assembling the full workbook document model from plan unit, spec, transcript segments, prose revoicer, learning objectives, answer keys, research/glossary enrichment, pre-work, deep-dive review, and cover inputs.

nodes.name: render_markdown
nodes.summary: Renders the composed workbook document model to canonical Markdown, the substrate downstream citation injection fills.

nodes.name: render_docx_from_model
nodes.summary: Renders the client DOCX deliverable from the same composed model via python-docx, then pins zip determinism so output bytes are stable.

nodes.name: TranscriptSegment
nodes.summary: Model for one narrated deck segment: id, narration text, visual file references, and source ref feeding workbook composition.

nodes.name: ResearchEntry
nodes.summary: Cited research supplement model with full provenance: provider, source hash, evidence-hierarchy tier, peer-review status, triangulation status, and reliability score.

nodes.name: WorkbookSidecar
nodes.summary: Audit sidecar model paired with the produced workbook asset: paths, numeric and citation audits, segment coverage, brief/depth receipts, overlay-loss notes, exercise composition, and cover receipt.

nodes.name: WorkbookProducer
nodes.summary: Concrete ModalityProducer that produces the workbook deliverable end-to-end: composes the document, renders Markdown + DOCX, runs fidelity audits, verifies workbook-superset-of-VO coverage, and emits the ProducedAsset with its sidecar.

nodes.name: dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrator-side dispatch seam routing Intake-originated pre-packet envelopes into the Lesson Plan log; the sole caller of write_api.emit_pre_packet_snapshot for Intake flows (Story 30-2b).

nodes.name: dispatch_intake_pre_packet
nodes.summary: Routes an Intake-built pre_packet_snapshot envelope into the Lesson Plan log by calling emit_pre_packet_snapshot with the orchestrator writer identity — the only sanctioned path for Intake-originated emissions.

nodes.name: dispatch_orchestrator_event
nodes.summary: Appends orchestrator-originated event envelopes to the Lesson Plan log as the sole non-write_api caller of LessonPlanLog.append_event, enforcing the writer/event matrix.

nodes.name: fanout.py
nodes.summary: Plan-lock fanout emitter seam (Story 30-4): builds step 05+ envelopes from a locked lesson plan and emits fanout.envelope.emitted log events through a caller-injected dispatch callable, preserving single-writer discipline.

nodes.name: emit_plan_lock_fanout
nodes.summary: Builds the full set of downstream step envelopes (including per-unit gap envelopes) from a locked plan and emits each through the injected dispatch callable, returning a PlanLockFanoutResult.

nodes.name: StepFanoutEnvelope
nodes.summary: Model for one step-targeted fanout envelope built from the locked plan's ref and step payload.

nodes.name: PlanLockFanoutResult
nodes.summary: Result model summarizing the envelopes emitted during a plan-lock fanout pass.

nodes.name: hil_intake.py
nodes.summary: Marcus HIL intake production bridge for Step 04A (Story 32-5): serves the operator's pre-collected conversational scope-ratification decisions to FourALoop.run_4a(), plus a confirmed-only lesson-plan log reset helper.

nodes.name: build_hil_intake_callable
nodes.summary: Constructs the MarcusPreCollectedIntakeCallable from operator-ratified decisions so the 4A loop can consume them programmatically via Facade.run_4a().

nodes.name: reset_lesson_plan_log
nodes.summary: Resets the lesson plan log only when explicitly confirmed, raising LogResetNotConfirmedError otherwise to prevent accidental state loss.

nodes.name: MarcusPreCollectedIntakeCallable
nodes.summary: Callable serving pre-collected operator scope decisions per unit to the 4A loop, raising MissingIntakeDecisionError when a unit has no ratified decision.

nodes.name: loop.py
nodes.summary: The 4A conversation loop (Story 30-3a): iterates plan units collecting Maya's scope decisions, tunes per-unit dials, synchronously reassesses with Irene's diagnostician, and triggers plan lock when every unit is ratified — all behind the single Marcus facade.

nodes.name: intake_scope_decision
nodes.summary: Records an operator scope decision for one plan unit, validating the transition and emitting the scope_decision.set event to the log.

nodes.name: tune_unit_dials
nodes.summary: Applies operator dial adjustments (enrichment, corroboration) to a plan unit within the 4A conversation, revising the plan and voicing the change back to Maya.

nodes.name: sync_reassess_with_irene
nodes.summary: Runs a synchronous Irene reassessment by calling diagnose_lesson_plan on the current plan, folding refreshed fitness diagnoses back into the units, and optionally weaving a Tracy retrieval narration into the voiced message.

nodes.name: trigger_plan_lock_if_ready
nodes.summary: Checks whether every plan unit has a ratified scope decision and, if so, locks the plan and emits the plan.locked event; raises PlanAlreadyLockedError on double lock.

nodes.name: FourAState
nodes.summary: Mutable state container for a 4A conversation pass: current plan, per-unit iteration cursor, and lock status accessors.

nodes.name: SyncReassessmentResult
nodes.summary: Result model for a synchronous Irene reassessment carrying the revised plan and the Maya-voiced message.

nodes.name: FourALoop
nodes.summary: Driver class running the full 4A pass: iterates plan units through the injected intake callable, applies decisions and reassessments, and triggers plan lock plus fanout when the plan is fully ratified.

nodes.name: maya_walkthrough.py
nodes.summary: Release-gate ceremony walkthrough (Story 32-4) driving the landed MVP chain through a canned 7-page SME fixture and recording stage-by-stage evidence for the pantomime AC: paste source, weather ribbon, gray card click, delegation proposal, operator rationale, card turns gold.

nodes.name: run_maya_walkthrough
