nodes.summary: Executes all six pantomime stages in order against the facade and canned fixture, collecting per-stage evidence models plus declined-articulation records into a MayaWalkthroughResult.

nodes.name: MayaWalkthroughResult
nodes.summary: Aggregate evidence model bundling the six stage-evidence records that prove the pantomime AC end-to-end.

nodes.name: trial_smoke_harness.py
nodes.summary: Trial-run smoke harness (Story 32-3) chaining the Lesson Planner surfaces end-to-end — coverage manifest, Irene packet emission, Step 4A workflow routing, step 05-07 envelope consumption, and the Quinn-R gate — into a pass/fail TrialSmokeReport.

nodes.name: run_trial_run_smoke_harness
nodes.summary: Runs the full smoke sequence: emits the coverage manifest, prepares and emits the Irene packet, routes step 04 gate to step 05, consumes step envelopes, evaluates the Quinn-R two-branch gate, and assembles the readiness battery into a TrialSmokeReport.

nodes.name: TrialReadinessBattery
nodes.summary: Model aggregating the individual readiness checks the smoke run evaluates before declaring trial readiness.

nodes.name: TrialSmokeReport
nodes.summary: Top-level smoke report model carrying per-step results, the readiness battery, and the overall pass/fail verdict.

nodes.name: workflow_runner.py
nodes.summary: Lesson Planner workflow runner seam (Story 32-1) that inserts the Step 4A conversation between the step-04 gate and step 05, supporting pre-collected operator decisions (32-5) so the production HIL intake callable can be wired in.

nodes.name: insert_between
nodes.summary: Generic helper inserting a step baton handoff between two adjacent workflow steps, validating adjacency before splicing.

nodes.name: route_step_04_gate_to_step_05
nodes.summary: Routes the workflow from the step-04 gate into step 05 by resolving the facade, running the 4A conversation (facade.run_4a) with optionally pre-collected decisions, and returning the Step4AWorkflowResult with the locked plan.

nodes.name: Step4AWorkflowResult
nodes.summary: Result model returned by the step-4A routing seam carrying the locked plan handoff.

nodes.name: write_api.py
nodes.summary: Single-writer entry point for Marcus-Orchestrator writes to the Lesson Plan log: the one sanctioned surface for appending pre_packet_snapshot events, enforcing the single-writer invariant with caller-scoped errors.

nodes.name: emit_pre_packet_snapshot
nodes.summary: Validates the caller identity and envelope, then appends the pre_packet_snapshot event to the Lesson Plan log under the orchestrator writer identity — the sole sanctioned append path for this event type.

nodes.name: append_event
nodes.summary: Thin authorized wrapper appending a validated event envelope to the Lesson Plan log with the orchestrator writer identity.

nodes.name: UnauthorizedFacadeCallerError
nodes.summary: Error raised when a non-sanctioned caller attempts to use the orchestrator write API, giving a caller-scoped message ahead of the log's last-mile check.

nodes.name: source_fidelity_audit.py
nodes.summary: L2 source-provenance audit (warn-only, non-gating): measures how faithfully a delivered narration's numeric assertions and semantic framing trace back to the source corpus, reporting drift without ever raising or blocking.
nodes.languageNotes: Binding warn-only contract: audit_numeric_provenance only ever returns a report dict; the sole FAIL condition is a zero-denominator guard when either side yields no numeric tokens.

nodes.name: audit_semantic_framing
nodes.summary: Audits the semantic framing leg of source fidelity, comparing narration framing against source-corpus token evidence and reporting drift warn-only.

nodes.name: audit_numeric_provenance
nodes.summary: The frozen-neck numeric leg: extracts and normalizes numeric tokens from narration, source, and research supplements, matches them with nearest-token analysis, and returns a warn-only drift report (FAIL only on zero-denominator).

nodes.name: source_fidelity_report.py
nodes.summary: CLI that runs the L2 numeric provenance audit on a trial run: loads narration from the run's segment manifest and the source corpus slides, calls audit_numeric_provenance, and writes the warn-only report into the run's exports directory.

nodes.name: load_narration
nodes.summary: Loads the delivered narration by joining segment narration_text from the trial's storyboard-B segment manifest YAML.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: parses arguments, loads narration and source corpus, runs audit_numeric_provenance, and writes the report JSON into the run's exports directory.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Section 02A LLM-driven directive composer, re-exporting the composer entry points (compose, write_directive_yaml), the ComposerCache, and the directive Pydantic models.

nodes.name: _cache.py
nodes.summary: In-memory cache for Section 02A LLM classification responses, keyed by SHA256 of the normalized prompt so fixture-replay and live runs can reuse equivalent classifications.

nodes.name: ComposerCache
nodes.summary: Pydantic model wrapping a dict of prompt-hash to LLM response text with get/set accessors, used to avoid repeated LLM classification calls for identical prompts.

nodes.name: _prompt.py
nodes.summary: Renders the Section 02A corpus-file classification prompt from a Jinja2 template and computes stable cache keys by stripping volatile identity (operator_id, timestamps, run_id) before SHA256 hashing.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Jinja2 StrictUndefined with a template loaded from docs/conversational-gates; cache-key normalization regex-strips ISO timestamps, UUIDs, and operator ids so semantically equivalent prompts hash identically.

nodes.name: render_classification_prompt
nodes.summary: Renders the classification prompt for a single corpus file, injecting its relative path, size, extension, an optional binary hex sample, and the full corpus summary into the Jinja2 template.

nodes.name: cli_adapter.py
nodes.summary: Canonical CLI-side bridge between the Section 02A composer's compose/write shape and the historical compose_legacy/materialize path-and-digest contract expected by trial callers.

nodes.name: compose_and_write
nodes.summary: Validates the corpus directory, resolves or builds the chat model, records a model-resolution trail, composes the directive, writes it as YAML, and returns the directive path plus its SHA256 digest.

nodes.name: composer.py
nodes.summary: LLM-driven Section 02A directive composition: walks the lesson corpus, applies rule-based exclusions, classifies each remaining file via an LLM prompt with response caching, and assembles a validated Directive written to YAML.
nodes.languageNotes: Guards against running on a non-leaf corpus scope (NonRunnableScopeError when course/module markers are present) before any LLM spend.

nodes.name: assert_lesson_corpus_leaf
nodes.summary: Raises NonRunnableScopeError when the corpus directory carries course- or module-level markers, ensuring composition only runs against a lesson-leaf corpus.

nodes.name: _classify_with_llm
nodes.summary: Classifies a single corpus file by rendering its prompt, consulting the ComposerCache by normalized-prompt hash, invoking the LLM only on cache miss, and converting the JSON payload into a DirectiveSource.

nodes.name: compose
nodes.summary: Main composition loop: enumerates corpus files, applies rule-based exclusions or LLM classification per file, and assembles the resulting sources into a timezone-aware, UUID-validated Directive.

nodes.name: write_directive_yaml
nodes.summary: Serializes a Directive to deterministic YAML (safe_dump) at the given path, creating parent directories as needed, and returns the written path.

nodes.name: directive_model.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic-v2 models for Section 02A directive composition: role and excluded-reason enums, the per-file DirectiveSource with role/field invariants, and the top-level Directive with UUID4 and tz-aware enforcement.
nodes.languageNotes: Follows the repo's Pydantic-v2 schema checklist: closed Literal enums with TypeAdapter red-rejection, legacy-key migration validator, and shared enforce_uuid4_version/enforce_tz_aware validators from app.models.state._base.

nodes.name: DirectiveSource
nodes.summary: Per-corpus-file directive entry (ref_id, locator, role, description, expected_min_words, excluded_reason) with validators that migrate the legacy source_id key, reject unknown roles/reasons, and enforce role-dependent field invariants.

nodes.name: Directive
nodes.summary: Top-level Section 02A directive (run_id, corpus_dir, sources, composed_at, schema_version, gamma_settings) enforcing UUID4 run ids and timezone-aware timestamps, with a model_dump override that prunes internal fields.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Section 02A G0 poll surface, re-exporting the canonical surface helpers (display, edit, verdict submission, digest, and resume) from poll_surface.

nodes.name: _transports.py
nodes.summary: In-process transport handlers (CLI, HTTP, MCP-stdio) for the Section 02A G0 poll surface; each transport submits an operator verdict through the shared poll-surface core and returns a uniform transport response.
nodes.languageNotes: All three transports funnel through the same submit_verdict/resume_from_verdict core, keeping transport parity by construction rather than by duplicated logic.

nodes.name: submit_cli_verdict
nodes.summary: CLI transport entry: submits an operator verdict (proceed/edit/reject) against a directive and returns the shared transport response including the resumed directive and verdict digest.

nodes.name: submit_http_verdict
nodes.summary: HTTP transport entry mirroring the CLI path: submits the operator verdict through the shared poll-surface core and returns the parity-shaped transport response.

nodes.name: submit_mcp_stdio_verdict
nodes.summary: MCP stdio transport entry mirroring the CLI/HTTP paths: submits the operator verdict through the shared poll-surface core and returns the parity-shaped transport response.

nodes.name: poll_surface.py
nodes.summary: Canonical Section 02A G0 poll-surface core: loads and displays directives with canonical SHA256 digests, applies operator edit payloads with strict field allow-lists, validates verdict submission, and resumes the run from a verdict with digest-match enforcement.
nodes.languageNotes: Registers a parity contract at import time and computes digests over canonical JSON (sorted keys, compact separators, ascii) so every transport sees byte-identical model bytes.

nodes.name: display_directive
nodes.summary: Coerces a directive or path into a Directive and returns the operator-facing display payload with its canonical model digest.

nodes.name: apply_directive_edit
nodes.summary: Applies an operator edit payload to directive sources by ref_id with unknown-ref and unknown-field rejection, then revalidates the edited directive through the Pydantic model, raising GateError on any violation.

nodes.name: submit_verdict
nodes.summary: Builds a validated Section02AOperatorVerdict for a proceed/edit/reject verb, applying edits first when present and stamping the resulting directive digest into the verdict.

nodes.name: resume_from_verdict
nodes.summary: Resumes the paused run from an operator verdict: re-derives the post-verdict directive, enforces that the verdict's recorded digest matches the recomputed digest, and returns the resume payload or raises GateError.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Marcus CLI bridge, re-exporting the ad-hoc ask, gate verdict/override, and trial-start entry points from adhoc_cli, gate_cli, and trial.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: python -m app.marcus.cli entry point: builds the top-level argparse grammar (trial, adhoc, gate, plan-ratify, plan-dialogue subcommands) and dispatches to the matching CLI handler.

nodes.name: build_parser
nodes.summary: Assembles the top-level ArgumentParser with subparsers for trial, adhoc, plan-ratify, and plan-dialogue, delegating each subcommand's flags to its owning module's parser builder.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI dispatcher: routes gate subcommands to gate_cli's main and other parsed subcommands (adhoc ask, plan ratify/dialogue, trial start/resume/recover/resume-batch) to their handlers.

nodes.name: adhoc_cli.py
nodes.summary: CLI bridge for one-shot Marcus ad-hoc asks: loads env, parses optional per-agent cascade model overrides, and routes the question through the Marcus facade, printing the JSON response.

nodes.name: _parse_cascade_override
nodes.summary: Parses an agent=model cascade-override string, normalizing the agent name and rejecting malformed or empty entries with ValueError.

nodes.name: run_adhoc_ask
nodes.summary: Executes a one-shot ad-hoc ask through the Marcus facade with optional cascade override and returns the model-dumped response payload.

nodes.name: adhoc_ask_cli
nodes.summary: Argparse-facing handler for the adhoc ask subcommand: enforces live-env presence, runs the ask, and prints the JSON result or a structured error to stdout/stderr with an exit code.

nodes.name: front_door.py
nodes.summary: Marcus-SPOC front door — the first conversational turn of a production run: presents the curated bundle catalog with honest readiness, treats model recommendations as advisory-only, and only an explicit confirmed operator pick of a runnable bundle yields a FrontDoorSelection.
nodes.languageNotes: Load-bearing deterministic guard: the LLM recommendation never auto-selects; unknown bundle ids raise UnknownBundleError before any selection, so a run can never start against a hallucinated bundle.

nodes.name: BundleOption
nodes.summary: Catalog row presented to the operator: bundle id, display name, description, readiness label, selectability, warning flag, and required seed inputs.

nodes.name: FrontDoorSelection
nodes.summary: Result contract of a confirmed front-door pick: the chosen bundle id, its ComponentSelection, and the operator-supplied seed inputs handed to trial start.

nodes.name: present_catalog
nodes.summary: Builds the tuple of BundleOption rows from the ratified bundle catalog, computing live readiness per bundle so unproven bundles are flagged rather than hidden.

nodes.name: render_catalog
nodes.summary: Renders the presented catalog options into the numbered operator-facing text listing with readiness and required-input annotations.

nodes.name: front_door_select
nodes.summary: Deterministic selection guard: validates the operator's explicit confirmed pick against the catalog, rejects unknown or non-runnable bundles and missing required seeds, and returns a FrontDoorSelection.

nodes.name: run_front_door
nodes.summary: Interactive front-door loop over injected IO seams: prints the catalog and advisory recommendation, prompts for a pick with bounded attempts, requires yes-confirmation, and delegates to front_door_select.

nodes.name: gate_cli.py
nodes.summary: CLI bridge for the gate verdict and override flow: validates operator verdicts into resume commands via the gates resume API, and submits/applies runtime overrides with cache-impact confirmation tokens.

nodes.name: run_gate_decide
nodes.summary: Validates an operator verdict payload, resumes the run from it through the gates resume API, and returns the CLI-transport response.

nodes.name: run_override_submit
nodes.summary: Submits a runtime override request through the override API, returning the cache-impact preview and confirmation token payload.

nodes.name: run_override_apply
nodes.summary: Applies a previously submitted override using its confirmation token through the override API, surfacing stale-token failures as structured errors.

nodes.name: gate_decide_cli
nodes.summary: Argparse-facing handler for gate decide: loads the verdict payload from flags or file, runs the decide flow, and prints the JSON transport response with an exit code.

nodes.name: build_parser
nodes.summary: Builds the gate subcommand argparse grammar (decide, override submit, override apply) with their flag sets.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Gate CLI entry: parses gate subcommand args and dispatches to the decide / override-submit / override-apply handlers.

nodes.name: hil_tabular_projector.py
nodes.summary: Operator-facing HIL surface projector (Story 42.1): re-shapes gate pause material — gate identity, enrichment metrics, ungrounded advisories, learning objectives, and per-gate content — into paginated markdown tables via a content-type renderer registry, replacing the unreviewable dense-JSON gate surface.
nodes.languageNotes: Renderer registry pattern: each gate content type registers a render function; pagination follows the Marcus HIL Display Standards (~15 rows per page with 'show next' affordance) and rows carry sequential indices.

nodes.name: render_enrichment_metrics
nodes.summary: Renders the G0 enrichment summary metrics (component counts, grounded/ungrounded totals) as a compact markdown table.

nodes.name: render_ungrounded_advisories
nodes.summary: Renders one-row-per-flag ungrounded-component advisories from enrichment results as a paginated markdown table, replacing the former unreadable log-line sheaf.

nodes.name: render_learning_objectives
nodes.summary: Renders one-row-per-LO learning objectives as a paginated markdown table with sequential indices.

nodes.name: render_generic_gate_content
nodes.summary: Fallback renderer for gate content without a registered specialist: summarizes nested values shallowly into a key/value markdown table.

nodes.name: render_directive_sources
nodes.summary: Renders the Section 02A directive review surface: included and excluded source tables with role, provider, and exclusion-reason columns plus gamma variant slugs and corpus basename context.

nodes.name: render_per_slide_mode
nodes.summary: Renders the per-slide-mode gate content as paginated per-slide rows after unwrapping the payload envelope.

nodes.name: render_variant_ab
nodes.summary: Renders the A/B variant comparison gate content as a paginated markdown table for operator review.

nodes.name: render_voice_candidates
nodes.summary: Renders the voice-candidate selection card: per-candidate voice options with attributes flattened into paginated table rows for the operator's pick.

nodes.name: render_plan_unit
nodes.summary: Renders the lesson-plan unit gate content, extracting the plan-unit body and presenting its fields and items as paginated markdown tables.

nodes.name: render_estimator
nodes.summary: Renders the cost/effort estimator gate content into summary and line-item markdown tables.

nodes.name: render_run_constants
nodes.summary: Renders the run-constants gate content (settings frozen for the run) as a key/value markdown table.

nodes.name: render_literal_visual
nodes.summary: Renders the literal-visual gate content rows (visual directives per slide element) as a paginated markdown table.

nodes.name: render_storyboard_targets
nodes.summary: Renders storyboard target rows (per-slide targets) as a paginated markdown table for operator review.

nodes.name: render_storyboard_b
nodes.summary: Renders the Storyboard-B gate content with its per-card structure flattened into paginated markdown tables.

nodes.name: render_input_package
nodes.summary: Renders the input-package gate content (assembled inputs handed to a downstream stage) as summary plus item tables.

nodes.name: render_final_handoff
nodes.summary: Renders the final-handoff gate content (deliverable summary at run close) into operator-reviewable markdown tables.

nodes.name: render_motion_plan
nodes.summary: Renders the motion-plan gate content (planned motion sequences per slide) with sequence counts as paginated markdown tables.

nodes.name: render_motion_clip
nodes.summary: Renders the motion-clip gate content (generated clip records) as paginated markdown tables for operator review.

nodes.name: render_gate_content
nodes.summary: Dispatches gate content to the registered renderer for its content type, falling back to the generic renderer when no specialist is registered.

nodes.name: render_hil_tables
nodes.summary: Renders a complete HIL gate surface — identity, enrichment metrics, ungrounded advisories, learning objectives, and typed gate content — into the ordered markdown-table block shown to the operator.

nodes.name: build_gate_surface
nodes.summary: Assembles the structured gate-surface object from gate identity, enrichment, learning objectives, and gate content ready for rendering and emission.

nodes.name: emit_gate_surface
nodes.summary: Writes the rendered HIL surface to the output stream with optional next-action command, shell context, and raw-artifact pointer so the operator can review and act without leaving the terminal.

nodes.name: next_action.py
nodes.summary: Next-action command builder (Epic 35 AD-3): the single authority that composes the exact copy-paste CLI command per pause class (paused-at-gate → trial resume inline verdict, paused-at-error → trial recover, provider batch → trial resume-batch), co-located with the CLI grammar and stdlib-only for lazy import.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately imports stdlib only so the assembler can import it without dragging in the app.marcus.cli package init; an L1 test round-trips every built string through the actual argparse parsers.

nodes.name: _read_card_fields
nodes.summary: Reads decision-card fields (card id, digest, allowed verbs, operator id) from the on-disk card JSON, tolerating missing or partial files.

nodes.name: _render_neutral_gate_next_action
nodes.summary: Renders the neutral paused-at-gate next-action block: a trial resume inline-verdict command template with card id, digest, operator id, and the allowed verbs list.

nodes.name: build_next_action
nodes.summary: Maps a pause envelope to its exact next CLI command: trial resume for gate pauses (inline-verdict mode), trial recover for error pauses, and trial resume-batch for provider-batch waits.

nodes.name: plan_dialogue_cli.py
nodes.summary: Marcus interactive planning dialogue CLI (Mine 2A): a thin pre-start REPL that elicits purpose, audience, workflow, gap-fill choice, and LOs, ratifies with confirm-before-writes, writes companion artifacts plus ratified-los.json, and persists a transcript — reusing the assess/ratify/write engine rather than reimplementing it.

nodes.name: PlanDialogueSession
nodes.summary: Stateful dialogue session that asks and confirms each planning question in turn, records DialogueTurns, ratifies the planning decision, writes ratification artifacts and ratified LOs, and persists the full transcript.

nodes.name: build_plan_dialogue_parser
nodes.summary: Defines the plan-dialogue subcommand's argparse flags (corpus/bundle inputs, output dir, scripted-answers file, non-interactive options).

nodes.name: _load_script
nodes.summary: Loads and validates a scripted-answers file (JSON/YAML) for non-interactive dialogue runs, rejecting malformed or incomplete scripts.

nodes.name: run_plan_dialogue
nodes.summary: Drives a PlanDialogueSession end-to-end from parsed args over injected input/output seams: elicitation, ratification, artifact writes, and transcript persistence.

nodes.name: plan_dialogue_cli
nodes.summary: Argparse-facing handler for the plan-dialogue subcommand: wires stdin/stdout or a scripted input source into run_plan_dialogue and maps failures to exit codes.

nodes.name: plan_ratify_cli.py
nodes.summary: Marcus planning ratification CLI: thin adapter that assesses the course source (from an input bundle or corpus dir), ratifies the planning decision with gap-fill tradeoffs and optional collateral spec, and writes the ratification artifacts.

nodes.name: build_plan_ratify_parser
nodes.summary: Defines the plan-ratify subcommand's argparse flags: source inputs (bundle/corpus), workflow and gap-fill choices, considered alternatives, collateral spec path, and output locations.

nodes.name: _load_collateral
nodes.summary: Loads and validates a CollateralSpec from a YAML/JSON file, surfacing validation failures as CLI errors.

nodes.name: _assess
nodes.summary: Produces the SourceAssessment for ratification, either from a built lesson-planning input bundle or directly from a corpus directory.

nodes.name: run_plan_ratify
nodes.summary: Executes assess → ratify → write: builds the assessment, ratifies the planning decision with the chosen workflow and gap-fill tradeoff, and writes the ratification artifacts, returning their paths.

nodes.name: plan_ratify_cli
nodes.summary: Argparse-facing handler for the plan-ratify subcommand: runs the assess/ratify/write flow and prints the artifact paths or a structured error with an exit code.

nodes.name: trial.py
nodes.summary: CLI shim for production-clone trial launches: implements start/resume/recover/resume-batch trial commands over the production runner, handling directive confirmation/editing, live-env preflight, styleguide picker integration, and gate-surface emission on pause.
nodes.languageNotes: Injectable function parameters (confirm_fn, picker_preflight_fn, input_fn) enable deterministic testing of interactive CLI flows without mocking stdin.

nodes.name: _require_live_env_unless_offline
nodes.summary: Fails fast with a RuntimeError when live OpenAI/LangSmith environment variables are missing, unless the offline cost-report escape hatch is explicitly enabled.

nodes.name: _emit_gate_surface_if_paused
nodes.summary: After a trial pauses at a HIL gate, reads the pause payload and decision-card JSON from disk and emits the operator-facing gate surface (tables plus next-action block) via the HIL tabular projector.

nodes.name: _confirm_or_edit_directive
nodes.summary: Interactive G0 loop letting the operator confirm, edit in $EDITOR, or decline the composed directive before the run starts; raises when confirmation is required but no TTY is attached.

nodes.name: _resolve_start_component_selection
nodes.summary: Resolves which ComponentSelection a starting trial should use, preferring a ratified collateral intent file, then a lesson-plan JSON, then the bundle catalog or front-door interactive selection.

nodes.name: start_trial
nodes.summary: Core trial-launch orchestration: validates the corpus leaf, runs picker preflight, composes and confirms the directive, wires workbook runtime context, then invokes run_production_trial and persists the trial-start receipt.

nodes.name: start_trial_cli
nodes.summary: Argparse adapter for the start command: maps parsed CLI args to start_trial, prints operator-friendly errors for each failure class, and emits the gate surface when the run pauses.

nodes.name: resume_trial
nodes.summary: Resumes a paused trial from an operator verdict (file or inline), re-checking live-env requirements and delegating to resume_production_trial before writing a trial-resume receipt.

nodes.name: resume_trial_cli
nodes.summary: Argparse adapter for the resume command supporting both verdict-file and inline verdict flags, with mutual-exclusion validation and gate-surface emission on re-pause.

nodes.name: recover_trial
nodes.summary: Recovers a trial from an error pause, optionally re-entering at a named node and migrating legacy workbook runtime context, then delegates to recover_production_trial and writes a trial-recover receipt.

nodes.name: recover_trial_cli
nodes.summary: Argparse adapter for the recover command; forwards recovery options to recover_trial and emits the gate surface if the recovered run pauses again.

nodes.name: _migrate_legacy_workbook_context
nodes.summary: Backfills the workbook-brief runtime context for legacy error-paused runs that predate the context file, validating the course-source root and the run envelope's contributions before writing the migrated context.

nodes.name: resume_batch_trial
nodes.summary: Resumes a trial paused on a provider batch wait (e.g., video generation), delegating to resume_batch_production_trial and reporting whether the batch completed or is still pending.

nodes.name: resume_batch_trial_cli
nodes.summary: Argparse adapter for the resume-batch command; invokes resume_batch_trial and emits the gate surface if the run pauses at a gate afterwards.

nodes.name: build_trial_parser
nodes.summary: Registers the start/resume/recover/resume-batch subcommands and their full flag surface (presets, collateral intent, picker options, encounter mode, prewalk settings) on the trial argument parser.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the course-source substrate: re-exports asset records, canonical processed-source validators, input bundles, manifest scanning/drift, course models, registry, SME registry, and syllabus metadata APIs.

nodes.name: asset_records.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic models and factories for canonical asset records describing course-provided and missing source assets, including asset kinds, statuses, source references, and requirement-gap emission.
nodes.languageNotes: Frozen Pydantic-v2 models with a model_validator enforcing that source-grounded records carry at least one content source ref.

nodes.name: make_syllabus_requirement_record
nodes.summary: Factory building a CanonicalAssetRecord for a syllabus-declared requirement with a single locator-backed source reference.

nodes.name: make_lesson_lo_record
nodes.summary: Factory building a CanonicalAssetRecord bound to lesson learning objectives with optional grounding source refs.

nodes.name: emit_requirement_gap_records
nodes.summary: Emits gap-status asset records for declared requirements whose source material is missing, so absent assets remain visible in planning.

nodes.name: CanonicalAssetRecord
nodes.summary: The canonical, schema-versioned record for one course asset: kind, course/module/lesson scoping, source refs, learning-objective refs, derivation, status, and review flags, with a validator requiring content refs for source-grounded records.

nodes.name: canonical_processed_source.py
nodes.summary: Canonical processed-source contract and validators (Mine 4A): enforces the greenlit lesson-leaf layout (slides/references/assessments) and run-dir layout (bundle/extracted.md, g0-enrichment.json), with AssetKind shape-pins on enrichment nodes and a contract-doc writer.

nodes.name: validate_lesson_leaf
nodes.summary: Validates a lesson-leaf directory against the canonical layout, collecting per-path digests and errors into a CanonicalValidationResult.

nodes.name: validate_run_dir
nodes.summary: Validates a run directory's canonical artifacts (bundle/extracted.md and g0-enrichment.json), including closed-kind checks on enrichment nodes.

nodes.name: validate_canonical_tree
nodes.summary: Scope-dispatching entry point that routes to lesson-leaf or run-dir validation based on the requested canonical scope.

nodes.name: annotate_typed_components_with_kind
nodes.summary: Annotates typed enrichment components in a G0 payload with their resolved AssetKind, pinning the closed-kind shape for downstream consumers.

nodes.name: write_canonical_contract_doc
nodes.summary: Writes the human-readable canonical processed-source contract document describing the enforced layouts and kind rules.

nodes.name: CanonicalValidationResult
nodes.summary: Validation outcome model carrying scope, root, ok flag, errors, path digests, and kind counts, with raise_if_failed and to_dict helpers.

nodes.name: input_bundle.py
nodes.summary: Builds the lesson-planning input bundle: merges course/module planning profiles, the scoped source manifest, asset records, gap ledger, styleguide resolution, and component selection into one YAML-serializable LessonPlanningInputBundle.

nodes.name: build_lesson_planning_input_bundle
nodes.summary: Top-level assembler that loads the course record and module proposal, scans the scoped manifest, resolves the SME styleguide, and returns the complete LessonPlanningInputBundle for a module.

nodes.name: LessonPlanningInputBundle
nodes.summary: Schema-versioned bundle model of everything lesson planning consumes — course/module profiles, learning objectives, scoped manifest, asset records, gap ledger, capabilities, and styleguide resolution — with a scope-and-gap contract validator.

nodes.name: manifest_drift.py
nodes.summary: Detects drift between a persisted course-source manifest snapshot and a fresh deterministic scan, returning a unified diff when the snapshot is stale.

nodes.name: check_manifest_snapshot
nodes.summary: Rescans the course root, normalizes both snapshot and fresh manifest text, and returns a ManifestDriftResult with staleness flag and diff lines.

nodes.name: manifest_scan.py
nodes.summary: Deterministic course-source scanner: walks a course root using git-tracked/ignored classification, builds the SourceManifest with scoped, hashed file entries, and derives the gap ledger comparing declared availability against detected files.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses git plumbing (ls-files) via subprocess to classify tracked vs ignored paths, keeping the scan reproducible against repository state.

nodes.name: _gaps
nodes.summary: Derives gap entries by cross-checking declared course/module/source expectations against detected manifest entries, emitting typed, severity-ranked GapEntry records.

nodes.name: scan_course
nodes.summary: Entry point producing a CourseSourceScan: loads the course record, enumerates hashed file entries with scope/module/lesson attribution, and pairs the manifest with its derived gap ledger.

nodes.name: models.py
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic records for course-source ingestion planning: course/module declarations, source availability, manifest entries, and the gap ledger, all pinned to schema version 0.1.

nodes.name: CourseRecord
nodes.summary: Top-level declaration of a course container: SME, course details, source references, expected modules, status, runtime contract, and source purpose/availability, with a pinned schema version.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the course.yaml container declaration at a course root into a CourseRecord.

nodes.name: load_course
nodes.summary: Reads course.yaml under the course root and validates it into a CourseRecord, raising if the declaration file is absent.

nodes.name: syllabus_metadata.py
nodes.summary: Deterministic syllabus readers (DOCX and MHTML) and module-metadata proposal builders: extracts course title, learner profile, learning objectives, and per-module records with source anchors, emitting gap entries for unsupported or missing content.
nodes.languageNotes: Every extracted value is wrapped in an AnchoredText carrying its source path and locator, so downstream proposals stay traceable to syllabus positions; MHTML tables are parsed with a custom HTMLParser subclass.

nodes.name: read_docx_syllabus
nodes.summary: Reads a .docx syllabus into an ExtractedSyllabusDocument of anchored paragraphs and tables using python-docx.

nodes.name: read_mhtml_syllabus
nodes.summary: Reads an MHTML-exported syllabus by decoding the MIME payload and parsing its HTML tables into an ExtractedSyllabusDocument.

nodes.name: build_module_metadata_proposal
nodes.summary: Reads the syllabus at a path and delegates to the document-based builder to produce a CourseMetadataProposal for the course root.

nodes.name: build_module_metadata_proposal_from_document
nodes.summary: Builds the full CourseMetadataProposal from an extracted syllabus document: course title, learner profile, course objectives, per-module proposals, and gap records for missing or unsupported extractions.

nodes.name: ModuleMetadataProposal
nodes.summary: Per-module proposal record (status, proposed slug/title, topics, source-bucket suggestions, source refs) with a validator requiring source anchors on proposed records.

nodes.name: _HtmlTableParser
nodes.summary: Minimal HTMLParser subclass that collects table rows and cell text from MHTML syllabus exports without external HTML dependencies.

nodes.name: bundle_catalog.py
nodes.summary: Curated catalog of the three ratified component bundles (S4): declarative BundleRecords over ComponentSelection plus per-component capability tiers so Marcus can gate honestly on what is proven wired versus regressed or unproven.
nodes.languageNotes: Frozen registry pattern: Pydantic-v2 closed-enum records wrapped in types.MappingProxyType, mirroring the modality/component-type registry idiom.

nodes.name: get_bundle
nodes.summary: Looks up a BundleRecord by id in the frozen catalog, raising KeyError for unknown bundle ids.

nodes.name: min_capability_tier
nodes.summary: Computes the weakest capability tier across a bundle's selected components, which drives the honesty gating of bundle readiness.

nodes.name: ComponentCapability
nodes.summary: Record pairing a component with its capability tier (proven_wired, proven_regressed_repairable, unproven) and an operator-facing note explaining current reality.

nodes.name: BundleRecord
nodes.summary: A named, blessed ComponentSelection with display metadata, required inputs, and a validator ensuring every selected component carries a capability tier.

nodes.name: collateral_selection.py
nodes.summary: The selection edge from a ratified lesson-plan collateral intent to a curated ComponentSelection: validates the intent, resolves it through the bundle catalog, and returns the blessed selection for the production runner.

nodes.name: resolve_lesson_plan_collateral_selection
nodes.summary: Resolves a raw intent payload into a ResolvedCollateralSelection, validating ratification status, bundle/collateral pairing, and unknown-bundle handling before returning the catalog's blessed selection.

nodes.name: load_lesson_plan_collateral_selection
nodes.summary: Loads a ratified collateral-intent file from disk and resolves it into a ResolvedCollateralSelection, wrapping parse failures in CollateralSelectionError.

nodes.name: derive_selection_from_lesson_plan
nodes.summary: Derives the component selection directly from an Irene lesson-plan payload's collateral declaration, mapping declared collateral kinds onto catalog bundles.

nodes.name: load_selection_from_lesson_plan_json
nodes.summary: Loads a lesson-plan JSON artifact from disk and derives the collateral-driven ComponentSelection from it, with defensive error wrapping.

nodes.name: RatifiedLessonPlanCollateralIntent
nodes.summary: The S8 intent contract: ratification status, bundle id, collateral declaration, and a local-only source_ref validator that rejects remote references at the selection edge.

nodes.name: collateral_spec.py
nodes.summary: CollateralSpec content-model family (Braid S1/DP4): the workbook spec the client collateral is built from — sections bound to learning objectives, depth-delta contracts, exercises with answer-key source refs, and research-enrichment goals — emitted on Irene Pass-1 lesson plans.
nodes.languageNotes: Contract discipline enforced by validators: every section must bind a learning_objective_id and carry a depth_delta; research goals reject raw fetch-query phrasing in favor of pedagogical intent.

nodes.name: DepthDeltaContract
nodes.summary: Explicit declaration of what depth is deferred off the glance-slide into the workbook section: deferred_from_slide open-id, non-empty deferred depth, and what stays on-slide.

nodes.name: Exercise
nodes.summary: Workbook exercise model with open-id exercise ids, Bloom level, prompt intent, origin, and a shape-validated answer_key_source_ref slot resolved later by the research wiring.

nodes.name: WorkbookSection
nodes.summary: A workbook section binding a learning_objective_id (contract violation if absent), carrying its depth-delta contract, exercises, and narrative intent.

nodes.name: WorkbookSpec
nodes.summary: Container for the workbook collateral kind holding the ordered list of objective-bound sections.

nodes.name: ResearchEnrichmentGoal
nodes.summary: Research goal bound to a learning objective, validated to express pedagogical intent rather than a raw fetch query, for the thin research wiring (S3) to resolve.

nodes.name: CollateralSpec
nodes.summary: Top-level schema-versioned collateral declaration whose discriminant kind must match the populated payload (workbook spec and/or research goals).

nodes.name: planning_context.py
nodes.summary: Loads optional planning context (purpose, audience, ratified LOs, source assessment) from run-dir artifacts for Irene Pass-1, and assesses lesson-plan coverage of learning objectives via token-overlap scoring with a fail-loud receipt.

nodes.name: assess_lo_coverage
nodes.summary: Scores each learning objective against the emitted lesson plan using significant-token overlap, classifying objectives as supported versus weak-or-missing into a PlanningContextCoverageReceipt.

nodes.name: assert_lo_coverage_or_fail
nodes.summary: Fail-loud guard raising PlanningContextError when the coverage receipt reports weak or missing learning objectives.

nodes.name: load_planning_context
nodes.summary: Merges on-disk planning-ratification.json and ratified-los.json under a run directory into a PlanningContext, returning None when artifacts are absent to preserve default Irene behavior.

nodes.name: PlanningContext
nodes.summary: Optional framing context for Irene Pass-1 carrying purpose, audience, learning objectives, source assessment, and claim fence, with payload-dict and has_framing helpers.

nodes.name: PlanningContextCoverageReceipt
nodes.summary: Receipt model recording LO coverage status, supported and weak/missing objective ids, and purpose/audience acknowledgement, with a validator keeping status consistent with the objective sets.

nodes.name: planning_ratification.py
nodes.summary: Lightweight planning ratification recorder (Phase-2 selection bridge): captures purpose, audience, workflow choice, and gap-fill tradeoffs, rejects overclaim language, and emits the canonical S8 RatifiedLessonPlanCollateralIntent plus selection-delta artifacts.

nodes.name: ratify_planning_decision
nodes.summary: Builds the PlanningRatificationRecord from assessment, purpose, audience, workflow, gap-fill, and collateral inputs, deriving the embedded S8 intent and enforcing workflow/gap-fill consistency.

nodes.name: write_ratification_artifacts
nodes.summary: Persists the ratification record and its S8 collateral intent as JSON/YAML artifacts into the output directory for the selection edge to consume.

nodes.name: compute_selection_delta
nodes.summary: Computes the before/after SelectionDelta between two resolved collateral selections so operators can see what the ratification changed.

nodes.name: GapFillTradeoff
nodes.summary: Records the chosen gap-fill approach, considered alternatives, and rationale, with a validator ensuring the chosen option appears among those considered.

nodes.name: PlanningRatificationRecord
nodes.summary: Schema-versioned ratification record binding purpose, audience, source assessment, planned assets, workflow, gap-fill tradeoff, claim fence, and the embedded S8 intent, with non-empty and fence-present validators.

nodes.name: source_assessment.py
nodes.summary: Assesses available source material for planning ratification: thin adapter over course-source input bundles and curated corpus leaves producing a richness-tagged SourceAssessment with gap summaries; performs no remote ingestion.

nodes.name: assess_from_input_bundle
nodes.summary: Derives a SourceAssessment (richness, tags, gap summaries, counts) from a LessonPlanningInputBundle's manifest, asset records, and gap ledger.

nodes.name: assess_from_corpus_dir
nodes.summary: Assesses a curated corpus leaf directly from disk, classifying detected files by kind to derive richness and gap summaries without an input bundle.

nodes.name: assessments_differ_in_kind_or_count
nodes.summary: Compares two SourceAssessments for material differences in richness kind or detected counts, used to detect drift between assessments.

nodes.name: SourceAssessment
nodes.summary: Assessment model carrying corpus id, richness classification, non-empty tags, gap summaries, and asset/file counts for ratification decisions.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_02a.py
nodes.summary: Section 02A HIL poll-surface verdict models: the operator's approve/edit/reject verdict bound to a run, surface, and decision-card digest, with strict UUID4, timezone-aware, SHA-256, and verb/payload consistency validation.
nodes.languageNotes: Follows the repo's Pydantic-v2 schema checklist: enforce_tz_aware and enforce_uuid4_version from state._base plus a model_validator tying the verb to its required payload.

nodes.name: DirectiveEditPayload
nodes.summary: Edit payload carrying directive update blocks for an edit verdict, rejecting empty update blocks at validation time.

nodes.name: Section02AOperatorVerdict
nodes.summary: The Section 02A verdict record: run/surface ids, verb (approve/edit/reject), operator id, tz-aware timestamp, decision-card digest, and verb-consistent edit or reject payloads enforced by validators.

nodes.name: component_selection.py
nodes.summary: ComponentSelection — the canonical record of which components (deck, motion, workbook) a run composes; lives on RunState so resume/recover walks rehydrate it from the frozen run record, with canonical-JSON bytes for content-addressed digests.
nodes.languageNotes: Canonical-JSON serialization (sorted keys, stable encoding) makes the selection hashable in a hashseed-independent way; guards the two-walk rehydration trap.

nodes.name: ComponentSelection
nodes.summary: Frozen selection model over the deck/motion/workbook component flags with production_default factory, as_map/selected_components accessors, and canonical_bytes for stable hashing.

nodes.name: wondercraft_dispatch.py
nodes.summary: Direct Wondercraft dispatch seam for the Wanda audio specialist, routing six capability codes (EP/DP/AS/MB/CM/AH) to per-capability handlers that validate payloads and invoke the WondercraftClient.
nodes.languageNotes: Capability-code table pattern: a frozenset of codes plus one underscore-prefixed handler function per code, unified behind a single dispatch entry point.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the tool API client library, re-exporting BaseAPIClient, the shared error types, and every tool-specific client (Botpress, Canvas, Descript, ElevenLabs, Gamma, Notion, Panopto, Qualtrics, Wondercraft).

nodes.name: base_client.py
nodes.summary: Foundation HTTP client that all tool-specific API clients extend: authenticated requests.Session management, exponential-backoff retry with Retry-After support, Link-header pagination, and structured error diagnostics via APIError/AuthenticationError/RateLimitError.
nodes.languageNotes: Template-method design: subclasses supply base_url and auth headers in __init__ while inheriting the full request/retry/pagination machinery.

nodes.name: botpress_client.py
nodes.summary: Botpress cloud API client for chatbot management: bot listing/creation, conversation creation, message sending, intent detection, and bot deployment over Bearer-token auth.

nodes.name: canvas_client.py
nodes.summary: Canvas LMS API client covering course, module, page, assignment, quiz, and enrollment operations with Link-header pagination, plus a reproduce_course_snapshot diagnostic that pulls a full course inventory.

nodes.name: descript_client.py
nodes.summary: Descript Public API (Early Access) client covering the project build lifecycle: media import via URL or signed-URL direct upload, job polling, Underlord agent edits, and publish/export.

nodes.name: elevenlabs_client.py
nodes.summary: ElevenLabs API client for narration and audio generation: text-to-speech (with request-id and character-timestamp variants), pronunciation dictionaries, sound effects, multi-voice dialogue, and music generation, each with file-writing convenience wrappers.
nodes.languageNotes: Paired method pattern: every generation endpoint has a bytes-returning core method plus a *_file wrapper that writes output and creates parent directories.

nodes.name: gamma_client.py
nodes.summary: Gamma API client for AI slide generation: theme listing, generation submission with warm-401 burst-throttle retry, template-based generation for Studio cards, and polling until generation completes.
nodes.languageNotes: Encodes the Gamma quirk that burst throttling returns 401 (not 429); _post_generation_with_warm_401_retry sleeps and retries once on a warm key.

nodes.name: kling_client.py
nodes.summary: Kling AI video-generation client supporting text-to-video, image-to-video, lip-sync, and video extension, with dual auth (static API token or auto-refreshed HS256 JWT from access/secret keys) and task polling plus download helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: Overrides _request/_request_raw to refresh the JWT before every call when in legacy-key mode; static-token mode bypasses JWT minting entirely.

nodes.name: WondercraftDispatchError
nodes.summary: Wanda-specific dispatch error subclassing SpecialistDispatchError, raised on unknown capabilities or invalid operation payloads.

nodes.name: _ep_podcast_episode_produce
nodes.summary: EP capability handler: validates a non-empty prompt and calls WondercraftClient.create_podcast to produce a podcast episode.

nodes.name: _dp_podcast_dialogue_produce
nodes.summary: DP capability handler: validates a dialogue script and calls WondercraftClient.create_conversation_podcast for a two-voice podcast.

nodes.name: _as_audio_summary_produce
nodes.summary: AS capability handler: validates script and voice_id then calls WondercraftClient.create_scripted_podcast to produce a single-voice audio summary.

nodes.name: _mb_music_bed_apply
nodes.summary: MB capability handler: checks Wondercraft connectivity and returns a music-bed application record for the assembly stage.

nodes.name: _cm_chapter_markers_emit
nodes.summary: CM capability handler: validates a segments list and emits enumerated chapter markers for the produced audio.

nodes.name: _ah_audio_assembly_handoff
nodes.summary: AH capability handler: builds a timestamped audio-assembly handoff record pointing downstream assembly at the produced assets.

nodes.name: dispatch_to_wondercraft
nodes.summary: Main dispatch entry point: normalizes the capability code, validates it against CAPABILITY_CODES, constructs a WondercraftClient when none is injected, routes to the matching handler, and wraps failures in WondercraftDispatchError.

nodes.name: APIError
nodes.summary: Base exception for API failures, carrying the HTTP status code and parsed response body for diagnostics.

nodes.name: AuthenticationError
nodes.summary: APIError subclass raised on 401/403 responses to distinguish credential failures from other API errors.

nodes.name: RateLimitError
nodes.summary: APIError subclass raised when retries are exhausted against a rate-limited (429) endpoint.

nodes.name: BaseAPIClient
nodes.summary: Shared HTTP client base class providing get/post/put/patch/delete plus raw variants, Link-header pagination via get_paginated, exponential-backoff retry with Retry-After parsing, and structured error-message composition.

nodes.name: BotpressClient
nodes.summary: Botpress cloud client exposing connectivity checks, bot list/create, conversation creation, message send, intent detection, and deployment, resolving the target bot id from arguments or environment.

nodes.name: CanvasClient
nodes.summary: Canvas LMS client with methods for courses, modules, pages, assignments, quizzes, and enrollments, using paginated GETs with per-page defaults and Bearer-token auth from environment.

nodes.name: reproduce_course_snapshot
nodes.summary: Diagnostic routine that resolves a course id (explicit or first available), then pulls the course record, modules, pages, assignments, and quizzes into a printed snapshot summary.

nodes.name: DescriptClient
nodes.summary: Descript Public API client: project/job listing and retrieval, wait_for_job polling with progress callbacks, media import (URL or direct upload with signed-URL PUT), Underlord agent_edit invocation, and publish/export.

nodes.name: TtsResult
nodes.summary: Lightweight result container pairing generated audio bytes with the ElevenLabs request-id header for traceability and stitching.

nodes.name: ElevenLabsClient
nodes.summary: Full-surface ElevenLabs client: voice/model/user queries, TTS with voice-settings, request-id capture and character timestamps, pronunciation dictionary CRUD, sound effects, multi-voice dialogue, and music generation, each with file-output wrappers.

nodes.name: GammaClient
nodes.summary: Gamma slide-generation client: list_themes, generate and generate_from_template submissions guarded by a warm-401 retry, generate_deck convenience that submits and waits, and wait_for_generation polling with timeout.

nodes.name: generate_jwt_token
nodes.summary: Mints a short-lived HS256 JWT from Kling access and secret keys with issued-at/expiry claims, used for the legacy Kling auth scheme.

nodes.name: KlingClient
nodes.summary: Kling video-generation client with automatic token management (_ensure_token refreshes JWT or applies static token before each request), text_to_video/image_to_video/lip_sync/extend_video submissions, generate_motion convenience, task-status polling, and video download.

nodes.name: kling_public_client.py
nodes.summary: Exploratory client for Kling's newer 3.0 / Singapore API surface, deliberately kept alongside the validated JWT-based KlingClient; normalizes model/mode names and heterogeneous status payloads until this surface is proven stable.
nodes.languageNotes: Parallel-lane pattern: the production-safe path stays on api.klingai.com/kling-v2-6; this client explores the alternate surface without replacing the validated one.

nodes.name: notion_client.py
nodes.summary: Notion REST API client for the Source Wrangler (Story 3.9): retrieves page metadata, walks block trees depth-first, exports simplified markdown, and appends feedback paragraphs using Notion-Version 2022-06-28.

nodes.name: panopto_client.py
nodes.summary: Panopto video-platform API client with OAuth2 client-credentials authentication and folder/session listing, retrieval, and search operations.

nodes.name: qualtrics_client.py
nodes.summary: Qualtrics API client for survey and assessment management (surveys, questions, distributions, response exports) that unwraps the standard result envelope, plus a reproduce_survey_snapshot diagnostic.

nodes.name: wondercraft_client.py
nodes.summary: Wondercraft API client for podcast and audio-episode generation: connectivity checks, episode listing, prompt-based/scripted/conversation podcast creation, and job-status polling.

nodes.name: elevenlabs_api_sweep.py
nodes.summary: Live ElevenLabs API exploration sweep (P5 directed-voice arc) that exercises every locally-available TTS request parameter with real calls, recording results or precise unavailability reasons; deliberately broader than the production heartbeat and mock-free.
nodes.languageNotes: First-run-stands honesty discipline: each parameter probe is a real paid call recorded as-is, never retried to green.

nodes.name: build_descript_narrated_lesson.py
nodes.summary: Operator CLI that builds a narrated-slide video in Descript from an APP assembly bundle: direct-uploads slide PNGs and per-segment narration MP3s, polls the import job, invokes Underlord to assemble the slideshow, and gates publication on an attested duration receipt.

nodes.name: descript_publication_receipt.py
nodes.summary: Fail-loud Descript publication-receipt helper: a pure, unit-testable gate that raises DescriptPublicationError when the assembled composition duration is missing/zero (server-side detachment) or drifts beyond tolerance from the expected audio total, returning an attested receipt only on pass.
nodes.languageNotes: Exists because Underlord assembly detaches server-side: a premature get_project returns duration 0/None, so the receipt must be code-attested rather than hand-authored.

nodes.name: run_story33_kling_queue.py
nodes.summary: Operational runner for Story 3.3 Kling validation clips: serializes submissions one at a time (Kling enforces a concurrency limit) with backoff on submit, polling, video download, and CSV manifest logging between requests.

nodes.name: _normalize_model_name
nodes.summary: Maps requested Kling model aliases onto the model names accepted by the Singapore API surface for the given mode.

nodes.name: _extract_video_url
nodes.summary: Digs the generated video URL out of the heterogeneous nested shapes the Kling public API returns in task payloads.

nodes.name: _extract_duration
nodes.summary: Extracts the clip duration from varying task-payload shapes, tolerating string or numeric encodings.

nodes.name: _normalize_status_payload
nodes.summary: Normalizes the Singapore surface's task-status responses into the canonical status/video-url/duration shape the rest of the pipeline expects.

nodes.name: KlingPublicClient
nodes.summary: Exploratory Kling 3.0/Singapore-surface client mirroring KlingClient's interface (text_to_video, image_to_video, status polling, download) with per-request JWT refresh via the shared generate_jwt_token helper and normalized status payloads.

nodes.name: _block_to_lines
nodes.summary: Converts a single Notion block into simplified markdown lines, handling headings, lists, paragraphs, and other supported block types.

nodes.name: NotionClient
nodes.summary: Notion REST client that retrieves and searches pages, iterates and walks block trees depth-first, renders pages to simplified markdown, and appends paragraph feedback blocks.

nodes.name: PanoptoClient
nodes.summary: Panopto client that authenticates via OAuth2 client credentials (lazy _ensure_auth) and exposes folder and session list/get/search operations.

nodes.name: _resolve_base_url
nodes.summary: Resolves the Qualtrics API base URL from an explicit argument or datacenter environment variables.

nodes.name: QualtricsClient
nodes.summary: Qualtrics client covering whoami, survey CRUD, question creation, distributions, and response-export start/progress, unwrapping the standard {result, meta} envelope via _result.

nodes.name: reproduce_survey_snapshot
nodes.summary: Diagnostic routine that authenticates, lists surveys page by page, and prints a survey inventory snapshot for verification.

nodes.name: WondercraftClient
nodes.summary: Wondercraft client exposing connectivity checks, episode listing, prompt-based/scripted/conversation podcast creation, job-status retrieval, and wait_for_job polling.

nodes.name: tts
nodes.summary: Sweep probe helper: performs one labeled live text_to_speech call with the given parameter combination, hashing and recording the outcome or the precise failure detail.

nodes.name: build_assembly_prompt
nodes.summary: Constructs the Underlord natural-language assembly prompt instructing Descript to hold each slide for its narration segment in order with captions on.

nodes.name: collect_media
nodes.summary: Gathers slide PNGs and per-segment narration MP3s from the bundle directories into the ordered media list for upload.

nodes.name: resolve_expected_audio_total_s
nodes.summary: Computes the expected total narration duration in seconds from the audio directory, providing the ground truth the publication receipt gate compares against.

nodes.name: publish_project
nodes.summary: Publishes the assembled Descript composition at the requested resolution and returns the publish result with share/download URLs.

nodes.name: _load_env_if_available
nodes.summary: Best-effort dotenv loader that populates DESCRIPT_API_KEY and related settings from .env when python-dotenv is installed.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Main orchestration: collects media, direct-uploads into a new Descript project, polls the import job, invokes the Underlord agent to assemble the narrated slideshow, verifies duration via build_publication_receipt, and optionally publishes.

nodes.name: parse_args
nodes.summary: Defines and parses the CLI arguments (bundle paths, dry-run, publish resolution) for the narrated-lesson builder.

nodes.name: DescriptPublicationError
nodes.summary: RuntimeError subclass raised when the Descript composition duration is missing/zero (detached assembly) or drifts beyond tolerance from the expected audio total.

nodes.name: build_publication_receipt
nodes.summary: Pure attestation gate: validates the assembled composition duration against the expected audio total within tolerance, raising DescriptPublicationError on detachment or drift, and returns the authoritative receipt dict only on pass.

nodes.name: write_manifest
nodes.summary: Writes the validation-clip job rows (status, task ids, file paths) to the CSV manifest for durable progress tracking.

nodes.name: submit_with_backoff
nodes.summary: Submits one Kling job and retries with backoff when the API rejects it for the concurrency limit, honoring Kling's one-at-a-time constraint.

nodes.name: poll_and_download
nodes.summary: Polls a submitted Kling task until completion and downloads the resulting video to the target filename.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Queue driver: loads or initializes the manifest, then for each pending validation clip submits with backoff, polls and downloads, and persists manifest state before moving to the next job.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the Cora dev-lane, re-exporting the block-mode guard node and the dev-graph manifest/compile public API.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel with explicit __all__ re-export list.

nodes.name: block_mode_node.py
nodes.summary: Cora's in-graph block-mode guard node: dynamically loads the bmad-agent-cora preclosure hook from the skills tree and raises a GateError when L1 preclosure checks fail against the story's diff paths.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses importlib.util.spec_from_file_location to load a skill-quarantined script (skills/bmad-agent-cora/scripts/preclosure_hook.py) outside the normal import path.

nodes.name: _load_preclosure_hook
nodes.summary: Loads the preclosure hook module from skills/bmad-agent-cora/scripts by file path via importlib, returning the PreClosureResult type and run_preclosure_check callable.

nodes.name: block_mode_node
nodes.summary: LangGraph node that runs the L1 preclosure check over the story's diff paths and raises GateError with violation details when block-mode triggers, otherwise passes the StoryState through.

nodes.name: graph.py
nodes.summary: Defines the Cora dev-lane graph substrate: Pydantic manifest models (nodes, edges, thread namespace), YAML manifest loading with validation, dynamic handler resolution, and compilation of the manifest into a LangGraph StateGraph handle.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic v2 models with field/model validators enforcing dev-lane thread-namespace prefixes and graph topology; handlers are resolved at compile time from dotted-path strings via import_module.

nodes.name: DevGraphNodeSpec
nodes.summary: Pydantic spec for a single dev-graph node: id, label, and the dotted-path handler reference.

nodes.name: DevGraphEdgeSpec
nodes.summary: Pydantic spec for a dev-graph edge with from_node/to endpoints, aliased for YAML manifest field names.

nodes.name: DevGraphManifest
nodes.summary: Top-level manifest model for the dev graph, validating schema version, node/edge lists, dev-lane thread-namespace prefix, and overall graph topology (non-empty, referenced nodes exist).

nodes.name: CompiledGraphHandle
nodes.summary: Wrapper holding the compiled LangGraph, its source manifest, and the thread-namespace template with a per-story formatter method.

nodes.name: _resolve_handler
nodes.summary: Resolves a dotted-path handler reference (module:attr) to a callable via import_module, raising CompileError on malformed refs, missing attributes, or non-callables.

nodes.name: load_dev_graph_manifest
nodes.summary: Loads and parses a dev-graph YAML manifest from disk, wrapping missing-file, YAML-parse, non-mapping, and Pydantic-validation failures in ManifestValidationError.

nodes.name: format_thread_namespace
nodes.summary: Formats the dev-lane thread-namespace string for a given story id.

nodes.name: _build_graph
nodes.summary: Constructs a LangGraph StateGraph from a validated manifest by adding nodes with resolved handlers and wiring edges including START/END targets.

nodes.name: compile_dev_graph
nodes.summary: Public entry point that coerces a manifest (path or model), builds the StateGraph, compiles it, and returns a CompiledGraphHandle.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Shared helper package for Cora dev-graph handlers, providing append_checkpoint which records a NodeCheckpoint with a SanctumFingerprint onto the StoryState.

nodes.name: append_checkpoint
nodes.summary: Builds a completed NodeCheckpoint (with sha256-derived SanctumFingerprint and UTC timestamps) for a graph node and returns the state update appending it to node_checkpoints.

nodes.name: close_story.py
nodes.summary: Dev-graph handler for the close_story node; delegates to append_checkpoint to record the node's completion on the StoryState.

nodes.name: close_story
nodes.summary: Graph node handler that records a close_story checkpoint via append_checkpoint.

nodes.name: implement_story.py
nodes.summary: Dev-graph handler for the implement_story node; delegates to append_checkpoint to record the node's completion on the StoryState.

nodes.name: implement_story
nodes.summary: Graph node handler that records an implement_story checkpoint via append_checkpoint.

nodes.name: plan_story.py
nodes.summary: Dev-graph handler for the plan_story node; delegates to append_checkpoint to record the node's completion on the StoryState.

nodes.name: plan_story
nodes.summary: Graph node handler that records a plan_story checkpoint via append_checkpoint.

nodes.name: review_story.py
nodes.summary: Dev-graph handler for the review_story node; delegates to append_checkpoint to record the node's completion on the StoryState.

nodes.name: review_story
nodes.summary: Graph node handler that records a review_story checkpoint via append_checkpoint.

nodes.name: test_story.py
nodes.summary: Dev-graph handler for the test_story node; delegates to append_checkpoint to record the node's completion on the StoryState.

nodes.name: test_story
nodes.summary: Graph node handler that records a test_story checkpoint via append_checkpoint.

nodes.name: node_checkpoint.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model for a per-node execution checkpoint (node id, step index, status, timestamps, sanctum fingerprint) with timezone-aware datetime enforcement and a complete-requires-completed_at invariant.
nodes.languageNotes: Follows the repo's Pydantic-v2 schema checklist: field validators delegate to shared _base helpers plus a dedicated invariant validator module.

nodes.name: NodeCheckpoint
nodes.summary: Checkpoint record for a single graph-node execution: enforces tz-aware checkpoint/completed timestamps and requires completed_at when status is complete; embeds an optional SanctumFingerprint.

nodes.name: sanctum_fingerprint.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model capturing a sanctum content snapshot fingerprint: UUID4 snapshot id, content sha256, and a timezone-aware capture timestamp.

nodes.name: SanctumFingerprint
nodes.summary: Fingerprint of a sanctum snapshot with UUID4-version and tz-aware datetime enforcement via shared _base validators.

nodes.name: story_state.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic v2 model for the dev-lane story execution state: story id, accumulated NodeCheckpoint list, and a tz-aware updated_at timestamp; serves as the LangGraph state type for the Cora dev graph.

nodes.name: StoryState
nodes.summary: Story-level graph state holding node_checkpoints and updated_at, with tz-aware datetime enforcement; consumed by every Cora dev-graph handler and the block-mode guard.

nodes.name: node_checkpoint_validators.py
nodes.summary: Standalone invariant validator for NodeCheckpoint: enforces that a complete status carries a completed_at timestamp and that non-complete statuses do not.

nodes.name: enforce_complete_requires_completed_at
nodes.summary: Raises ValueError when status/completed_at combinations violate the checkpoint completion invariant (complete without timestamp, or timestamp without complete).

nodes.name: data.py
nodes.summary: The ONLY projection reader on the HUD path: reads state/config/runs/<trial_id>/operator-surface.json from an explicit run-dir in one open-read-close gulp and parses it through the contract's lenient reader, deriving the schema_version:seq ETag for the poll loop.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately avoids ambient run discovery (no coordination.db, no mtime-newest fallback) to kill the April wrong-run defect class; consumers never strict-parse.

nodes.name: ProjectionSnapshot
nodes.summary: Immutable bundle of the raw projection file bytes, the file mtime_ns, and the lenient-parsed value, so the server can serve raw bytes (zero-lie) while deriving the ETag and identity guard from the parsed value.

nodes.name: projection_path
nodes.summary: Returns the canonical operator-surface.json path inside an explicit run directory.

nodes.name: read_snapshot
nodes.summary: Reads the projection file in a single open-read-close pass and returns a ProjectionSnapshot pairing the raw bytes with the lenient-parsed Projection/Unrecognized value.

nodes.name: read_operator_surface
nodes.summary: Convenience reader returning just the parsed projection value (Projection, Unrecognized, or None when the file is absent).

nodes.name: projection_etag
nodes.summary: Derives the schema_version:seq ETag from a parsed projection, falling back to unrecognized:<mtime_ns> so the poll loop still cycles on unparseable snapshots.

nodes.name: public.py
nodes.summary: Public read-only HUD overlay (Story 42.4): a separate tunnel-facing FastAPI app that serves a positive-allowlist scrubbed view of the live projection, never the raw bytes, and never exposes launch_nonce, decision-card, error text, or export paths.
nodes.languageNotes: Security seam is a positive allowlist (copy only named fields), so future projection fields are dropped by default and can never silently leak onto the wire.

nodes.name: build_public_view
nodes.summary: The single security seam: builds a new dict copying ONLY explicitly-named non-sensitive projection fields (identity, steps, health, specialists, trace subsets) into the public wire view.

nodes.name: create_public_hud_app
nodes.summary: Factory for the GET-only public overlay FastAPI app bound to one trial_id and run_dir, serving the rendered flight deck, the allowlisted /projection with ETag/304 handling, and a secretless /healthz.

nodes.name: run_public_hud_server
nodes.summary: Process entry that reads required PUBLIC_HUD_* environment configuration and serves the public overlay app via uvicorn.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Barrel for the flight-deck render package, re-exporting ZONE_IDS, render_page, and render_zones; the render layer never imports the orchestrator or gate machinery (import-linter HUD1).

nodes.name: client.py
nodes.summary: Client-side poll renderer: a vanilla-JS string constant embedded by page.py that polls GET /projection with ETag If-None-Match and re-renders dynamic HUD zones via zone-scoped innerHTML replacement, preserving <details> open state and honoring the urgency auto-expand contract.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure Python string constant containing JavaScript — no framework, no build step; the Python render_zones remains the byte-pinned source of truth under golden test.

nodes.name: page.py
nodes.summary: Pure projection-to-HTML renderer for the Operator HUD flight deck: builds each zone (annunciator, identity, health, walk map, specialists, main-deck context panels, trace) from the projection dict and assembles the full cold-load page with embedded styles and client JS.
nodes.languageNotes: Server renders the full page on cold load; the browser then re-renders only dynamic zones client-side from the projection JSON, mirroring render_zones.

nodes.name: render_page
nodes.summary: Top-level page assembler: wraps render_zones output in the full HTML document with embedded stylesheet and client-side poll script.

nodes.name: render_zones
nodes.summary: Renders every HUD zone from the projection data dict and returns the zone-id to HTML mapping consumed by both the cold-load page and the golden tests.

nodes.name: _view
nodes.summary: Normalizes the raw render data dict into the internal view model (projection sections, envelope, steps, active entry, mode flags) shared by all zone builders.

nodes.name: _annunciator_parts
nodes.summary: Derives the annunciator banner text, CSS class, and next-action hint from run status, pause identity, and staleness signals.

nodes.name: _zone_identity
nodes.summary: Renders the identity zone: trial id, status badge, modality chips (llm execution mode, batch, public overlay), notifications echo, freshness age, and the run-settings panel.

nodes.name: _health_tile
nodes.summary: Renders one health tile with state class, value, last-reading clock, and stale-age handling against the freshness budget.

nodes.name: _map_node
nodes.summary: Renders a single walk-map node with kind (done/active/gate/pending), pause marker, per-step summary tooltip, and locked-artifact details.

nodes.name: _zone_map
nodes.summary: Renders the walk-map zone: orders step entries into the node rail with the walk-index caption and current-step highlighting.

nodes.name: _ctx_preflight
nodes.summary: Renders the preflight context panel listing each phase-01/02 item with pass/fail/missed marker classes and detail text.

nodes.name: _ctx_gate
nodes.summary: Renders the gate-pause context panel: decision card with operator prompt, drafted proposal, evidence artifacts, and the resume command block.

nodes.name: _ctx_error
nodes.summary: Renders the error-pause context panel with the runtime error message, failed node, and recovery command guidance.

nodes.name: _ctx_completed
nodes.summary: Renders the run-completed context panel with deliverable components, export paths, and total-cost readout.

nodes.name: _panel_message
nodes.summary: Builds the main-deck headline message and caption for the current run state (preflight, walking, gate, error, batch, completed, failed).

nodes.name: styles.py
nodes.summary: Flight-deck stylesheet: DESIGN.md color tokens copied verbatim into named constants plus the single dark cockpit CSS theme; golden tests assert token presence so palette drift is a red test.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure string constants, no imports or I/O — the render layer never reaches back into the runtime.

nodes.name: server.py
nodes.summary: GET-only localhost HUD server: a FastAPI app with exactly three GET routes (flight deck, /projection with raw bytes + ETag/304, /healthz with launch_nonce), bound to one trial_id and refusing with a typed 409 on identity mismatch.
nodes.languageNotes: Read-only guarantee is structural: any mutation route on this app is a defect regardless of intent; the raw projection bytes are always served (zero-lie).

nodes.name: _raw_identity_trial_id
nodes.summary: Best-effort extraction of identity.trial_id from raw projection bytes so a readable foreign identity cannot ride a lenient-parse failure past the identity guard.

nodes.name: _if_none_match_matches
nodes.summary: RFC-compliant If-None-Match comparison (quoted and weak-prefixed forms) implementing the 304 decision the route handles itself.

nodes.name: _flight_deck_data
nodes.summary: Assembles the render-data dict for the flight-deck page from a fresh projection snapshot, enforcing the bound-trial identity guard before any render.

nodes.name: create_hud_app
nodes.summary: Factory for the local authority HUD app: GET / renders the flight deck, GET /projection serves raw bytes with quoted ETag and self-implemented 304, GET /healthz returns the launch_nonce for reattach probing.

nodes.name: run_hud_server
nodes.summary: Process entry that reads the pinned HUD_* environment contract (trial id, run dir, launch nonce, port, mode) and serves the local HUD app via uvicorn on localhost.

nodes.name: operator_surface_assembler.py
nodes.summary: The single writer of the operator-surface.json projection: a stateless read-merge-write assembler with atomic Windows-safe writes, seq/progress_seq semantics, a freshness-tick daemon thread, and run-settings resolution/override application for the HUD run-settings panel.
nodes.languageNotes: Construct-on-demand (no singleton) dissolves the two-walk gotcha: each walk constructs an assembler and writes, and the on-disk document IS the state; write failures log-and-skip, never propagate into the walk.

nodes.name: OperatorSurfaceAssembler
nodes.summary: Stateless projection writer keyed (trial_id, runs_root): emits and merges every projection section (steps, preflight items, health, ambient, trace, notifications echo), bumps seq on every write and progress_seq only on progress events, and provides the freshness_tick context manager.

nodes.name: UnknownRunSettingError
nodes.summary: Typed error raised when a run-settings change names a setting key outside the known vocabulary.

nodes.name: resolve_run_settings
nodes.summary: Resolves the effective run-settings section for a run dir from directive content, environment flags, component selection, and previous projection state.

nodes.name: load_run_settings_overrides
nodes.summary: Loads the persisted run-settings override mapping for a run dir, returning an empty mapping when none exists.

nodes.name: apply_run_settings_change
nodes.summary: Validates and persists an operator run-settings change into the run dir's override file, rejecting unknown keys with UnknownRunSettingError.

nodes.name: apply_run_settings_overrides_to_run
nodes.summary: Applies the persisted run-settings overrides onto the live run state so the walk honors operator toggles made from the HUD surface.

nodes.name: preflight.py
nodes.summary: Runtime start-path pre-flight executor: runs ordered phase-01 local checks and phase-02 live heartbeats (OpenAI, Gamma, LangSmith), writing each item into the projection as it completes, and owns launching/reattaching the HUD server, the public overlay tunnel, and status-aware child teardown.
nodes.languageNotes: Launch failures return None rather than raising (a child bind failure is a pre-flight FAIL, never a fall-through); Windows children spawn with CREATE_NO_WINDOW and gate-pause exits leave the HUD child alive and listening.

nodes.name: PreflightResult
nodes.summary: Result of a pre-flight run: the ordered item outcomes plus a blocking_items view distinguishing fail from missed (two different alarms per AD-11).

nodes.name: PreflightDeps
nodes.summary: Injectable dependency bundle for run_preflight (env, item producers, heartbeat callables) enabling deterministic fixture-driven testing of the start path.

nodes.name: PublicOverlayConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for the public overlay lane (tunnel provider, ports, hostname, enablement) built from environment via from_env with validation of the supported provider set.

nodes.name: PublicOverlayHandles
nodes.summary: Handle bundle for the launched public overlay lane: the overlay server child, the tunnel child, and the resolved public URL.

nodes.name: run_preflight
nodes.summary: Runs the ordered pre-flight item set, writing each item into the operator-surface projection as it completes via OperatorSurfaceAssembler.update_preflight_item, and returns a PreflightResult gating SPOC spawn.

nodes.name: _default_healthz_item
nodes.summary: Probes the launched HUD server's /healthz, verifying the launch_nonce round-trip so a stale or foreign HUD process cannot pass pre-flight.
