nodes.name: _default_openai_heartbeat
nodes.summary: Phase-02 live OpenAI heartbeat: a real minimal API call proving key validity and reachability before the walk spends money.

nodes.name: _default_gamma_heartbeat
nodes.summary: Phase-02 live Gamma API heartbeat verifying the key and endpoint are reachable before deck-producing nodes run.

nodes.name: _status_aware_teardown
nodes.summary: atexit teardown that reads run status and leaves the HUD child alive at a gate pause, tearing it down with grace only on terminal status or explicit operator stop.

nodes.name: request_hud_stop
nodes.summary: Writes the explicit operator stop marker into the run dir so the status-aware teardown tears the HUD child down on next exit.

nodes.name: launch_hud_server
nodes.summary: Launches the GET-only HUD server as a subprocess child with the pinned HUD_* env contract and CREATE_NO_WINDOW on Windows; never raises — returns None on launch failure so the caller records a pre-flight FAIL.

nodes.name: probe_hud_identity
nodes.summary: Probes a port's /healthz for trial identity and launch nonce to decide whether an already-listening HUD process belongs to this run.

nodes.name: reattach_or_launch_hud_server
nodes.summary: Reattaches to a surviving HUD child when the identity probe matches this trial, otherwise launches a fresh HUD server — the resume-path counterpart to launch_hud_server.

nodes.name: tunnel_argv
nodes.summary: Builds the tunnel-provider command line (per PublicOverlayConfig provider) that exposes the public overlay port.

nodes.name: public_overlay_url
nodes.summary: Resolves the public URL the operator uses to reach the overlay through the configured tunnel provider.

nodes.name: launch_public_overlay
nodes.summary: Launches the public overlay lane: spawns the allowlist overlay server child and the tunnel child with per-child logfiles, returning PublicOverlayHandles or None on any failure (never raises).

nodes.name: operator_surface.py
nodes.summary: The operator-surface projection contract: the strict Pydantic producer model (extra=forbid, lifecycle invariants), the lenient consumer reader that never raises, contract-owned event derivation (derive_event_transitions, stall_condition), and the HudConfig single config owner.
nodes.languageNotes: Layer rule enforced: imports nothing from app.marcus, app.hud, or app.notify — pure Pydantic + stdlib + PyYAML; producers strict-serialize while consumers strip-then-validate so additive schema evolution never white-screens the HUD.

nodes.name: OperatorSurfaceProjection
nodes.summary: The strict producer model for the whole projection document (byte-pin source, extra=forbid) with per-lifecycle presence rules enforced witness-by-default and strict-via-context.

nodes.name: HudConfig
nodes.summary: Single owner of HUD/notifier configuration (ports, poll intervals, notification rules, stall budget) backed by HUD_CONFIG_DEFAULTS.

nodes.name: StepsSection
nodes.summary: Projection section carrying the ordered walk-step entries with per-step status, pause markers, and summaries rendered as the HUD walk map.

nodes.name: HealthTile
nodes.summary: One health tile with its current reading and a trimmed bounded history of prior HealthReading values.

nodes.name: TraceSection
nodes.summary: Bounded ring of TraceEvent entries (trimmed on validation) giving the HUD its recent-activity feed.

nodes.name: DecisionCardSection
nodes.summary: Gate-pause decision card: operator prompt, drafted proposal, pick context, and evidence references shown in the HUD gate panel.

nodes.name: Unrecognized
nodes.summary: Typed sentinel returned by the lenient reader for unknown schema_version, unknown status, or garbage input — consumers branch on it instead of catching exceptions.

nodes.name: load_hud_config
nodes.summary: Loads and validates state/config/hud-config.yaml into HudConfig, falling back to HUD_CONFIG_DEFAULTS for missing or invalid sections.

nodes.name: read_operator_surface_lenient
nodes.summary: The consumer parse surface: strips unknown added fields then validates, returning a typed Unrecognized on unknown schema_version/status or garbage — never raises (AD-4).

nodes.name: derive_event_transitions
nodes.summary: Contract-owned pure derivation of notification event classes from a (prev, cur) projection pair so notifier and view can never drift; run_stalled is deliberately excluded (watchdog-owned).

nodes.name: stall_condition
nodes.summary: Watchdog predicate: true when the current projection has exceeded the stall budget without progress, the companion to derive_event_transitions for the run_stalled class.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Notifier package barrel: re-exports the service surface (NotifierService, push URL validation, defaults) for the push + watchdog lane that runs as its own OS process and consumes only the projection contract.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: Standalone notifier process entry (python -m app.notify) plus launch_notifier, which detaches the child (DETACHED_PROCESS/CREATE_NEW_PROCESS_GROUP on Windows, new session on POSIX) so the stall watchdog survives the launching shell and runtime session.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately a separate OS process so the watchdog cannot die with what it guards; stdout/stderr redirect to a per-trial logfile for detached debuggability.

nodes.name: run_forever
nodes.summary: Main poll loop: calls NotifierService.poll_once on the poll interval until the run reaches terminal status plus a grace window, with injectable clocks for deterministic testing.

nodes.name: _detached_popen_kwargs
nodes.summary: Builds the platform-specific Popen kwargs (Windows detach flags vs POSIX start_new_session) plus logfile redirection for the detached notifier child.

nodes.name: launch_notifier
nodes.summary: Launches the notifier as a detached child process bound to a trial and run dir, passing the producer PID so the watchdog can detect a dead producer.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry: parses arguments, constructs the NotifierService, and runs the poll loop until terminal status.

nodes.name: service.py
nodes.summary: Notifier service: polls operator-surface.json mtime-gated, lenient-parses it, derives event classes only through the contract, runs the stall watchdog (projection monotonicity + producer-dead reading), and pushes via Apprise — every failure is logged and swallowed, never raised.
nodes.languageNotes: Channel split per AD-9: this lane owns push (Pushover primary, ntfy fallback, env-only credentials); on-HUD toast and sound belong to the HUD page.

nodes.name: NotifierState
nodes.summary: Persisted notifier state (last seen seq/status, pause episode, outcome ledger, failed-push retry queue) serialized to and from dict for crash-safe resumption.

nodes.name: NotificationEvent
nodes.summary: One derived notification occurrence: event class, composed title/body, ack key, and channel disposition.

nodes.name: NotifierService
nodes.summary: The poll-once engine: reads the projection, derives transitions via the contract, evaluates the stall watchdog, deduplicates by ack key, pushes through Apprise with retry of failed pushes, and persists state — all fault-tolerant by construction.

nodes.name: validate_push_urls
nodes.summary: Validates Apprise push URL strings against the allowed scheme set (Pushover, ntfy), rejecting anything outside the supported transports.

nodes.name: _default_pid_alive
nodes.summary: Cross-platform producer-liveness probe used by the watchdog to distinguish a stalled run from a dead producer process.

nodes.name: _compose
nodes.summary: Composes the push title and body for an event class from the current projection (node, status, reason), keeping message text out of the transport layer.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the LLM batch-execution subsystem, re-exporting the LiteLLM batch adapter, error types, JSONL encoding, output joining, and receipt persistence into a single public surface.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel using explicit re-imports so callers can import from app.runtime.llm_batch directly.

nodes.name: adapter.py
nodes.summary: LiteLLM-backed adapter for provider batch APIs: uploads batch JSONL files, creates/retrieves/cancels batches, downloads outputs, and orchestrates the submit-and-receipt plus join-completed-output flows.
nodes.languageNotes: Guards against accidental use of chat-completion callables by checking function identity at construction, and normalizes heterogeneous SDK file/batch objects.

nodes.name: cost_report.py
nodes.summary: Builds and persists per-batch cost reports by extracting token usage from joined output rows, pricing them via the cascade pricing table, and comparing against realtime-equivalent cost; includes a fail-soft emitter so reporting never breaks a run.

nodes.name: errors.py
nodes.summary: Error taxonomy for the batch subsystem: a general LlmBatchError and the control-flow WaitingForProviderBatchError that carries receipt context when a run pauses to await a provider-side batch.

nodes.name: join.py
nodes.summary: Joins provider batch output rows back to their submitted requests by custom_id, classifying per-row success/failure and detecting missing, duplicate, or unexpected ids; also parses raw output JSONL text into row dicts.
nodes.languageNotes: Frozen dataclasses (JoinedBatchRow, JoinResult) give an immutable join result with derived ok_ids/failed_ids properties.

nodes.name: jsonl.py
nodes.summary: Encodes batch request rows into provider-ready JSONL with byte-size budget enforcement, and provides the /v1/chat/completions row constructor used for batch submissions.

nodes.name: prompt_cache.py
nodes.summary: Derives stable prompt-cache keys from configured strategies, resolves the vision node's cache key per execution mode, builds the extra_body payload carrying the key, and extracts cached-token counts from usage blocks.

nodes.name: receipts.py
nodes.summary: Defines the BatchReceipt Pydantic model and its durable read/write persistence under the run directory, plus normalization of heterogeneous provider batch objects into receipts for resumable batch runs.
nodes.languageNotes: Receipt files are the resume anchor for paused batch runs: normalize_batch_object tolerates both dict-shaped and attribute-shaped SDK batch objects.

nodes.name: llm_batch_eligibility.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic model and YAML loader for the batch-eligibility matrix that governs which call sites may route through provider batch APIs, with validators enforcing that vision-perception is v1-routable.
nodes.languageNotes: Model validators encode governance invariants (v1_routed implies batch_eligible; the vision site must exist and be routable) so a bad YAML fails loudly at load time.

nodes.name: llm_execution_config.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic config family and YAML loader describing per-node LLM execution: realtime vs batch transport profiles (provider, model, token limits, prompt-cache strategy) with per-node default-mode resolution.

nodes.name: batch_route.py
nodes.summary: Batch execution route for vision slide perception: builds per-slide chat-completion batch rows, submits them via the LiteLLM adapter, pauses or polls until the provider batch is terminal, then joins outputs, emits a cost report, and parses each slide's perception response.
nodes.languageNotes: Supports two wait policies — raise-and-pause (WaitingForProviderBatchError carries the receipt for later resume) and in-process polling — with receipts making the route idempotent across resumes.

nodes.name: LiteLlmBatchAdapter
nodes.summary: Adapter wrapping LiteLLM batch primitives (create_file, create_batch, retrieve_batch, cancel, file_content) with high-level submit_and_receipt and join_completed_output operations that produce receipts and joined results.

nodes.name: _coerce_file_bytes
nodes.summary: Normalizes the many shapes a LiteLLM file-content response can take (bytes, str, .content attribute, .read() streams) into raw bytes, raising LlmBatchError on unrecognized types.

nodes.name: _usage_from_row
nodes.summary: Extracts prompt/completion/cached token counts from a joined batch row's nested response body usage block, returning None when the shape is absent or malformed.

nodes.name: build_batch_cost_report
nodes.summary: Aggregates token usage across joined rows, prices batch vs realtime cost via the pricing table, computes savings and turnaround time from receipt timestamps, and assembles the full cost-report dict with row counts and notes.

nodes.name: write_batch_cost_report
nodes.summary: Persists a batch cost report as JSON under the run's llm_batch directory, creating directories as needed and returning the written path.

nodes.name: emit_batch_cost_report_fail_soft
nodes.summary: Builds and writes the batch cost report but swallows any exception, returning None so cost reporting can never fail a production run.

nodes.name: LlmBatchError
nodes.summary: Base runtime error for the LLM batch subsystem, carrying an optional batch_id alongside the message.

nodes.name: WaitingForProviderBatchError
nodes.summary: Control-flow exception raised when a submitted provider batch is still in flight; carries run_id, batch_id, status, and the receipt path so the orchestrator can pause the walk and resume later.

nodes.name: JoinedBatchRow
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass representing one joined batch output row: custom_id, ok flag, response body, error payload, and the raw row dict.

nodes.name: JoinResult
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding the by-custom_id join map, observed order, and missing/unexpected id sets, with derived ok_ids and failed_ids properties.

nodes.name: _row_ok
nodes.summary: Classifies a raw batch output row as success or failure by checking the error field and the HTTP status code inside the response envelope.

nodes.name: join_output_rows
nodes.summary: Joins raw output rows to expected custom_ids, raising on blank or duplicate ids, and returns a JoinResult recording per-row outcomes plus missing and unexpected ids.

nodes.name: parse_output_jsonl
nodes.summary: Decodes bytes or text output JSONL into a list of row dicts, skipping blank lines and non-dict entries.

nodes.name: estimate_jsonl_bytes
nodes.summary: Estimates the encoded byte size of a set of batch rows by summing compact-JSON line lengths plus newlines.

nodes.name: assert_within_size_budget
nodes.summary: Raises LlmBatchError when the estimated JSONL payload exceeds the provider's maximum upload size budget.

nodes.name: encode_batch_jsonl
nodes.summary: Encodes batch rows into newline-delimited UTF-8 JSONL bytes after enforcing the size budget.

nodes.name: make_chat_completions_row
nodes.summary: Constructs a single /v1/chat/completions batch request row with custom_id, model, messages, optional token/temperature limits, and extra_body merge (e.g., prompt-cache key).

nodes.name: derive_prompt_cache_key
nodes.summary: Maps a configured prompt-cache-key strategy (or literal key) plus node name to the concrete cache key string, returning None when caching is disabled.

nodes.name: resolve_vision_prompt_cache_key
nodes.summary: Loads the LLM execution config, resolves the vision-perception node's transport profile for the requested mode, and derives its prompt-cache key from the configured strategy.

nodes.name: prompt_cache_extra_body
nodes.summary: Wraps a cache key into the extra_body dict shape expected by chat-completion requests, or returns None when no key applies.

nodes.name: extract_cached_tokens
nodes.summary: Reads cached_tokens from a usage block, preferring prompt_tokens_details and falling back to a top-level cached_tokens field.

nodes.name: BatchReceipt
nodes.summary: Pydantic model capturing everything needed to resume a provider batch: batch/file ids, status, endpoint, provider, model, completion window, timestamps, row count, request counts, and metadata.

nodes.name: receipt_dir
nodes.summary: Returns the canonical llm_batch receipt directory for a run under the runs root.

nodes.name: write_receipt
nodes.summary: Serializes a BatchReceipt to batch_receipt.json in the run's receipt directory, creating directories as needed.

nodes.name: read_receipt
nodes.summary: Loads and validates a stored BatchReceipt from disk, returning None when no receipt exists for the run.

nodes.name: utc_now_iso
nodes.summary: Returns the current UTC time as a second-precision ISO-8601 string with a Z suffix, used to stamp receipt timestamps.

nodes.name: normalize_batch_object
nodes.summary: Converts a provider batch object (dict- or attribute-shaped) into a BatchReceipt, tolerating missing fields and normalizing request counts and timestamps.

nodes.name: EligibilitySite
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for one batch-eligibility call site (batch_eligible, v1_routed, criteria_met, rationale) with a validator enforcing that v1 routing implies batch eligibility.

nodes.name: BatchEligibilityMatrix
nodes.summary: Top-level eligibility matrix keyed by site name with lookup helpers (site, is_batch_eligible, is_v1_batch_routable, v1_routable_sites) and a validator requiring the vision-perception site to be v1-routable.

nodes.name: load_batch_eligibility
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the batch-eligibility YAML into a BatchEligibilityMatrix, failing loudly on non-mapping content.

nodes.name: NodeTransportProfile
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for a single transport profile: provider, model, max completion tokens, reasoning/verbosity knobs, prompt-cache strategy and retention, and batch fallback metadata.

nodes.name: NodeExecutionProfile
nodes.summary: Per-node execution profile pairing realtime and batch transport profiles with a default mode, and profile_for() selection that raises when the requested mode has no profile.

nodes.name: LlmExecutionConfig
nodes.summary: Root execution config mapping node names to NodeExecutionProfile, with a validator requiring a well-formed vision-perception node and resolve_profile() to pick the effective transport for a node and mode.

nodes.name: load_llm_execution
nodes.summary: Loads and validates the llm-execution YAML config into an LlmExecutionConfig, failing loudly on non-mapping content.

nodes.name: resolve_vision_execution_mode
nodes.summary: Reads the llm_execution_mode override from a specialist payload, defaulting to realtime when absent or blank.

nodes.name: _assert_unique_slide_ids
nodes.summary: Validates that every slide has a non-blank, unique slide_id before batch row construction, raising VisionProviderError on violations.

nodes.name: build_vision_batch_rows
nodes.summary: Builds one chat-completions batch row per slide using the shared perception message builder, deterministic run/slide custom_ids, and the resolved prompt-cache extra_body.

nodes.name: extract_assistant_text
nodes.summary: Walks a joined row's response envelope (response → body → choices → message) to extract the assistant's text content, raising VisionProviderError at each malformed layer.

nodes.name: poll_until_terminal
nodes.summary: Polls the adapter for batch status on an interval until a terminal state or timeout, normalizing each poll into a receipt and raising LlmBatchError when time runs out.

nodes.name: run_vision_batch_perception
nodes.summary: End-to-end batch perception driver: enforces eligibility, resumes from a stored receipt or submits new rows, pauses (raise wait policy) or polls to completion, joins outputs, emits the fail-soft cost report, and parses per-slide perception responses in slide order.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the audit-chain integrity helpers, re-exporting verify_audit_chain and the three audit-chain exception classes as the app.audit public surface.

nodes.name: chain.py
nodes.summary: Pure integrity-check logic for TripwireLedgerEntry rows: verifies per-tripwire monotonic fired_at ordering and requires trace_id linkage on fired/marginal-fired verdicts, raising typed audit-chain errors on violation.
nodes.languageNotes: Side-effect-free verification function raising domain-specific exceptions rather than returning a status, keeping fail-loud semantics for the FR-7c-50 audit-chain scaffold.

nodes.name: errors.py
nodes.summary: Defines the audit-chain integrity exception hierarchy (FR-7c-50): a root AuditChainIntegrityError with AuditChainOrderError and AuditChainParentLinkError subclasses for ordering and trace-linkage violations.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for app.models, re-exporting the tripwire-ledger schema types as the package's public surface.

nodes.name: tripwire_ledger.py
nodes.summary: Pydantic-v2 schema for Slab 7c tripwire-ledger rows (Story 7c.0a, NFR-7c-OD2): closed TripwireId and TripwireSeverity enums plus the strictly-validated TripwireLedgerEntry model consumed by sprint-status tripwire_events writers and the audit-chain integrity checks.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses the triple-layer closed-enum idiom: a Literal type, a StrEnum, and a TypeAdapter-backed field validator that rejects unknown tripwire IDs, plus shared tz-aware datetime and UUID4 validators from app/models/state/_base.py.

nodes.name: verify_audit_chain
nodes.summary: Validates a list of TripwireLedgerEntry rows: enforces strictly increasing fired_at per tripwire_id and raises when a fired or marginal-fired entry lacks a trace_id.

nodes.name: AuditChainIntegrityError
nodes.summary: Root exception for all audit-chain integrity violations (FR-7c-50), allowing callers to catch every audit-chain failure with a single except clause.

nodes.name: AuditChainOrderError
nodes.summary: Raised when a ledger entry's fired_at timestamp is not strictly monotonic for its tripwire_id.

nodes.name: AuditChainParentLinkError
nodes.summary: Raised when a fired or marginal-fired ledger entry is missing its parent trace_id linkage.

nodes.name: TripwireId
nodes.summary: Closed StrEnum of the six Slab 7c tripwire identifiers (TW-7c-1 through TW-7c-6).

nodes.name: TripwireSeverity
nodes.summary: Closed StrEnum of severity tiers (critical, high, medium) for tripwire fires.

nodes.name: TripwireLedgerEntry
nodes.summary: Strict Pydantic model for one tripwire-ledger row, carrying tripwire_id, owner, fired_at, verdict, measured value, escalation action, severity, and trace_id, with field validators rejecting unknown tripwire IDs and enforcing tz-aware timestamps and UUID4 trace IDs.

nodes.name: _shared.py
nodes.summary: Shared foundation for all Marcus PR-* production-readiness capabilities: defines the pinned invocation/return envelope dataclass contracts (Story 26-6 AC-C.1/C.2) and the dual-mode (summarize|execute) CLI harness every capability script runs through.
nodes.languageNotes: Frozen dataclasses pin the envelope contract; capability-level failures exit 0 with errors in the envelope, reserving non-zero exit for contract violations.

nodes.name: stub_envelope
nodes.summary: Builds a canonical NOT_YET_IMPLEMENTED ReturnEnvelope for capability stubs, so unimplemented PR-* codes still answer with an operator-observable, contract-conformant envelope.

nodes.name: run_cli
nodes.summary: Generic CLI entry harness: parses --mode/--args/--run-id flags, constructs the Invocation envelope, dispatches to the capability's summarize or execute function, injects duration telemetry, and prints the JSON return envelope to stdout.

nodes.name: InvocationContext
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass carrying the optional context Marcus passes to a capability: run_id, bundle_path, and invoked_by.

nodes.name: Invocation
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass for the 6-field Marcus-to-capability invocation envelope: capability_code, mode, args, optional context, and idempotency_key.

nodes.name: LandingPoint
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass describing where a capability's output landed on disk: bundle_path, manifest entry, and content sha256.

nodes.name: CapabilityError
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass for a structured capability-level error entry: machine code, human message, and remediation hint carried inside the return envelope.

nodes.name: ReturnEnvelope
nodes.summary: The 7-field capability-to-Marcus return envelope (status, capability_code, run_id, result, landing_point, errors, telemetry) with a to_dict serializer that prunes empty optional fields.

nodes.name: pr_hc.py
nodes.summary: PR-HC (Health Check) capability stub: registered with pinned contracts, returning the canonical NOT_YET_IMPLEMENTED envelope in both summarize and execute modes until story 26-10 implements it.

nodes.name: pr_pf.py
nodes.summary: PR-PF (Preflight) capability, fully implemented: wraps the app_session_readiness --with-preflight subprocess to give Marcus a structured readiness report before firing Prompt 1 on a tracked production run, writing a session-preflight receipt on execute.
nodes.languageNotes: Verbose-posture split: summarize reports the planned command without side effects; execute runs the subprocess with a 120s timeout and maps timeout/JSON-parse/write failures into distinct CapabilityError codes.

nodes.name: summarize
nodes.summary: Reports the preflight plan (command to run, receipt path, flags) as an ok envelope without touching disk or spawning subprocesses.

nodes.name: _resolve_session_receipt_path
nodes.summary: Resolves where the session-preflight receipt should be written: an explicit args override wins, otherwise defaults inside the bundle directory when it exists.

nodes.name: _run_subprocess
nodes.summary: Executes the readiness command via subprocess.run with a 120-second timeout, capturing stdout/stderr for envelope construction.

nodes.name: execute
nodes.summary: Runs the app_session_readiness subprocess for real, parsing its JSON report, mapping timeout / non-zero-exit / parse / receipt-write failures into structured CapabilityError entries, writing the session receipt, and returning an envelope with a LandingPoint.

nodes.name: pr_rc.py
nodes.summary: PR-RC (Run-Constants) capability, fully implemented: Marcus authors run-constants.yaml in the canonical lowercase-nested form the validator expects (author sub-mode) or validates an existing file (validate sub-mode), eliminating the operator hand-transcription schema-drift vector that halted the 2026-04-17 Tejal trial.
nodes.languageNotes: Normalizes UPPERCASE prompt-pack display keys to canonical snake_case via an explicit mapping table, then round-trips the authored YAML through parse_run_constants before writing to guarantee validator conformance.

nodes.name: _normalize_values
nodes.summary: Accepts UPPERCASE prompt-pack-style keys and converts them into the canonical lowercase-nested run-constants form using the _UPPERCASE_TO_SNAKE mapping.

nodes.name: _canonical_ordered
nodes.summary: Orders run-constants values into the canonical required-then-optional field sequence matching the RunConstants dataclass order, sorting any unknown extras deterministically.

nodes.name: summarize
nodes.summary: Dry-run for both sub-modes: normalizes the supplied values, renders the candidate YAML, pre-validates it through parse_run_constants, and reports what author/validate would do without writing anything.

nodes.name: _author
nodes.summary: Author sub-mode: renders canonical YAML from normalized values, validates it via parse_run_constants BEFORE writing, then writes run-constants.yaml to the target path and returns an envelope with sha256-bearing LandingPoint; validation or write failures become structured CapabilityErrors.

nodes.name: _validate
nodes.summary: Validate sub-mode: loads an existing run-constants.yaml and runs it through load_run_constants_dict + parse_run_constants, converting missing-file and schema failures into structured error envelopes.

nodes.name: execute
nodes.summary: Execute-mode dispatcher: routes on args.mode_sub to _validate or (after checking values is a dict) normalizes input and delegates to _author.

nodes.name: pr_rs.py
nodes.summary: PR-RS (Run Selection) capability stub: registered with pinned contracts, returning the canonical NOT_YET_IMPLEMENTED envelope in both summarize and execute modes until story 26-10 implements it.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package initializer for the v4.2 prompt-pack generator, re-exporting the render entry point from the render module.

nodes.name: env.py
nodes.summary: Constructs the Jinja2 Environment used by the v4.2 pack renderer, wiring a FileSystemLoader rooted at the bundled (or overridden) templates directory.
nodes.languageNotes: Accepts an optional template_root override — the seam used by template-removal red-tests.

nodes.name: manifest.py
nodes.summary: Adapts the canonical pipeline manifest into a generator-friendly model for v4.2 pack rendering: projects renderable steps (GeneratorStep/GeneratorManifest), resolves each step's section template with fail-loud MissingSectionTemplateError, and excludes orchestration-only/pack-invisible nodes via the shared is_pack_excluded predicate.
nodes.languageNotes: Frozen dataclasses for immutable projections; deliberately raises instead of fabricating template names (anti-silent-lie pattern documented inline).

nodes.name: render.py
nodes.summary: Renders the v4.2 prompt pack markdown from the pipeline manifest and Jinja2 templates, combining generator steps with workflow policy; also provides the argparse CLI entry point.

nodes.name: make_env
nodes.summary: Builds and returns a Jinja2 Environment with a FileSystemLoader over the template root (default: bundled templates directory).

nodes.name: MissingSectionTemplateError
nodes.summary: RuntimeError raised when a renderable step has no section template, naming the step and expected template path up front instead of letting Jinja crash later with TemplateNotFound.

nodes.name: GeneratorStep
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass projecting a pipeline step into generator-friendly fields: id, label, section anchor, template name, HUD id, module path, audience, and ordering hints.

nodes.name: GeneratorManifest
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding the generator input model: schema/pack versions, generator reference, and the ordered tuple of renderable GeneratorStep entries.

nodes.name: _template_name
nodes.summary: Resolves a step's section template by globbing sections/<step.id>-*.md.j2 under the template root, raising MissingSectionTemplateError when none exists.

nodes.name: _module_path
nodes.summary: Maps a pipeline step id to the owning code/skill module path (specialists, agent skills, orchestrator loop, or run_hud fallback) for pack attribution.

nodes.name: _orchestration_only_ids
nodes.summary: Loads the graph-shaped manifest and returns the frozenset of node ids that are runtime-only (orchestration-only plus folded HIL gate nodes) via the shared is_pack_excluded predicate; legacy steps-shaped manifests yield an empty set.

nodes.name: load_generator_manifest
nodes.summary: Loads the canonical pipeline manifest, drops orchestration-only steps, and shapes the remaining renderable steps into a GeneratorManifest with resolved templates, module paths, and audiences.

nodes.name: render_pack
nodes.summary: Renders the v4.2 pack markdown to the output path: loads the generator manifest and workflow policy, builds the Jinja env, renders layout/pack.md.j2, and writes UTF-8 output with LF newlines.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: parses --manifest/--output arguments and invokes render_pack.

nodes.name: __main__.py
nodes.summary: MCP stdio runtime entry point (`python -m app.mcp_server`) that boots the shared MCP server over stdin/stdout so clients like Claude Desktop or the parity-test subprocess can connect.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses asyncio.run over an async context-managed stdio_server transport; runnable via `python -m` module execution.

nodes.name: protocol.py
nodes.summary: Pins the MCP protocol version for the migration runtime by re-exporting the shipped SDK's DEFAULT_NEGOTIATED_VERSION and LATEST_PROTOCOL_VERSION constants, so an SDK upgrade can never silently shift the protocol surface.
nodes.languageNotes: Re-exports SDK constants under project names rather than hard-coding a date string, keeping parity tests and the server on one upstream source of truth.

nodes.name: server.py
nodes.summary: MCP server scaffold (Slab 1 Story 1.1c) that builds the shared module-level `mcp.server.lowlevel.Server` instance with the `ping` tool registered; the parity smoke asserts byte-equivalence against the FastAPI /invoke transport.
nodes.languageNotes: Tool registration uses a deliberate side-effect import inside the factory so decorator-based registration binds against the concrete server instance.

nodes.name: _serve
nodes.summary: Async coroutine that opens the stdio transport and runs the shared MCP server with its initialization options until the client disconnects.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Synchronous console entry point that drives the async _serve coroutine via asyncio.run.

nodes.name: _build_server
nodes.summary: Factory that constructs the named/versioned MCP Server instance and registers the ping tool via a side-effect import so decorators bind to this concrete server.

nodes.name: load_env.cjs
nodes.summary: Minimal dotenv-style loader that parses the repo-root .env file and populates process.env without overriding existing variables, failing loudly with a pointer to the admin guide when .env is missing.
nodes.languageNotes: CommonJS module (.cjs) so it can be required or imported from both CJS and ESM smoke scripts; avoids a dotenv dependency.

nodes.name: loadEnv
nodes.summary: Reads .env at the repo root, parses KEY=VALUE lines while skipping comments and blanks, sets each key on process.env only if not already set, and returns the resolved .env path; throws if the file is absent.

nodes.name: smoke_elevenlabs.mjs
nodes.summary: Live smoke check for ElevenLabs API access: loads .env credentials, fetches the /v1/voices endpoint with a 15s timeout, and reports voice count plus sample voice names, exiting non-zero on missing key or HTTP failure.
nodes.languageNotes: Top-level await ESM script using native fetch and AbortSignal.timeout — no external HTTP dependencies.

nodes.name: smoke_qualtrics.mjs
nodes.summary: Live smoke check for Qualtrics API access: loads .env credentials, calls the whoami and surveys endpoints via a small authenticated getJson helper, and reports the authenticated user plus sample survey records.
nodes.languageNotes: Top-level await ESM script; getJson centralizes the X-API-TOKEN header and 15s AbortSignal timeout.

nodes.name: getJson
nodes.summary: Fetches a Qualtrics API URL with the X-API-TOKEN header and a 15-second timeout, throwing on non-OK responses and returning the parsed JSON body.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the state-management infrastructure, re-exporting DB_PATH and init_database so callers can initialize the SQLite coordination database without importing db_init directly.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package __init__ with explicit __all__ re-export.

nodes.name: db_init.py
nodes.summary: Defines the SQLite schema for runtime agent-coordination state (production_runs, agent_coordination, quality_gates, observability_events tables) and provides init_database() to create the database idempotently at state/runtime/coordination.db.
nodes.languageNotes: Embeds the full DDL as a SCHEMA_SQL string with CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for idempotent initialization; enables SQLite foreign keys via PRAGMA at connect time.

nodes.name: init_database
nodes.summary: Creates the coordination database directory, opens a SQLite connection, executes the embedded schema DDL idempotently, and returns the resolved database path.

nodes.name: init_state.py
nodes.summary: CLI entry point (python -m scripts.state_management.init_state) that bootstraps state infrastructure: creates the SQLite coordination database, then verifies expected YAML configs under state/config/ and the 18 agent memory directories under _bmad/memory/, reporting any missing entries as errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Hard-codes the roster of 18 BMAD agent memory directories (17 persona sidecars plus the bmad-agent-desmond sanctum) as the verification checklist.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Runs the full state-infrastructure initialization sequence: calls init_database, checks for three expected YAML config files, verifies each agent memory directory entry file exists, and returns a nonzero exit code when anything is missing.

nodes.name: override_event.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Versioned JSON Schema pin for OverrideLedgerEvent — a runtime model-override proto-event recorded in the trial ledger, capturing gate/node identity, operator, previous vs. new model, a required confirm token, and a submitted/applied phase.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic-v2-emitted JSON Schema with additionalProperties:false and a const-pinned kind discriminator ("override"), making the event envelope closed and version-frozen (v1).

nodes.name: sanctum_mutation_event.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Versioned JSON Schema pin for SanctumMutationLedgerEvent — records a detected mutation of a sanctum file during a trial, with file path, before/after content hashes, mutation timestamp, and an optional suggested invalidating commit.
nodes.languageNotes: Closed schema (additionalProperties:false) with const kind discriminator ("sanctum_mutation") and a nullable anyOf field for the suggested invalidating commit.

nodes.name: verdict_event.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Versioned JSON Schema pin for VerdictLedgerEvent — an operator verdict emitted at a gate boundary, tying trial/gate/card/verdict UUIDs to an approve/edit/reject verb and the transport kind that carried the decision.
nodes.languageNotes: Closed schema with const kind discriminator ("verdict") and a closed three-value verb enum (approve/edit/reject) enforcing the gate decision vocabulary.

nodes.name: collateral_spec.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Emitted JSON Schema (v1) for the CollateralSpec model — the DP4 collateral content contract carried on lesson_plan["collateral"], defining WorkbookSpec/WorkbookSection/Exercise/DepthDeltaContract/ResearchEnrichmentGoal with an explicit 'none'/'present' declaration discriminant for deck-only lessons.
nodes.languageNotes: Pydantic-v2-emitted JSON Schema with closed Bloom-level enums (red values rejected at three layers) and additionalProperties:false throughout; DepthDeltaContract is a frozen value object anchoring the deck-vs-workbook dual-coding split.

nodes.name: coverage_manifest.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Emitted JSON Schema (v1) for the CoverageManifest artifact — per-surface coverage records (CoverageSurface) plus a machine-readable CoverageSummary rollup (implemented/pending/deferred counts, plan-ref coverage, freshness-gate verification, trial_ready flag) consumed by the 32-3 smoke harness.

nodes.name: fit_report.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Emitted JSON Schema (v1) for the FitReport artifact (AC-B.9) — the attestor's per-unit FitDiagnosis verdicts (fitness sufficient/partial/absent, recommended scope decision and weather band) anchored to a source_ref and a PlanRef into the lesson plan, with an irene_budget_ms budget field.

nodes.name: learning_objective.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Emitted JSON Schema (v1) for the canonical LearningObjective — a provenance-anchored, lifecycle-aware LO with status-conditional invariants (provisional/refined/ratified), structured SourceRef provenance locators with verbatim quoted spans, and Irene's advisory-only SourceAdequacy assessment (alert, never a gate).
nodes.languageNotes: Encodes governance semantics directly in the schema docs: adequacy verdicts are advisory alerts (ratification 3.1), and 'missing' must name a concrete gap on thin/gap verdicts — object well-formedness distinct from pipeline gating.

nodes.name: lesson_plan.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Emitted JSON Schema (v1) for the root LessonPlan artifact (AC-B.1) — learning model, plan structure and PlanUnits, operator Dials for enrichment/corroboration, ScopeDecision and IdentifiedGap records, revision/digest tracking, and the BlueprintSignoff pointer for the 29-3 Irene + writer sign-off seam.
nodes.languageNotes: Root artifact of the schema family; timezone-aware date-time formats and [0.0, 1.0]-bounded nullable dial floats reflect the Pydantic-v2 schema checklist idioms.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Minimal package note identifying app.models as the shared migration model package and citing its justifying functional requirements (FR17, FR18, FR19, FR25).

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_04_5.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for Section04_5OperatorVerdict — the tamper-evident operator verdict emitted by the Section 04.5 G1.5 estimator surface, with closed verb enum, sha256 decision-card digest, and required identity/timestamp fields.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_04_55.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for Section04_55OperatorVerdict — the tamper-evident operator verdict emitted by the Section 04.55 G1.5 surface, pinning verb vocabulary, decision-card digest, and submission metadata.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_04a.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for Section04AOperatorVerdict — the tamper-evident operator verdict emitted by the Section 04A G1A poll surface, with closed verb enum and sha256 decision-card digest.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_05_5.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for Section05_5OperatorVerdict — the tamper-evident verdict from the Section 05.5 G2B poll surface, carrying PerSlideModePayload (selected per-slide presentation mode plus rationale) and PerSlideModeEditPayload for field-level edits.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_06b.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for Section06BOperatorVerdict — the tamper-evident verdict emitted by the Section 06B build surface; the largest verdict schema in the family, with verb-conditional payload requirements.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_07b.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for Section07BOperatorVerdict — the tamper-evident verdict emitted by the Section 07B G2M poll surface, with closed verb enum and digest-pinned decision-card linkage.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_07c.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for Section07COperatorVerdict — the tamper-evident verdict from the Section 07C storyboard build surface, carrying StoryboardBuildPayload (slide count, HTML artifact path and sha256 digest, target section) under a submit|edit|discard verb set.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_07d.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for Section07DOperatorVerdict — the tamper-evident verdict emitted by the Section 07D G2.5 poll surface, with closed verb enum and sha256 decision-card digest.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_07f.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for Section07FOperatorVerdict — the tamper-evident verdict emitted by the Section 07F G2F poll surface, pinning verb vocabulary and submission metadata.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_08b.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for Section08BOperatorVerdict — the tamper-evident verdict emitted by the Section 08B G3B poll surface, with closed verb enum and sha256 decision-card digest.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_11.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for Section11OperatorVerdict — the tamper-evident verdict from the Section 11 G4A voice-selection surface, carrying VoiceSelectionPayload (selected ElevenLabs voice id plus rationale) under a select|edit|reject verb set.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_11b.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for Section11BOperatorVerdict — the tamper-evident verdict emitted by the Section 11B G4B poll surface, with closed verb enum and sha256 decision-card digest.

nodes.name: operator_verdict_section_15.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for Section15OperatorVerdict — the tamper-evident verdict emitted by the Section 15 G5 poll surface, pinning verb vocabulary, decision-card digest, and submission metadata.

nodes.name: registry.yaml
nodes.summary: Model catalog (Slab 1 Story 1.3) listing the OpenAI models available to cascade resolution — gpt-5 / gpt-5-mini / gpt-5-nano / gpt-5.4 / gpt-5.5 — with per-million-token pricing, context windows, and reasoning/fast/code tier assignments; edits are tier-governed per architecture D13.
nodes.languageNotes: Entries with available=false stay in the catalog for audit but are excluded from cascade resolution; catalog was remediated 2026-04-26 to replace fictitious scaffold model ids with the real OpenAI catalog.

nodes.name: selection_policy.yaml
nodes.summary: Auto-select policy consulted by the model selector at the cascade's auto_select_fallback level: first-matching-rule-wins predicates mapping tier requests (reasoning/fast/code) to preferred tiers and model fallback chains, with a universal default-fallback rule.
nodes.languageNotes: Conflicting rules (same when-predicate, different prefer_tier) raise a named ValidationError at YAML load time rather than silently overriding (AC-1.3-B).

nodes.name: trial3_transcript.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for Trial3Transcript — the append-only transcript envelope for the Trial-3 closeout attempt, pinning the record structure used to capture the trial run.

nodes.name: decision_card_base.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema shape-pin for the base operator-facing DecisionCard gate payload, defining shared $defs such as DecisionCardMeta (cache state, override trail, party-mode contributions, sanctum warnings) and OverrideEvent used by every gate-family card.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON Schema emitted from a Pydantic v2 model with additionalProperties:false and closed enums, serving as the frozen contract for shape-pin tests.

nodes.name: g0.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for the G0Card — the trial-open corpus-confirmation DecisionCard shown at the first gate, embedding the shared DecisionCardMeta, CacheState, and OverrideEvent definitions.

nodes.name: g1.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for the G1Card — the trial-open DecisionCard for the first operator checkpoint, sharing the gate-family metadata and override-event definitions.

nodes.name: g2a.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for the G2ACard — the plan-unit-ratification DecisionCard for the G2A gate family, sharing the closed cache-state vocabulary and override-trail metadata.

nodes.name: g2c.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for the G2CCard — the pre-execution sanity-check DecisionCard, sharing the gate-family DecisionCardMeta and OverrideEvent definitions.

nodes.name: g3.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for the G3Card — the mid-trial DecisionCard for in-flight operator review, sharing the gate-family metadata and override-event definitions.

nodes.name: g4.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for the G4Card — the post-trial closeout DecisionCard, sharing the gate-family DecisionCardMeta, CacheState, and OverrideEvent definitions.

nodes.name: g5.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for the G5Card — the final-operator-handoff DecisionCard for the G5 gate family, sharing the gate-family metadata and override-event definitions.

nodes.name: g6.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Frozen v1 JSON Schema for the G6Card — the slab-close ceremony DecisionCard, sharing the gate-family DecisionCardMeta, CacheState, and OverrideEvent definitions.

nodes.name: decision_cards.schema.json
nodes.summary: Emitted JSON Schema pin for the VocabularyDecisionCard: the minimal closed-enum vocabulary card (Story 7a.6) with GateDecisionToken (confirm/revise/reject/escape/skip-slide/abort-run) and GateDirectiveToken (accept-as-is/apply-edit/re-emit/halt-for-repair) enums plus a mandatory rationale.
nodes.languageNotes: Shape-pin schema with additionalProperties:false and closed enums, enforcing triple-layer red-rejection on the gate-decision vocabulary.

nodes.name: operator-surface.v1.schema.json
nodes.summary: Shape pin for the v1 OperatorSurfaceProjection (AD-1 strict producer model): 24 section definitions (envelope, steps, specialists, decision card, deliverables, health, preflight, trace, notifications echo, HUD config, etc.) with AD-15 lifecycle-presence rules governing which sections are mandatory at each run stage.
nodes.languageNotes: Large composed JSON Schema — 24 $defs sub-models feeding one projection object; monotonic seq/progress_seq and envelope_digest fields make the surface tamper-evident and resumable.

nodes.name: operator_verdict.schema.json
nodes.summary: Shape pin for OperatorVerdict — the frozen, tamper-evident receipt of one HIL gate decision, binding a verb (approve/edit/reject/select) to trial, gate, card, operator identity, and a sha256 decision-card digest, with a max-3 revise_count guard (Story 7a.4).
nodes.languageNotes: Verb-conditional payload fields (edit_payload iff verb=='edit', reject_reason iff verb=='reject'); 'select' is the BETA picker verb (surgical overlay) vs 'edit' full-replace per party-ratified Option B.

nodes.name: specialist_state.schema.json
nodes.summary: Shape pin for SpecialistState — the persisted per-specialist snapshot for adjacent-gate pickup, covering the closed SpecialistId roster (texas, irene, dan, tracy, gary, kira, wanda, enrique, compositor, quinn_r, vera), run status, artifact/summary paths, cache-prefix identity, and the ModelResolutionEntry cascade-audit trail (NFR-X4).
nodes.languageNotes: Carries a const schema_version ('specialist-state.v1') and a 64-hex-char regex-pinned cache_prefix_hash; the model_resolution_trail records per_call → per_specialist → registry_default → auto_select_fallback cascade resolution.

nodes.name: config.yaml
nodes.summary: Runtime guardrail and voice-roster configuration for the Enrique TTS specialist: rate limit, daily budget, per-invocation cost cap, and a default candidate roster of four ElevenLabs voices with sample-audio URLs and characteristic metadata.

nodes.name: model_config.yaml
nodes.summary: Per-specialist LLM model selection for Enrique: default model gpt-5 with a per-node override for the act node and temperature 0.0, sitting at Level 2 of the three-level override chain (RunState override > specialist default > registry default).
nodes.languageNotes: Comments document a three-level model-override precedence: per-call RunState.model_overrides, this per-specialist default, then app/models/registry.py.

nodes.name: voice_direction_map.yaml
nodes.summary: Governance-controlled directed-voice mapping table (voice-direction-map.v1) translating control-card pace/energy/emotional_tone spellings into bounded ElevenLabs TTS settings (speed, stability, style); loaded once at import into a frozen constant and pinned by a shape-pin test so values cannot drift silently.
nodes.languageNotes: Header declares edits are governance, not refactor: any value change must bump map_version and stay within ElevenLabsSettings bounds (stability/style in [0,1], speed in [0.7,1.2]); energy overrides tone on shared stability/style fields.

nodes.name: first_clone_bootstrap.ps1
nodes.summary: One-command Windows/PowerShell bootstrap for a fresh clone: verifies Python 3.11+ and git, creates .venv, pip-installs dev dependencies, stubs .env from .env.example, installs pre-commit hooks, optionally pings Postgres, and finishes with a GREEN/YELLOW/RED verdict from trial_run_preflight.py.
nodes.languageNotes: Linear 7-step script with -SkipPostgres/-SkipPreCommit switches; uses an inline Python here-string for the psycopg Postgres reachability ping and propagates the preflight exit code as its own.

nodes.name: first_clone_bootstrap.sh
nodes.summary: Linux/macOS counterpart of the fresh-clone bootstrap: same 7-step sequence (Python/git check, .venv creation, pip install, .env stub, pre-commit hooks, optional Postgres ping, trial_run_preflight.py verdict) driven by --skip-postgres/--skip-pre-commit flags.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses set -euo pipefail with an explicit '|| true' escape on the non-fatal Postgres ping; mirrors the PowerShell variant step-for-step so both platforms share one documented workflow.

nodes.name: ready_for_trial.ps1
nodes.summary: Windows pre-flight harness that gates the first real trial run: executes 9 checks (preflight, migration health dashboard, M5 pre-vote audit, cascade-config validation, model-id guard tests, required API-key presence, ruff, lint-imports, migration test suite) plus a GCM account-picker neutralization pre-step, then prints a READY FOR TRIAL / BLOCKED banner.
nodes.languageNotes: Central Invoke-Step helper wraps each check with scriptblock execution, LASTEXITCODE capture, and result accumulation into a typed list; exits 0 only when every step passes.

nodes.name: ready_for_trial.sh
nodes.summary: Unix pre-flight harness mirroring ready_for_trial.ps1: runs the same 9 trial-readiness checks (preflight, health dashboard, M5 audit, cascade-config validate, guard tests, key presence, ruff, lint-imports, migration tests) via a run_step wrapper and exits 0 only when all pass.
nodes.languageNotes: Required-key check runs an embedded Python heredoc that loads env via scripts.utilities.env_loader before asserting OPENAI_API_KEY and LANGSMITH_API_KEY are present.

nodes.name: Invoke-Step
nodes.summary: Step-runner helper that executes a check scriptblock with strict error handling, captures output and exit code (defaulting null to 0), records the result in the shared results list, and prints a per-step PASS/FAIL line.

nodes.name: run_required_key_check
nodes.summary: Runs an embedded Python heredoc that loads the project's env_loader (tolerating its absence) and asserts OPENAI_API_KEY and LANGSMITH_API_KEY are present in the environment.

nodes.name: run_step
nodes.summary: Generic step-runner that executes a numbered/named command, records its exit code into STEP_RESULTS, prints PASS/FAIL, and remembers the first failed step for the final BLOCKED banner.

nodes.name: BrowserOpenError

nodes.name: GateAdvanceBlockedError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when a gate attempts to advance without a recorded browser-open log for the review pack.

nodes.name: ReviewPackRow
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass representing one per-slide row in a review pack (slide_index, slide_label, preview, output), with an output_digest property computing a sha256 hash of the row's output.

nodes.summary: Writes the per-slide HTML review pack file for a trial/gate to disk, archiving any prior version first.

nodes.name: open_review_pack
nodes.summary: Opens the review pack file in the operator's browser and writes a timestamped browser-open log entry used to satisfy the gate-advance guard.

nodes.name: assert_gate_advance_allowed
nodes.summary: Raises GateAdvanceBlockedError unless the browser-open log for the given gate exists and is non-empty, gating advancement past the review pack.

nodes.name: _render_review_pack
nodes.summary: Private helper that renders the full HTML document (head plus per-slide row table) for a review pack.

nodes.name: _render_row
nodes.summary: Private helper that renders one HTML table row (slide preview plus radio-button decision inputs) for the review pack.

nodes.name: Cp1252FixtureComparisonVerdict
nodes.summary: Dataclass holding the verdict of a cp1252 fixture byte comparison: equivalence flag, byte counts for each side, and the first divergence offset with surrounding context.

nodes.name: compare_fixture_bytes
nodes.summary: Reads two fixture files as bytes, normalizes newlines, and returns a Cp1252FixtureComparisonVerdict describing whether they are byte-equivalent and where they first diverge.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses expected/actual fixture file paths and prints the comparison verdict as JSON.

nodes.name: render_glossary
nodes.summary: Renders the vocabulary glossary as markdown text from the parsed registry dict, iterating namespaces and token groups.

nodes.name: emit_glossary
nodes.summary: Loads the vocabulary registry YAML and writes the rendered glossary markdown to the configured output path.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point invoking emit_glossary with its default registry and output paths.

nodes.summary: Records a Gate 2 (G2C) approval learning event and appends it to the run's ledger.

nodes.summary: Records a Gate 3 (G3) revision learning event and appends it to the run's ledger.

nodes.name: record_gate_4_decision
nodes.summary: Records a Gate 4 (G4) waiver learning event and appends it to the run's ledger.

nodes.name: StubDialsAffordance

nodes.name: registry_validators.py
nodes.summary: Placeholder lockstep slot for PipelineRegistry cross-field validators; the default-model-must-match-an-available-entry invariant currently lives inline in the model and would be lifted here if provider-specific availability rules grow more complex.
nodes.languageNotes: NFR-M5 four-file-lockstep pattern: this file exists purely as a documented extension point, not active logic.

nodes.name: selection_policy_validators.py
nodes.summary: Placeholder lockstep slot for ModelSelectionPolicy cross-field validators; the 'no silent conflicts' invariant currently lives inline in the model and would be lifted here for future per-context-key conflict matrix logic.
nodes.languageNotes: NFR-M5 four-file-lockstep pattern: documented extension point, not active logic.

nodes.name: _flake_rate.py
nodes.summary: Defines the per-cell-class flake-rate budget contract used by test-parity gating: computes allowed flake budgets by CI cell class and evaluates whether a cell's observed failure rate stays within that budget.

nodes.name: budget_for_cell_class
nodes.summary: Looks up the allowed flake-rate budget for a given CI cell class.

nodes.name: evaluate_cell_flake_budget
nodes.summary: Evaluates a CellFlakeInput against its class budget and returns a CellFlakeVerdict describing whether the observed flake rate is within budget.

nodes.name: CellFlakeInput
nodes.summary: Pydantic input model capturing per-cell test run totals and failure counts, with a cross-field validator ensuring failed runs never exceed total runs.

nodes.name: CellFlakeVerdict
nodes.summary: Pydantic output model reporting the computed flake rate, budget, and within-budget verdict for a test cell.

nodes.name: tw_7c_3_firing.py
nodes.summary: Implements the pipeline-manifest four-file-lockstep check contract (LOCKSTEP_CHECK): verifies that all four expected artifact files exist for a given gate_id and reports which are missing.

nodes.name: _gate_id_lower
nodes.summary: Normalizes a gate_id to a lowercase, hyphen-safe token used to build glob paths.

nodes.name: _paths_for
nodes.summary: Resolves the four expected file glob paths for a gate_id relative to the repo root.

nodes.name: LOCKSTEP_CHECK
nodes.summary: Entry point that checks presence of all four lockstep files for a gate_id and returns a LockstepResult with a failure_reason when any file is missing.

nodes.name: LockstepResult
nodes.summary: Result model reporting per-file presence, whether all four lockstep files are present, and an optional failure reason.

nodes.name: parity_comparison.py
nodes.summary: CLI/library that compares production-substrate artifacts (course/module context, asset specs, motion plan) between a primary bundle and a clone replay run, computing tier1/tier2 structural parity scores and rendering a markdown parity evidence report.

nodes.name: _load_mapping
nodes.summary: Loads a JSON or YAML file into a dict mapping, raising ValueError if the content isn't a mapping.

nodes.name: _expect_string
nodes.summary: Fetches a required string field from a mapping, raising ValueError if missing or empty.

nodes.name: _parse_lesson_slug
nodes.summary: Parses a lesson slug into normalized course/module/lesson components via regex.

nodes.name: _normalize_scalar
nodes.summary: Normalizes a scalar value to a comparable string by stripping run IDs, UUIDs, and timestamps so semantic comparisons ignore run-specific noise.

nodes.name: _flatten_mapping
nodes.summary: Recursively flattens a nested mapping/list into sorted, normalized key=value comparison lines.

nodes.name: _compare_semantic_lines
nodes.summary: Compares flattened primary and clone mappings, producing a structural match score and divergence rationale for mismatched lines.

nodes.name: _canonical_course_context_from_run_constants
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes course context fields from a run-constants artifact for parity comparison.

nodes.name: _canonical_course_context
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes course context fields from the primary bundle's course-context artifact for parity comparison.

nodes.name: _canonical_module_context_from_run_constants
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes module context fields from a run-constants artifact for parity comparison.

nodes.name: _canonical_module_context
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes module context fields from the primary bundle's module-context artifact for parity comparison.

nodes.name: _canonical_asset_specs_from_gary_outbound
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes asset-spec fields from Gary's outbound artifact for parity comparison.

nodes.name: _canonical_asset_specs
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes asset-spec fields from the primary bundle's asset-specs artifact for parity comparison.

nodes.name: _canonical_motion_plan
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes motion-plan fields including motion budget sub-fields for parity comparison.

nodes.name: compare_actual_substrate_parity
nodes.summary: Top-level parity comparison entry point: walks all registered ArtifactParitySpecs, compares primary bundle vs clone run artifacts, and assembles a ParityComparisonReport with tier1/tier2 scores.

nodes.name: render_parity_evidence_markdown
nodes.summary: Renders a ParityComparisonReport into a human-readable markdown evidence document.

nodes.name: write_parity_evidence_markdown
nodes.summary: Writes the rendered parity evidence markdown report to disk.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses arguments, runs compare_actual_substrate_parity, and writes the evidence markdown report.

nodes.name: ArtifactParitySpec
nodes.summary: Declarative spec describing one comparable artifact pair (primary vs clone relative paths and canonicalizer functions).

nodes.name: ArtifactParityResult
nodes.summary: Result model for a single artifact's parity comparison, including presence flags, structural match score, and tier1/tier2 pass helper properties.

nodes.name: ParityComparisonReport
nodes.summary: Aggregate report model bundling all artifact parity results with overall tier1/tier2 scores, thresholds, and notes.

nodes.summary: Resolves the Postgres database URL from environment variables, raising CheckpointerConfigError if unset.

nodes.name: resolve_checkpointer_conninfo
nodes.summary: Builds a psycopg conninfo string from the resolved database URL.

nodes.name: make_checkpointer
nodes.summary: Constructs and sets up an AsyncPostgresSaver checkpointer using the resolved connection info.

nodes.name: CheckpointerConfigError
nodes.summary: Exception raised when checkpointer database configuration is missing or invalid.

nodes.name: cleanup_threads.py
nodes.summary: CLI script that deletes stale LangGraph checkpoint threads from Postgres according to a RetentionPolicy, supporting dry-run and live-delete modes.

nodes.name: _threads_to_delete
nodes.summary: Filters candidate threads down to those eligible for deletion under the given retention policy.

nodes.name: cleanup
nodes.summary: Queries stale checkpoint threads from a Postgres connection, applies the retention policy, and deletes matching threads unless dry_run is set, returning a CleanupResult.

nodes.name: _build_parser
nodes.summary: Builds the argparse CLI parser for the cleanup_threads script, including a mutually exclusive dry-run/live-delete mode group.

nodes.name: _print_policy_summary
nodes.summary: Prints a human-readable summary of the active retention policy to stdout.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: parses arguments, loads the retention policy, connects to Postgres, runs cleanup, and prints the result.

nodes.name: CleanupResult
nodes.summary: Result model reporting how many threads were deleted, whether the run was a dry-run, and the retention policy version used.

nodes.name: README.md

nodes.name: retention_policy.py

nodes.name: load_policy
nodes.summary: Loads and validates a RetentionPolicy from a YAML file path, raising FileNotFoundError or ValueError on bad input.

nodes.name: RetentionPolicy
nodes.summary: Pydantic model for thread retention settings (max age, cron hint, retain-completed/failed flags) with a cron-shape enforcing validator.

nodes.name: retry_policy.py
nodes.summary: Defines a retryable validation error and helper functions for validating LangGraph node payloads against Pydantic models with a configurable exponential-backoff RetryPolicy.

nodes.name: validate_for_retry
nodes.summary: Validates a node's payload against a Pydantic model, wrapping ValidationError into a RetryableValidationNodeError so LangGraph's retry machinery can catch and retry it.

nodes.name: pydantic_retry_policy
nodes.summary: Factory that builds a LangGraph RetryPolicy configured with exponential backoff parameters for retrying pydantic validation failures.

nodes.name: RetryableValidationNodeError
nodes.summary: Exception subclass signaling a node validation failure that LangGraph should retry rather than treat as fatal.

nodes.name: _jaccard

nodes.name: _iou
nodes.summary: Computes the intersection-over-union (IoU) score between two bounding boxes.

nodes.name: _levenshtein
nodes.summary: Computes the Levenshtein (edit) distance between two strings using dynamic programming, used to score drift between extracted-text outputs.
