nodes.languageNotes: Uses imageio_ffmpeg to locate a bundled ffmpeg binary and subprocess to decode audio without external audio-processing libraries.

nodes.name: reading_path_corpus_scan.py
nodes.summary: Batch-scans the working slide corpus, perceives each PNG, computes layout feature vectors, classifies reading paths, clusters residual/ambiguous slides, and writes an evidence report summarizing corpus-wide reading-path coverage.

nodes.name: reading_path_holdout_perceive.py
nodes.summary: Runs the slide-perception and reading-path classification pipeline over a held-out slide set, writing per-slide perception artifacts and a summary JSON for holdout validation.

nodes.name: reading_path_p2_4b_measure_fresh.py
nodes.summary: Loads freshly perceived slide artifacts, applies LLM-primary reading-path classification, scores them against gold labels, and writes a P2-4b measurement report with per-axis accuracy and degraded-count metrics.

nodes.name: reading_path_p2_4b_run.py
nodes.summary: Live-runs reading-path classification over a perceptions summary against gold-labeled data and scores the emitted primary-key classifications, reporting results via the shared scoring module.

nodes.name: reading_path_p2_4b_score.py
nodes.summary: Defines the ReadingPathTuple/ScoreReport data model and score() function used to compare emitted vs gold reading-path classifications across primary-key, full-tuple, and per-axis accuracy metrics, with demo-row scaffolding for smoke testing.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package marker for the bmb_agent_migration scripts package.

nodes.name: init_sanctum.py
nodes.summary: CLI tool that migrates a legacy skill-based BMAD agent into the sanctum tree: parses agent config/frontmatter, discovers capabilities and evolvable files, plans file operations, and (optionally) executes them to scaffold the agent's persistent sanctum directory.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Empty package marker for the dev tooling scripts package.

nodes.name: check_co_commit.py
nodes.summary: Pre-commit-style guardrail script that inspects staged files and fails the commit if governance-critical file pairs (e.g., manifest + regression tests) are not co-committed together.

nodes.name: check_gate_guardrails.py
nodes.summary: Small CLI wrapper that scans all files under app/gates for guardrail violations by delegating to a shared scan_paths checker.

nodes.name: decode_to_mono_float
nodes.summary: Decodes an audio file to mono float32 PCM samples by invoking a bundled ffmpeg binary via subprocess and parsing its raw stdout.

nodes.name: analyze_clip
nodes.summary: Computes RMS and peak amplitude metrics for an audio clip and returns a ClipAnalysis record.

nodes.name: materially_different
nodes.summary: Compares control vs treatment clip pairs on a chosen acoustic scalar and returns a Verdict on whether the treatment produced a materially different (threshold-exceeding) result.

nodes.name: ClipAnalysis
nodes.summary: Data record holding an audio clip's duration, RMS, peak, sample count, and sample rate.

nodes.name: Verdict
nodes.summary: Data record capturing the materially_different comparison result: scalar name, thresholds, control/treatment values, delta, and pass/fail outcome.

nodes.name: _load_dotenv
nodes.summary: Loads KEY=VALUE pairs from a local .env file into the process environment without external dependencies.

nodes.name: _parse_bbox
nodes.summary: Parses a raw bounding-box dict/tuple from an artifact dump into a normalized 4-tuple of floats.

nodes.name: _positioned_elements
nodes.summary: Extracts positioned slide elements (kind, text, bounding box) from a raw perception artifact dump for downstream feature computation.

nodes.name: compute_feature_vector
nodes.summary: Computes a structured layout feature vector (band positions, element counts, keyword flags) from a slide's positioned elements, used to characterize reading-path residue clusters.

nodes.name: _feature_signature
nodes.summary: Reduces a feature vector to a compact boolean signature string used as a clustering key for residual slides.

nodes.name: cluster_residue
nodes.summary: Groups residual (unclassified/ambiguous) slide rows by feature signature into named clusters with size and average-metric summaries.

nodes.name: _rough_character
nodes.summary: Builds a human-readable rough-character description string for a residue cluster's centroid feature vector.

nodes.name: enumerate_working_slides
nodes.summary: Enumerates PNG slide files in the working corpus directory, validating that a minimum slide count is present.

nodes.name: scan
nodes.summary: Drives the full corpus scan: perceives each slide, persists per-slide perception artifacts, computes feature vectors, classifies reading paths, and aggregates rows plus errors for reporting.

nodes.name: _fv_brief
nodes.summary: Formats a short human-readable summary of a feature vector's notable flags for inclusion in the evidence report.

nodes.name: write_report
nodes.summary: Renders the full corpus-scan evidence report (markdown) from scan results, including histograms, residue clusters, and error listings, and writes it to disk.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: loads environment, runs the corpus scan, writes the evidence report and scan-summary JSON, and prints a completion summary.

nodes.name: _load_dotenv
nodes.summary: Loads KEY=VALUE pairs from a local .env file into the process environment without external dependencies.

nodes.name: enumerate_heldout_slides
nodes.summary: Enumerates the held-out slide PNGs used for reading-path validation, verifying a minimum count.

nodes.name: scan
nodes.summary: Perceives each held-out slide, persists perception artifacts, computes feature vectors, classifies reading paths, and aggregates rows/errors for the holdout summary.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: loads environment, runs the holdout scan, writes the summary JSON, and prints completion status.

nodes.name: _load_dotenv
nodes.summary: Loads KEY=VALUE pairs from a local .env file into the process environment without external dependencies.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: loads gold labels and fresh perception artifacts, applies LLM-primary reading-path classification, scores results per-axis, and writes a P2-4b measurement report with confusion diagnostics.

nodes.name: load_gold
nodes.summary: Loads gold-labeled reading-path tuples from a JSON file into ReadingPathTuple records keyed by slide id.

nodes.name: score_emitted
nodes.summary: Scores a dict of emitted reading-path classifications against gold labels, raising if slide-id sets don't match, and delegates to the shared score() function.

nodes.name: run_live
nodes.summary: Live-runs LLM-primary reading-path classification over each perception artifact in a summary file and collects emitted ReadingPathTuple results for scoring.

nodes.name: fold_image_role
nodes.summary: Normalizes/folds raw image-role labels (including numeric-string variants) into a canonical role category for scoring comparisons.

nodes.name: score
nodes.summary: Computes primary-key, full-tuple, and per-axis accuracy between emitted and gold ReadingPathTuple rows, building a ScoreReport with confusion matrices and quarantined-row tracking.

nodes.name: build_demo_rows
nodes.summary: Builds a small set of demo emitted/gold row pairs for smoke-testing the score() function without live data.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that runs score() over demo rows and prints summary and confusion diagnostics.

nodes.name: ReadingPathTuple
nodes.summary: Structured tuple of reading-path classification axes (macro layout, image role, text substructure, narration cadence, callout intent) with primary_key/full_tuple_key derivation methods used for scoring comparisons.

nodes.name: ScoreReport
nodes.summary: Aggregated scoring report over reading-path classification accuracy, exposing per-axis rates, pass/fail thresholds, and human-readable summary/confusion-matrix rendering.

nodes.name: parse_yaml_config
nodes.summary: Minimal hand-rolled YAML parser for the skill's config file, extracting simple key-value pairs and list fields without a YAML library dependency.

nodes.name: parse_frontmatter
nodes.summary: Parses a markdown file's YAML frontmatter block into a metadata dict (name, description, other fields).

nodes.name: discover_capabilities
nodes.summary: Scans a skill's references directory for capability reference files, extracting name/description metadata and flagging any missing skill-only capabilities.

nodes.name: script_purpose
nodes.summary: Extracts a script's declared PURPOSE docstring/comment via regex for inclusion in generated capability documentation.

nodes.name: render_capabilities_md
nodes.summary: Renders the sanctum's CAPABILITIES.md content, listing capability references and companion scripts with their purposes.

nodes.name: plan_actions
nodes.summary: Builds the ordered list of file-copy/render/substitute actions needed to migrate a skill into its sanctum, including capabilities.md generation, based on discovered templates and config.

nodes.name: print_plan
nodes.summary: Pretty-prints the migration plan (files to create, variables to substitute) to the console before execution.

nodes.name: execute_plan
nodes.summary: Executes the migration plan: creates the sanctum directory structure and writes/renders each planned file, respecting force-overwrite semantics.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: parses arguments, locates the target skill and sanctum paths, builds and prints the migration plan, and executes it unless in dry-run mode.

nodes.name: staged_files
nodes.summary: Runs `git diff --cached --name-only` to list currently staged files for the co-commit guardrail check.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Checks staged files for governance-critical co-commit pairs (e.g. manifest + tests) and exits non-zero with a remediation message if a required pair is only partially staged.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Collects all files under app/gates and passes them to a shared scan_paths guardrail checker.

nodes.name: check_mojibake.py
nodes.summary: Scans specified text files for known mojibake byte sequences (common UTF-8/cp1252 mis-decoding artifacts) and reports offending files with escaped before/after samples.

nodes.name: check_orphans.py
nodes.summary: Static-analysis guardrail that parses each Python file's AST, resolves its imported module aliases, and flags orphaned/unused imports by checking whether imported names are referenced elsewhere in the file.

nodes.name: flake_measure_1_1d.py
nodes.summary: Runs a targeted pytest module repeatedly (with and without per-run sleeps) to measure flaky-test failure rates for the 1-1d investigation, printing pass/fail counts for each mode.

nodes.name: init_postgres.sql
nodes.summary: Postgres bootstrap script that idempotently creates the migration app's login role and database, grants schema/default privileges, and enables the pgcrypto extension for local/native Postgres setup (no Docker).
nodes.languageNotes: This is a role/database provisioning script rather than a table-definition migration; it uses psql \gexec and \connect meta-commands to conditionally create the role and database.

nodes.name: fidelity_drift_check.py
nodes.summary: Extracts thematic sections from a source-extraction markdown file and checks whether a generated artifact adequately represents each theme's keywords, computing an aggregate global fidelity-drift score against a configurable pass threshold.

nodes.name: pack.md.j2
nodes.summary: Top-level Jinja2 layout template for the v4.2 generated lockstep-witness prompt pack: assembles the header, TL;DR crosswalk, pre-run launchpad, per-step sections, and provenance appendix into the rendered pack markdown.
nodes.languageNotes: Composes partials via {% include %} and imports a macro via {% from %} to lay out a generated documentation pack from a manifest-driven step list.

nodes.name: audience_tag.j2
nodes.summary: Single-line Jinja2 macro that renders an audience tag (e.g. '[M→O]') used to label who a prompt-pack instruction addresses.

nodes.name: pack_header.md.j2
nodes.summary: Jinja2 partial rendering the v4.2 generated pack's frontmatter and banner, explaining its role as the lockstep determinism-target among three co-resident prompt-pack files.

nodes.name: pre_run_launchpad.md.j2
nodes.summary: Jinja2 partial rendering the '0.5) Pre-Run Operator Launchpad' section covering environment verification, system-health/preflight gating, and production run-constants visibility checks for the generated prompt pack.

nodes.name: provenance_appendix.md.j2
nodes.summary: Jinja2 partial that renders a provenance appendix table listing each pack step's id and rationale.

nodes.name: tldr_crosswalk.md.j2
nodes.summary: Jinja2 partial that renders the '0) TL;DR Crosswalk' table mapping each pack section to its HUD id, Marcus module, audience, and one-line purpose.

nodes.name: 01-activation-preflight.md.j2
nodes.summary: Jinja2 section template rendering the activation-preflight prompt step, instructing Marcus to return an activation receipt and run preflight/venv-health commands before a trial proceeds.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: reads each target file, scans for mojibake byte sequences via _find_hits, and prints violations with escaped before/after samples, exiting non-zero on any hit.

nodes.name: _module_aliases_from_tree
nodes.summary: Walks a parsed AST to build a mapping of imported module aliases to their canonical dotted module names for orphan-import resolution.

nodes.name: check_file
nodes.summary: Parses a Python file's AST, resolves its import aliases, and checks each imported module for orphaned/unused symbol references, returning a list of findings.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: resolves target files (explicit args or discovered test files), runs check_file on each, and prints findings, exiting non-zero if any orphans are found.

nodes.name: _run_pytest
nodes.summary: Runs a target pytest module a given number of times (optionally with per-iteration sleeps to reduce session-state coupling) and records pass/fail/duration results for each run.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point: parses iteration count, runs the flake measurement in single-session and delayed modes, and prints a comparative pass/fail summary.

nodes.name: extract_source_themes
nodes.summary: Parses a source-extraction markdown file into a list of (heading, body) theme tuples by splitting on H2/H3 headings.

nodes.name: check_theme_representation
nodes.summary: Checks whether a theme's significant keywords appear in a generated artifact's text, returning a representation ratio used for per-theme drift scoring.

nodes.name: compute_global_drift
nodes.summary: Computes an aggregate global fidelity-drift score by checking every extracted source theme's representation in the artifact text against a gate-specific threshold, returning pass/fail plus per-theme diagnostics.

nodes.name: 02-source-authority-map.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 2 template: instructs Marcus to produce the source authority and ingestion map for a run, gated by a mandatory source-directory scan and operator role assignment before the map may be drafted.

nodes.name: 02A-operator-directives.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 2A template: polls the operator for focus/exclusion/special-treatment source-processing directives with a hard minimum poll-open window, binding Texas and Irene during ingestion and planning, and supports reusing prior-run defaults for the same lesson.

nodes.name: 03-ingestion-evidence-log.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 3 template: delegates ingestion to Texas's wrangling runner (fetch/extract/validate/cross-validate in one pass), authoring directive.yaml and verifying the ten canonical bundle artifacts plus a belt-and-suspenders extracted-word-count floor check.

nodes.name: 04-ingestion-quality-gate-irene-packet.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 4 template: runs the six-dimension ingestion quality gate (each dimension requires an evidence sentence, not a bare score) plus Vera G0, persists the gate receipt, then generates the Irene packet via prepare-irene-packet.py which fail-closes if the receipt is missing or templated.

nodes.name: 04.5-parent-slide-count-polling.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 4.5 template: runs the profile-aware slide-count/runtime estimator, polls the operator for exactly parent_slide_count and target_total_runtime_minutes, and re-loops on BLOCK feasibility before locking derived values into run-constants.yaml.

nodes.name: 04.55-estimator-run-constants-lock.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 4.55 template: a terse transactional-lock checkpoint that commits the Prompt 4.5 estimator outputs and run constants into a single frozen baseline before downstream emission proceeds.

nodes.name: 04A-lesson-plan-coauthoring-scope-lock.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 4A template: mandatory operator-facing scope-ratification checkpoint run between Prompt 4 and Prompt 4.5, where Marcus and the operator lock per-unit lesson plan scope before any downstream estimator or dispatch-prep work proceeds.

nodes.name: 05-irene-pass-1-gate-1-fidelity.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 5 (+5.5) template: delegates Irene Pass 1 planning, runs Vera G1/G2 and Quinn-R quality gates enforcing one mode per slide, then runs the required HIL review of mode assignments against operator proportions before Gate 1 can close.

nodes.name: 05B-cluster-plan-g1-5-gate.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 5B template: conditional Cluster Plan G1.5 gate run only when CLUSTER_DENSITY is enabled, requiring the gate script to pass and then explicit operator approve/reject of the cluster plan before Prompt 6.

nodes.name: 06-pre-dispatch-package-build.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 6 template: after Gate 1 approval, builds Gary's pre-dispatch package (slide brief, content/fidelity/diagram JSON, theme resolution, outbound envelope) and stops before dispatch, with double-dispatch variant-B artifact support.

nodes.name: 06B-literal-visual-operator-build.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 6B template: mandatory literal-visual operator checkpoint capturing the visual-led vs text-led experience profile, then gates Gary dispatch on every diagram card having a resolved hosted image URL or on-disk PNG before Prompt 7.

nodes.name: 07-gary-dispatch-export.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 7 template: dispatches Gary (single or A/B double-dispatch) once all pre-dispatch checks pass, then validates the dispatch result, PNG export-to-card mapping, and Vera G3 on every dispatch set produced.

nodes.name: 07B-variant-selection-gate.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 7B template (double-dispatch only): operator reviews paired A/B Gary variants per slide and selects the stronger treatment, with auto-select fallback recorded explicitly when one side failed.

nodes.name: 07C-storyboard-a-gate-2-approval.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 7C template: builds the pre-Irene review storyboard (storyboard.json + index.html) for the winner deck, writes authorized-storyboard.json, and obtains explicit Gate 2 approval that motion planning and Irene Pass 2 must consume.

nodes.name: 07D-gate-2m-motion-designation.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 7D template: presents a per-slide motion recommendation set for every authorized slide and captures the operator's static/video/animation motion-type designation, writing motion-designations.json and motion_plan.yaml via motion_plan.py.

nodes.name: 07D.5-motion-planning.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 7D.5 template: deterministic Motion-Plan producer node that reuses the Epic-14 engine to build kira's consumable motion_plan shape from the authorized storyboard and Gate 2M designations; touches no model client and is the single declared motion source for kira (07E).

nodes.name: 07E-motion-generation-import.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 7E template: routes non-static slides from motion_plan.yaml to Kling video generation or manual animation import, enforcing fail-closed budget-downgrade rules, receipt-based resume, and reviewer inspection-pack artifacts before Motion Gate closes.

nodes.name: 07F-motion-gate.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 7F template: Motion Gate operator review confirming every non-static slide's generated/imported asset is approved (or intentionally reset to static) before Irene Pass 2 handoff is unblocked.

nodes.name: 07G-png-grounded-vision-perception.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 7G template: runs the Vision specialist over Gary's approved slide PNG exports to emit one PNG-grounded PerceptionArtifact per slide (or an explicit not-covered marker), consumed by Quinn-R's G5 detector with no brief-derived fallback allowed.

nodes.name: 07W-companion-workbook-producer.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 07W template: HUD-hidden, gate-free deterministic Companion-Workbook producer band that assembles a WorkbookSidecar (canonical MD + client DOCX) from the Storyboard-B segment manifest and Irene Pass-1 lesson plan; a terminal leaf with no downstream consumers, pruned entirely if the workbook component is deselected.

nodes.name: 08-irene-pass-2-segment-manifest.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 8 template: delegates Irene Pass 2 narration-script and segment-manifest authoring once Gate 2, motion plan, and approved motion assets are ready, enforcing a 19-criteria Vera G4 gate (behavioral_intent, motion_brief fidelity, cluster narration coherence) and motion-first narration rules.

nodes.name: 08B-storyboard-b-hil-review.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 8B template: regenerates Storyboard B with full script and motion context after Irene Pass 2 G4 and Motion Gate approval, publishes the hydrated snapshot to the jlenrique.github.io site repo, and requires explicit operator HIL approval from the live URL before Gate 3.

nodes.name: 09-gate-3-lock-pass-2-package.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 9 template: Gate 3 approve/revision decision that, on approval, locks narration-script.md, segment-manifest.yaml, pass2-envelope.json, and the final Motion Gate-approved motion_plan.yaml as the frozen execution source of truth for downstream spend.

nodes.name: 10-fidelity-quality-pre-spend.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 10 template: runs downstream readiness checks (Vera G4 rerun if artifacts changed, Quinn-R review, motion-cue/asset-path drift checks) on the Gate 3-approved package before ElevenLabs and compositor spend, with explicit no-go conditions.

nodes.name: 11-elevenlabs-voice-selection-hil.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 pipeline Prompt 11 template: runs the Voice Director in preview-only mode against the locked Gate 3 package to let the operator pick a lesson-level ElevenLabs voice (continuity, default-plus-alternatives, or description-driven search) before any narration synthesis spend.

nodes.name: 11B-elevenlabs-input-package-hil.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 prompt-pack template section instructing Marcus to display the locked ElevenLabs input package (script, manifest, voice selection, voice-direction overrides) and require explicit operator GO before Prompt 12 narration synthesis begins.

nodes.name: 12-elevenlabs-audio-generation.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 prompt-pack template section directing Marcus to delegate manifest-driven ElevenLabs narration synthesis to the Voice Director from the locked Gate 3 package, including hash verification and motion-preserving rules.

nodes.name: 13-quinn-r-pre-composition-qa.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 prompt-pack template section defining Quinn-R's pre-composition QA checklist (WPM pacing, VTT monotonicity, segment coverage, motion/narration duration coherence) run after ElevenLabs and before compositor packaging.

nodes.name: 14-compositor-assembly-bundle.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 prompt-pack template section instructing Marcus/compositor to build the final Descript assembly bundle (visuals, motion, audio, captions) from the pre-composition-approved manifest.

nodes.name: 14.5-desmond-run-scoped-operator-brief.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 prompt-pack template section delegating to the Desmond agent to translate the generic compositor guide into a Descript-product-specific operator brief with a mandatory Automation Advisory subsection.

nodes.name: 15-operator-handoff-descript-ready.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 prompt-pack template section presenting the final Descript-ready operator handoff package, mandatory per-prompt receipts, and prompt-pack lineage/changelog documentation for motion-enabled narrated runs.

nodes.name: 4.75-creative-directive-resolution.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 prompt-pack template section (conditional on EXPERIENCE_PROFILE) delegating Creative Directive resolution to the CD agent and persisting resolved run-constants and narration-profile controls.

nodes.name: 6.2-cluster-prompt-engineering.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 prompt-pack template section (conditional on CLUSTER_DENSITY) rendering cluster-aware prompts from the G1.5-approved cluster plan via cluster_prompt_engineering.py.

nodes.name: 6.3-cluster-dispatch-sequencing.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 prompt-pack template section (conditional on CLUSTER_DENSITY) building a deterministic cluster dispatch plan via cluster_dispatch_sequencing.py.

nodes.name: 7.5-cluster-coherence-g2-5-gate.md.j2
nodes.summary: v4.2 prompt-pack template section (conditional on CLUSTER_DENSITY) running the G2.5 cluster coherence validation gate on generated cluster outputs before Storyboard A.

nodes.name: heartbeat_check.mjs
nodes.summary: Standalone Node.js diagnostic script that loads .env and performs lightweight read-only heartbeat checks against every API-accessible tool (Gamma, ElevenLabs, etc.) without creating or consuming credits.
nodes.languageNotes: Manual .env parsing avoids adding a dependency; async check()/skip() helpers accumulate a results array for a final PASS/FAIL/SKIP summary.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package entry point for Marcus's production-readiness capability router, re-exporting CAPABILITY_REGISTRY and UnknownCapability from the registry module.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: Builds and cross-validates Marcus's capability registry by parsing frontmatter from skills/bmad-agent-marcus/capabilities/*.md and cross-checking against the supplementary registry.yaml index and per-capability schema files.

nodes.name: run_prompt12_narration.py
nodes.summary: Marcus shim script invoking elevenlabs_operations.generate_manifest_narration() with voice-direction parameter overrides drawn from voice-selection.json, used as a Prompt 12 stand-in pending CLI canonicalization.

nodes.name: run_prompt13_quinnr_precomposition.py
nodes.summary: Marcus shim implementing the §13 Quinn-R pre-composition QA checks (narration WPM, VTT monotonicity, segment coverage, motion/narration duration coherence, asset existence) and writing a quinnr-precomposition-receipt.json.

nodes.name: _launch_fresh_c1m1_p1_trial.ps1
nodes.summary: Operator PowerShell launcher that activates the venv and starts a fresh Marcus-SPOC production trial for the C1-M1-P1 course with live G0 dispatch/research-detective flags and a $10 budget.

nodes.name: bundle_health_check.py
nodes.summary: Slab 6 trial-experience bundle health check that runs focused pytest regression slices (manifest dependency_map, Step 02A directives, Irene Pass 2 template, HUD summaries, composition substrate) with no live API calls.

nodes.name: check_keys.py
nodes.summary: Operator helper that auto-loads .env and prints a masked presence/absence report for every provider API key used across generation, orchestration, and Texas retrieval providers.

nodes.name: diagnose_wondercraft.py
nodes.summary: Wondercraft auth/permission diagnostic that walks the API in increasing privilege (auth header, connectivity, list episodes, scripted-podcast preflight) to isolate whether a 403 is account-tier or auth/config related.

nodes.name: dual_gate_slab_6_0.py
nodes.summary: Re-runnable Slab 6.0 dual-gate evidence ceremony that executes the composition test suite (tests/composition/) to verify the production envelope substrate and prints a paste-ready evidence summary.

nodes.name: dual_gate_slab_6_1.py
nodes.summary: Re-runnable Slab 6.1 dual-gate evidence ceremony (LIVE API) that runs the production-graph-runner smoke test with checkpoint pause/resume, requiring OpenAI and LangSmith credentials, and streams pytest output live.

nodes.name: gate5_slab_6_4.py
nodes.summary: Slab 6.4 Gate 5 dual-gate evidence ceremony that runs the validator-oracle alignment, composition smoke, and strict Pydantic red-rejection suites for the Irene Pass 2 authoring template, printing a paste-ready evidence block.

nodes.name: harvest_wondercraft_job.py
nodes.summary: Downloads a finished Wondercraft podcast job's audio, computes its SHA256, saves it to the wanda live-artifacts fixture path with metadata, and prints an addendum block for the M2 verdict artifact.

nodes.name: m2_wondercraft_ceremony.py
nodes.summary: Runs a single Wondercraft scripted-podcast invocation via corrected direct API calls (bypassing known WondercraftClient payload-shape defects), waits for render, harvests the audio artifact, and prints the M2 verdict addendum.

nodes.name: m3_texas_ceremony_notion.py
nodes.summary: Runs a single Notion search call via NotionClient to close the M3 acceptance condition's locator-shape evidence requirement, capturing results and provenance to a fixture path and printing a verdict addendum.

nodes.name: loadEnv
nodes.summary: Manually parses the project .env file into process.env without external dependencies, exiting with an error if .env is missing.

nodes.name: UnknownCapabilityError
nodes.summary: Router-level KeyError subclass raised when a capability code is not registered in Marcus's capability registry.

nodes.name: RegistryEntry
nodes.summary: Frozen dataclass holding one capability's registration data (code, name, description, script_module, schema_path, full_or_stub, markdown_path).

nodes.name: _parse_frontmatter
nodes.summary: Reads a capability markdown file and parses its YAML frontmatter block into a dict, returning None if absent or unparseable.

nodes.name: _scan_capabilities_dir
nodes.summary: Walks skills/bmad-agent-marcus/capabilities/*.md, filters for frontmatter with both name and code, and builds RegistryEntry objects keyed by capability code.

nodes.name: _load_registry_yaml
nodes.summary: Loads the supplementary registry.yaml capabilities index used for cross-reference auditing against the markdown-derived registry.

nodes.name: _build_registry
nodes.summary: Thin wrapper that builds the module-level capability registry by scanning the capabilities directory.

nodes.name: get
nodes.summary: Looks up a capability by code in CAPABILITY_REGISTRY, raising UnknownCapability with the sorted list of known codes on a miss.

nodes.name: cross_reference_audit
nodes.summary: Cross-checks the markdown-derived capability registry against registry.yaml and per-capability schema files, returning a list of skew findings (empty means clean, per AC-T.9).

nodes.name: _load_operations_module

layers.name: Orchestration Core
layers.description: The Marcus-SPOC operator surface, CLI, lesson-plan flow, and the manifest graph compiler that compiles the deterministic LangGraph dispatch that drives every production run.

layers.name: Specialist Agents
layers.description: The 14+ specialist agent graphs built on the 9-node scaffold (Irene, Gary, Vera, Texas, etc.) that Marcus dispatches to perform course-content production work.

layers.name: Gates, HIL & Governance
layers.description: Human-in-the-loop gate surfaces and resume APIs plus the tamper-evident governance subsystems — parity anti-drift guard, Cora block-mode, audit, replay, and the learning ledger.

layers.name: Runtime Substrate & Observability
layers.description: Runtime overrides, LLM batch/cascade config, HTTP + MCP transports, HUD and notify observability surfaces, styleguide resources, output composers, and the runtime server entry point.

layers.name: Contracts & Models
layers.description: Pydantic state models, dispatch contracts, DecisionCard verdicts, and operator-verdict schemas under app/models — the foundational, highest-fan-in layer every other app subsystem imports.

layers.name: Scripts, Tool Clients & Generators
layers.description: Operator/dev scripts, media-tool API clients (Gamma, Descript, ElevenLabs, Kling), v4.2 template generators, Marcus-capability tooling, and top-level project config files.

layers.name: Skills & Agent Personas
layers.description: BMAD agent SKILL.md persona definitions and sanctum asset templates that back the custom production and specialist personas.

tour.title: The Operator Surface
tour.description: Start where a human starts: Marcus-SPOC is the conversational single-point-of-contact that sits over the whole production engine. At each gate it narrates the relevant content in persona (sources, ingestion, lesson plan, research, voice) and holds a turn with the operator, while the trial CLI is the plumbing that actually launches, resumes, recovers, and batch-runs production-clone trials over the runner. Read these two together to see the product's shape: a chat surface backed by a deterministic pipeline the operator drives through explicit commands.

tour.title: Chatting at the Gate
tour.description: Building on the SPOC surface from Step 1, the interlocutor is the LLM-driven stop-and-chat REPL that runs at every paused gate. It narrates the real decision card in persona and holds a free-form turn loop with the operator through an injected chat model, but crucially it only ever confirms a validated gate verdict rather than inventing one. This is the boundary the whole system defends: the chatting LLM proposes, but the operator's confirmed verdict is what advances the run.

tour.title: Compiling the Deterministic Graph
tour.description: Before anything runs, a validated PipelineManifest is compiled into a LangGraph StateGraph. The compiler adds nodes and edges, resolves the specialist dispatch builders, and runs compile-time contract lint over gate codes, model-config references, and the frozen-graph version. This is the 'manifest-compiled' half of the project's identity: the pipeline is data first, executable graph second, which is what makes every run reproducible and auditable.
tour.languageLesson: LangGraph models a workflow as a StateGraph: nodes are functions that receive and return a shared state object, and edges (static or conditional) decide the next node. Compiling from a manifest rather than hand-wiring the graph means the topology is validated and frozen before any specialist executes.

tour.title: Walking the Production Pipeline
tour.description: The production runner is the composition layer that walks the compiled manifest node by node: it dispatches specialists, pauses at operator gates with decision cards, checkpoints, and resumes or recovers trials while enforcing budget and preflight guards. Note the design detail that matters most here: it has TWO node walks — a start walk that runs up to gate G1 and stops, and a separate resume walk — so any gate-pause side effect must be handled in both. The dispatch adapter is the seam that turns a manifest node into an actual specialist invocation.

tour.title: The Run State Contract
tour.description: Everything the runner does is recorded in RunState, the top-level state model encoding the five reproducibility invariants: byte-for-byte replay, frozen graph version, sanctum snapshot identity, model-selection trail, and documented temperature variance. Its shared base module centralizes the Pydantic-v2 idioms — timezone-aware datetimes and UUID4 version pins — that the whole state family reuses. This is the contracts substrate the rest of the tour keeps returning to; RunState has the highest fan-in of any file in the codebase.
tour.languageLesson: Pydantic v2 models validate and coerce data at construction. Centralizing validators (tz-aware datetimes, UUID4 pins) in a shared base and reusing them across a model family avoids per-file drift — a schema idiom this codebase enforces deliberately.

tour.title: Specialist Envelope & Return Contracts
tour.description: Specialists never talk to the orchestrator ad hoc — every invocation is wrapped in a SpecialistEnvelope carrying correlation identity (a UUID4 request_id and tz-aware timestamp) so async outputs can be paired back to their inputs, and every reply is a SpecialistReturn carrying a verb (accept/edit/reject) with a cross-field consistency check. Typed dispatch errors let the runner convert a transient failure into a recoverable error-pause instead of killing the trial, and the parity-contract DSL pins these surfaces so they cannot silently diverge. Together these are the request/response spine the runner from Step 4 relies on.

tour.title: The 9-Node Specialist Scaffold
tour.description: Every specialist agent is built from the same canonical 9-node LangGraph scaffold: receive, plan, act, verify, reflect, emit_spans, gate_decision, finalize, handoff, wired through a reachability-asserted transition table. The contract file is the production-side authority that defines the required node set and validates conformance, while the scaffold graph is the stub template the specialist generator instantiates. Understanding this one shape unlocks all 14+ specialists — they differ only in what their act and verify nodes do.

tour.title: Ingestion & Lesson Planning Specialists
tour.description: Now see the scaffold from Step 7 filled in by two real specialists at the front of the pipeline. Texas ingests source material and enforces a sanctum lock — digest-pinned persona references — before decoding its dispatch envelope, while Irene's Pass-2 graph is the narration-authoring core that assembles the deterministic prompt, invokes the LLM, then enforces the figure-fidelity and reading-path invariants that keep narration aligned to what is on screen. Both are the same 9 nodes; the discipline lives in their verify steps.

tour.title: Slide Dispatch & the Lesson Plan Schema
tour.description: Gary is the slide-generation specialist: its 9-node graph adds dispatch-trail entries, envelope decoding, and strict dispatch-receipt parsing around the standard pipeline before handing off to the Gamma deck generator. What every downstream specialist reads and writes against is the Lesson Plan schema — the Pydantic-v2 contract for LessonPlan, PlanUnit, the ScopeDecision state machine, BlueprintSignoff, and the FitReport diagnosis family. The schema is the shared vocabulary that lets Irene, Gary, and the rest agree on what the lesson actually is.

tour.title: The HIL Verdict Machinery
tour.description: This is the tamper-evident heart the README promises. The resume substrate registers each issued decision card with a SHA-256 digest and a one-time server nonce, then converts an operator verdict into a LangGraph resume Command with replay and tamper protection. OperatorVerdict is the frozen value object (approve/edit/reject) carrying that digest, and DecisionCardMeta validates cache-state and affected-nodes on the card itself. This closes the loop from Step 2: the chat can narrate, but only a digest-matched, nonce-valid verdict resumes the graph.

tour.title: Media Tool API Clients
tour.description: The pipeline reaches the outside world through a family of HTTP clients that all extend one foundation: authenticated session management, exponential-backoff retry with Retry-After support, Link-header pagination, and structured error diagnostics. On top of that base sit the concrete integrations — the Gamma client for AI slide generation (with its warm-401 burst-throttle retry) and the ElevenLabs client for narration, timestamps, and sound effects. This is how Gary's decks and Irene's narration from Steps 8-9 actually get produced.

tour.title: Runtime Observability: HUD, Notifier & Economics
tour.description: A live trial is watched by supporting runtime services. The public HUD is a separate tunnel-facing FastAPI app that serves an allowlist-scrubbed view of the live projection — never the raw bytes, and never launch_nonce or decision-card internals. The notifier polls the operator-surface file, derives event classes only through the contract, and runs a stall watchdog, while the economics module measures per-trial spend and computes per-agent cost drift against history. Together they give the operator situational awareness without ever leaking gate secrets.

tour.title: The Learning Ledger
tour.description: Durability lives in Postgres. The append-only ledger_events table (event_id, trial_id, gate_id, kind, payload, idempotency_key) is the tamper-resistant record backing gate accountability, and the idempotent emitter ensures the schema, deduplicates by idempotency key, and tracks emission-failure counters. This is the substrate that makes the reproducibility invariants from Step 5 auditable after the fact — a run leaves a trail nobody can quietly rewrite.
tour.languageLesson: An append-only event ledger never updates or deletes rows — it only inserts, keyed by an idempotency key so replays don't double-count. This is the durable-log pattern behind audit trails and event sourcing: the table of facts is the source of truth, and any derived state is a projection over it.

tour.title: Skills & Agent Personas
tour.description: Finally, step outside the runtime to the persona layer that gives these agents their voice. The Marcus SKILL.md is the activation contract for the Creative Production Orchestrator — the single conversational point of contact who routes work through the capability registry and honors fidelity gates — and it sits alongside a whole skills tree of BMAD personas and their sanctum templates. This closes the tour where it began (Step 1's SPOC surface), now understood as a persona-shaped orchestrator sitting atop the deterministic, contract-governed pipeline you have just walked end to end.
